question.english: What are the most popular electronic devices in today’s world?
question.chinese: 在現今世界，最受歡迎的電子設備是甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the most popular electronic devices in today’s world?
在現今世界，最受歡迎的電子設備是甚麼？

components.english: I’d say smartphones are by far the most popular electronic devices today, followed by laptops, tablets, and increasingly common wearables like wireless earbuds and smartwatches.
components.chinese: 我會說，現今最受歡迎的電子設備毫無疑問是智能手機，其次是手提電腦、平板電腦，以及愈來愈普及的穿戴式裝置，例如無線耳機和智能手錶。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say smartphones are by far the most popular electronic devices today,
followed by laptops, tablets, and increasingly common wearables like wireless
earbuds and smartwatches.
[我會說，現今最受歡迎的電子設備毫無疑問是智能手機，其次是手提電腦、平板電腦，以及愈來
愈普及的穿戴式裝置，例如無線耳機和智能手錶。]

components.english: The main reason is that smartphones have become the centre of modern life. People use them for communication, work, entertainment, shopping, navigation, banking, and just about everything in between. In many ways, the smartphone is now the Swiss Army knife of everyday life. Laptops and tablets are still hugely important as well, especially for work and study, while wearables are carving out a bigger space because they fit so neatly into people’s routines.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，智能手機已經成為現代生活的中心。人們用它來溝通、工作、娛樂、購物、導航、理財，幾乎所有日常事情都可以透過它完成。某程度上，智能手機現在就像日常生活中的瑞士軍刀一樣。手提電腦和平板電腦同樣非常重要，特別是在工作和學習方面；而穿戴式裝置之所以愈來愈受歡迎，是因為它們能很自然地融入人們的生活節奏之中。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that smartphones have become the centre of
modern life. People use them for communication, work, entertainment,
shopping, navigation, banking, and just about everything in between. In many
ways, the smartphone is now the Swiss Army knife of everyday life. Laptops and
tablets are still hugely important as well, especially for work and study, while
wearables are carving out a bigger space because they fit so neatly into people’s
routines.
[主要原因是，智能手機已經成為現代生活的中心。人們用它來溝通、工作、娛樂、購物、導
航、理財，幾乎所有日常事情都可以透過它完成。某程度上，智能手機現在就像日常生活中
的瑞士軍刀一樣。手提電腦和平板電腦同樣非常重要，特別是在工作和學習方面；而穿戴式
裝置之所以愈來愈受歡迎，是因為它們能很自然地融入人們的生活節奏之中。]

components.english: For example, a person may wake up to a phone alarm, reply to messages on the way to work, use a laptop in the office, listen to music through wireless earbuds during the commute, and check their steps or heart rate on a smartwatch later in the day. That kind of pattern is now so common that these devices have become part and parcel of daily life.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能早上被手機鬧鐘叫醒，在上班途中回覆訊息，到辦公室後用手提電腦工作，通勤時用無線耳機聽音樂，到了晚上再用智能手錶查看步數或心跳。這種生活模式現在已經非常普遍，所以這些裝置幾乎已經成為日常生活不可分割的一部分。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a person may wake up to a phone alarm, reply to
messages on the way to work, use a laptop in the office, listen to music through
wireless earbuds during the commute, and check their steps or heart rate on a
smartwatch later in the day. That kind of pattern is now so common that these
devices have become part and parcel of daily life.
[例如，一個人可能早上被手機鬧鐘叫醒，在上班途中回覆訊息，到辦公室後用手提電腦工
作，通勤時用無線耳機聽音樂，到了晚上再用智能手錶查看步數或心跳。這種生活模式現在
已經非常普遍，所以這些裝置幾乎已經成為日常生活不可分割的一部分。]

components.english: So, if I had to rank them, smartphones would come first without a shadow of a doubt, while laptops, tablets, and wearables would follow close behind.
components.chinese: 所以，如果一定要排次序，我會毫不猶豫地把智能手機放在第一位，而手提電腦、平板電腦和穿戴式裝置則緊隨其後。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to rank them, smartphones would come first without
a shadow of a doubt, while laptops, tablets, and wearables would follow close
behind.
[所以，如果一定要排次序，我會毫不猶豫地把智能手機放在第一位，而手提電腦、平板電
腦和穿戴式裝置則緊隨其後。]

components.english: That said, the most popular device often depends on what people need it for, because different tools shine in different situations.
components.chinese: 不過，最受歡迎的設備其實也取決於人們想用它來做甚麼，因為不同工具在不同情境下各有優勢。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, the most popular device often depends on what people need
it for, because different tools shine in different situations.
[不過，最受歡迎的設備其實也取決於人們想用它來做甚麼，因為不同工具在不同情境下各
有優勢。]

components.english: A phone may rule the roost for convenience, but it is not always the best tool for every task. When people need to write at length, design something, or work in depth, laptops still hold their ground. In the same way, tablets are useful for reading and media, while wearables are popular because they are quick, light, and always on hand. So the picture is not one-size-fits-all; it is more a case of one device leading the pack while others play strong supporting roles.
components.chinese: 手機在方便程度上也許是一枝獨秀，但它並不一定是每一種工作的最佳工具。當人們需要長時間打字、做設計，或者深入處理工作時，手提電腦仍然站得很穩。同樣地，平板電腦在閱讀和娛樂方面很有優勢，而穿戴式裝置之所以受歡迎，是因為它們快捷、輕便，而且隨手可用。所以，整體情況並不是一刀切的；更準確地說，是一種設備跑在最前面，而其他設備則扮演很強的配角。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A phone may rule the roost for convenience, but it is not
always the best tool for every task. When people need to write at length, design
something, or work in depth, laptops still hold their ground. In the same way,
tablets are useful for reading and media, while wearables are popular because
they are quick, light, and always on hand. So the picture is not one-size-fits-all;
it is more a case of one device leading the pack while others play strong
supporting roles.
[手機在方便程度上也許是一枝獨秀，但它並不一定是每一種工作的最佳工具。當人們需要
長時間打字、做設計，或者深入處理工作時，手提電腦仍然站得很穩。同樣地，平板電腦在
閱讀和娛樂方面很有優勢，而穿戴式裝置之所以受歡迎，是因為它們快捷、輕便，而且隨手
可用。所以，整體情況並不是一刀切的；更準確地說，是一種設備跑在最前面，而其他設備
則扮演很強的配角。]

components.english: For instance, a student may rely on a smartphone for everyday communication, but still turn to a laptop for essays and presentations. A fitness-minded person, on the other hand, may care much more about a smartwatch or wireless earbuds.
components.chinese: 例如，一個學生可能在日常溝通上很依賴智能手機，但到了寫論文或做簡報時，仍然會轉向手提電腦。另一方面，一個很重視運動的人，也許就會更在意智能手錶或無線耳機。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a student may rely on a smartphone for everyday
communication, but still turn to a laptop for essays and presentations. A
fitness-minded person, on the other hand, may care much more about a
smartwatch or wireless earbuds.
[例如，一個學生可能在日常溝通上很依賴智能手機，但到了寫論文或做簡報時，仍然會轉
向手提電腦。另一方面，一個很重視運動的人，也許就會更在意智能手錶或無線耳機。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say smartphones are the most popular electronic devices in today’s world, but the wider picture includes laptops, tablets, and wearables too. Different devices do different jobs, but the smartphone is clearly the beating heart of the whole set-up.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說智能手機是現今世界最受歡迎的電子設備，但更完整的畫面其實也包括手提電腦、平板電腦，以及穿戴式裝置。不同設備各有分工，不過智能手機顯然仍然是整套數碼生活中的核心。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say smartphones are the most popular electronic
devices in today’s world, but the wider picture includes laptops, tablets, and
wearables too. Different devices do different jobs, but the smartphone is clearly
the beating heart of the whole set-up.
[總結來說，我會說智能手機是現今世界最受歡迎的電子設備，但更完整的畫面其實也包括
手提電腦、平板電腦，以及穿戴式裝置。不同設備各有分工，不過智能手機顯然仍然是整套
數碼生活中的核心。]

question.english: What devices do you think will be popular in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為未來甚麼設備會受歡迎？

source_lines.text: Topic: What devices do you think will be popular in the future?
你認為未來甚麼設備會受歡迎？

components.english: I think devices that save time and make daily life smoother will be especially popular in the future, particularly AI-powered personal devices and smart home technology.
components.chinese: 我認為，未來最受歡迎的，會是那些能節省時間、令日常生活更順暢的設備，尤其是人工智能個人設備，以及智能家居科技。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think devices that save time and make daily life smoother will be
especially popular in the future, particularly AI-powered personal devices and
smart home technology.
[我認為，未來最受歡迎的，會是那些能節省時間、令日常生活更順暢的設備，尤其是人工
智能個人設備，以及智能家居科技。]

components.english: The reason is quite simple: people are busy, distracted, and always looking for ways to make life a little lighter. So, devices that can think faster, respond more intelligently, and take small tasks off people’s plate are likely to take off. In a way, technology is moving away from being just a tool and becoming more like a quiet assistant in the background. If a device can organise your schedule, answer questions, control your home, and adapt to your habits, it will probably be hard for many people to resist.
components.chinese: 原因其實很簡單：現代人生活忙碌、注意力分散，而且總是在找方法讓生活輕鬆一點。所以，那些能更快思考、更聰明回應，並且替人分擔一些瑣碎事情的設備，很可能會越來越受歡迎。某程度上，科技正逐漸由單純的工具，變成一位安靜地在背後幫忙的助手。如果一部設備能替你整理行程、回答問題、控制家居，甚至慢慢適應你的習慣，那很多人應該都很難抗拒。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is quite simple: people are busy, distracted, and
always looking for ways to make life a little lighter. So, devices that can think
faster, respond more intelligently, and take small tasks off people’s plate are
likely to take off. In a way, technology is moving away from being just a tool and
becoming more like a quiet assistant in the background. If a device can organise
your schedule, answer questions, control your home, and adapt to your habits, it
will probably be hard for many people to resist.
[原因其實很簡單：現代人生活忙碌、注意力分散，而且總是在找方法讓生活輕鬆一點。所
以，那些能更快思考、更聰明回應，並且替人分擔一些瑣碎事情的設備，很可能會越來越受
歡迎。某程度上，科技正逐漸由單純的工具，變成一位安靜地在背後幫忙的助手。如果一部
設備能替你整理行程、回答問題、控制家居，甚至慢慢適應你的習慣，那很多人應該都很難
抗拒。]

components.english: For example, I can easily imagine AI phones, smart speakers, or even smart glasses becoming more common. A person might ask their device to summarise messages, book a restaurant, remind them of meetings, and switch on the air conditioner before they get home. That kind of convenience is hard to beat, because once people get used to it, going back can feel like stepping from a lift back onto the stairs.
components.chinese: 例如，我很容易想像人工智能手機、智能喇叭，甚至智能眼鏡會變得越來越普遍。一個人可能只要對設備說幾句話，它就能幫他整理訊息、訂餐廳、提醒會議，甚至在他回家前先開好冷氣。這種便利很難不受歡迎，因為人一旦習慣了，再回頭就可能像從升降機重新走回樓梯一樣。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, I can easily imagine AI phones, smart speakers, or even
smart glasses becoming more common. A person might ask their device to
summarise messages, book a restaurant, remind them of meetings, and switch
on the air conditioner before they get home. That kind of convenience is hard to
beat, because once people get used to it, going back can feel like stepping from a
lift back onto the stairs.
[例如，我很容易想像人工智能手機、智能喇叭，甚至智能眼鏡會變得越來越普遍。一個人
可能只要對設備說幾句話，它就能幫他整理訊息、訂餐廳、提醒會議，甚至在他回家前先開
好冷氣。這種便利很難不受歡迎，因為人一旦習慣了，再回頭就可能像從升降機重新走回樓
梯一樣。]

components.english: So, one major trend will probably be the rise of devices that reduce friction in everyday life and make people feel that things simply run better.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個很明顯的趨勢，很可能就是那些能減少日常生活阻力、讓人覺得事情運作得更順的設備會越來越受歡迎。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major trend will probably be the rise of devices that
reduce friction in everyday life and make people feel that things simply run
better.
[所以，其中一個很明顯的趨勢，很可能就是那些能減少日常生活阻力、讓人覺得事情運作
得更順的設備會越來越受歡迎。]

components.english: On top of that, I think health-related and wearable devices will become even more popular, because people are paying more attention to well-being than they used to.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為和健康有關的穿戴式設備會變得更加受歡迎，因為現在的人比以前更重視身心健康。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think health-related and wearable devices will
become even more popular, because people are paying more attention to
well-being than they used to.
[除此之外，我也認為和健康有關的穿戴式設備會變得更加受歡迎，因為現在的人比以前更
重視身心健康。]

components.english: These days, many people want devices that do more than entertain them; they want devices that help them live better. That is why smart watches, fitness bands, sleep trackers, and maybe even more advanced health monitors are likely to become part of everyday life. After all, prevention is better than cure, isn’t it? If a device can track your heart rate, sleep quality, stress level, or exercise habits, it becomes more than a gadget. It becomes a kind of daily checkpoint, almost like a mirror quietly showing people how well they are really looking after themselves.
components.chinese: 如今，很多人希望設備不只是用來娛樂自己；他們更希望設備能幫助自己活得更好。這就是為甚麼智能手錶、運動手環、睡眠追蹤器，甚至更先進的健康監測設備，很可能會成為日常生活的一部分。畢竟，預防勝於治療，不是嗎？如果一部設備能追蹤你的心率、睡眠質素、壓力水平，或者運動習慣，它就不再只是一件電子產品，而更像是一個每天都在提醒你的健康檢查點，彷彿一面安靜的鏡子，照出你究竟有沒有好好照顧自己。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: These days, many people want devices that do more than
entertain them; they want devices that help them live better. That is why smart
watches, fitness bands, sleep trackers, and maybe even more advanced health
monitors are likely to become part of everyday life. After all, prevention is
better than cure, isn’t it? If a device can track your heart rate, sleep quality,
stress level, or exercise habits, it becomes more than a gadget. It becomes a kind
of daily checkpoint, almost like a mirror quietly showing people how well they
are really looking after themselves.
[如今，很多人希望設備不只是用來娛樂自己；他們更希望設備能幫助自己活得更好。這就
是為甚麼智能手錶、運動手環、睡眠追蹤器，甚至更先進的健康監測設備，很可能會成為日
常生活的一部分。畢竟，預防勝於治療，不是嗎？如果一部設備能追蹤你的心率、睡眠質
素、壓力水平，或者運動習慣，它就不再只是一件電子產品，而更像是一個每天都在提醒你
的健康檢查點，彷彿一面安靜的鏡子，照出你究竟有沒有好好照顧自己。]

components.english: For instance, in the future, it may be perfectly normal for someone to wear a device that warns them when they have been sitting too long, sleeping too little, or pushing themselves too hard. Busy workers, older people, and even students under pressure might all find that useful. In that sense, wearable tech could slip into daily life not with a bang, but with a quiet kind of necessity.
components.chinese: 例如，未來一個人戴著一部設備，而它會提醒他坐得太久、睡得太少，或者把自己逼得太緊，這可能會變得非常普通。忙碌的上班族、長者，甚至壓力很大的學生，都可能會覺得這類設備很實用。從這個角度來看，穿戴式科技進入日常生活，未必是轟轟烈烈地出現，而更可能是一種安靜但實際的必需。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, in the future, it may be perfectly normal for someone
to wear a device that warns them when they have been sitting too long, sleeping
too little, or pushing themselves too hard. Busy workers, older people, and even
students under pressure might all find that useful. In that sense, wearable tech
could slip into daily life not with a bang, but with a quiet kind of necessity.
[例如，未來一個人戴著一部設備，而它會提醒他坐得太久、睡得太少，或者把自己逼得太
緊，這可能會變得非常普通。忙碌的上班族、長者，甚至壓力很大的學生，都可能會覺得這
類設備很實用。從這個角度來看，穿戴式科技進入日常生活，未必是轟轟烈烈地出現，而更
可能是一種安靜但實際的必需。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the most popular devices in the future will probably be the ones that are smart, useful, and almost effortless to live with. Flashy gadgets may come and go, but devices that save time, support health, and fit naturally into people’s routines are far more likely to stand the test of time.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，未來最受歡迎的設備，很可能會是那些聰明、實用，而且幾乎不需要人費力適應的設備。花巧的新玩意也許會一時流行，但真正能節省時間、支援健康，並且自然融入日常生活的設備，才更有可能經得起時間考驗。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the most popular devices in the future will
probably be the ones that are smart, useful, and almost effortless to live with.
Flashy gadgets may come and go, but devices that save time, support health, and
fit naturally into people’s routines are far more likely to stand the test of time.
[總結來說，我會說，未來最受歡迎的設備，很可能會是那些聰明、實用，而且幾乎不需要人
費力適應的設備。花巧的新玩意也許會一時流行，但真正能節省時間、支援健康，並且自然
融入日常生活的設備，才更有可能經得起時間考驗。]

question.english: Do you think people spend too much money on electronic devices?
question.chinese: 你認為人們在電子產品上花太多錢嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think people spend too much money on electronic devices?
[你認為人們在電子產品上花太多錢嗎？]

components.english: Yes, I do think many people spend more than they really need to on electronic devices, especially when they keep upgrading things that still work perfectly well.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為很多人在電子產品上花的錢，比真正需要的還要多，尤其是當他們不斷更換那些其實仍然運作得很好、完全未壞的產品時。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think many people spend more than they really need to on
electronic devices, especially when they keep upgrading things that still work
perfectly well.
[是的，我的確認為很多人在電子產品上花的錢，比真正需要的還要多，尤其是當他們不斷
更換那些其實仍然運作得很好、完全未壞的產品時。]

components.english: Technology moves quickly, and companies are very good at making each new model look irresistible. So people can easily get swept up in the habit of chasing the latest screen, the latest camera, or the latest bells and whistles, even when the difference is fairly minor. In many cases, the purchase is driven less by need than by novelty, convenience, or even status. A phone, for example, can quietly shift from being a tool in your hand to a trophy in your pocket.
components.chinese: 科技發展很快，而公司也很擅長把每一代新產品都包裝得令人難以抗拒。所以，人們很容易被捲進一種習慣裡，不斷追逐最新的螢幕、最新的鏡頭，或者最新的功能，即使實際差別其實並沒有那麼大。在很多情況下，購買的動機與其說是需要，不如說是新鮮感、方便，甚至某種身份象徵。以手機為例，它有時會在不知不覺之間，從手中的工具變成口袋裡的獎盃。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Technology moves quickly, and companies are very good at
making each new model look irresistible. So people can easily get swept up in the
habit of chasing the latest screen, the latest camera, or the latest bells and
whistles, even when the difference is fairly minor. In many cases, the purchase is
driven less by need than by novelty, convenience, or even status. A phone, for
example, can quietly shift from being a tool in your hand to a trophy in your
pocket.
[科技發展很快，而公司也很擅長把每一代新產品都包裝得令人難以抗拒。所以，人們很容
易被捲進一種習慣裡，不斷追逐最新的螢幕、最新的鏡頭，或者最新的功能，即使實際差別
其實並沒有那麼大。在很多情況下，購買的動機與其說是需要，不如說是新鮮感、方便，甚
至某種身份象徵。以手機為例，它有時會在不知不覺之間，從手中的工具變成口袋裡的獎

components.english: For example, some people replace their phones every year even though the old one is still fast, reliable, and good enough for everyday use. They may be drawn in by better marketing, a sleeker design, or the feeling that they are missing out. Before they know it, they are paying a premium for a very small jump forward.
components.chinese: 例如，有些人每年都會換手機，儘管舊手機其實仍然夠快、夠穩定，而且足以應付日常需要。他們可能是被更厲害的宣傳、更流線的設計，或者一種「怕自己落後」的感覺吸引住。等他們回過神來，其實已經為一點點小升級付出了很高的代價。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, some people replace their phones every year even
though the old one is still fast, reliable, and good enough for everyday use. They
may be drawn in by better marketing, a sleeker design, or the feeling that they
are missing out. Before they know it, they are paying a premium for a very small
jump forward.
[例如，有些人每年都會換手機，儘管舊手機其實仍然夠快、夠穩定，而且足以應付日常需
要。他們可能是被更厲害的宣傳、更流線的設計，或者一種「怕自己落後」的感覺吸引住。等
他們回過神來，其實已經為一點點小升級付出了很高的代價。]

components.english: So yes, in that sense, I would say a lot of people do spend too much on electronic devices, because they are buying into the excitement of the upgrade rather than the value of the device itself.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，從這個角度來看，我會說很多人的確在電子產品上花了太多錢，因為他們買下的往往不是產品本身真正的價值，而是升級所帶來的那種興奮感。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, in that sense, I would say a lot of people do spend too
much on electronic devices, because they are buying into the excitement of the
upgrade rather than the value of the device itself.
[所以，是的，從這個角度來看，我會說很多人的確在電子產品上花了太多錢，因為他們買
下的往往不是產品本身真正的價值，而是升級所帶來的那種興奮感。]

components.english: That said, I would not say all spending on electronic devices is excessive, because these days many of them are no longer luxuries; they are basic tools for modern life.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不會說所有在電子產品上的花費都是過度的，因為在今天，很多電子產品早已不再是奢侈品，而是現代生活中的基本工具。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would not say all spending on electronic devices is
excessive, because these days many of them are no longer luxuries; they are basic
tools for modern life.
[不過，我也不會說所有在電子產品上的花費都是過度的，因為在今天，很多電子產品早已
不再是奢侈品，而是現代生活中的基本工具。]

components.english: People now rely on phones, laptops, and tablets for work, study, communication, banking, navigation, and even healthcare. So if someone spends a fair amount on a device that is reliable, long-lasting, and suitable for their needs, that can be money well spent. The real issue is not spending itself, but whether the spending is sensible. In other words, there is a world of difference between investing in a useful device and throwing money at shiny upgrades for the sake of it.
components.chinese: 如今，人們依賴手機、手提電腦和平板去工作、學習、溝通、理財、導航，甚至處理醫療相關事情。所以，如果一個人願意花一筆合理的錢去買一部可靠、耐用，而且真正適合自己需要的裝置，那其實可以是花得很值得。真正的問題不在於「花錢」本身，而在於這筆錢花得是否明智。換句話說，為有用的裝置作投資，和單純為了追逐亮眼升級而亂花錢，兩者之間其實有很大的分別。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: People now rely on phones, laptops, and tablets for work,
study, communication, banking, navigation, and even healthcare. So if someone
spends a fair amount on a device that is reliable, long-lasting, and suitable for
their needs, that can be money well spent. The real issue is not spending itself,
but whether the spending is sensible. In other words, there is a world of
difference between investing in a useful device and throwing money at shiny
upgrades for the sake of it.
[如今，人們依賴手機、手提電腦和平板去工作、學習、溝通、理財、導航，甚至處理醫療相
關事情。所以，如果一個人願意花一筆合理的錢去買一部可靠、耐用，而且真正適合自己需
要的裝置，那其實可以是花得很值得。真正的問題不在於「花錢」本身，而在於這筆錢花得是
否明智。換句話說，為有用的裝置作投資，和單純為了追逐亮眼升級而亂花錢，兩者之間其
實有很大的分別。]

components.english: For instance, a university student may need a good laptop for essays, research, and online classes, while an office worker may need a dependable phone and computer to stay productive. In those cases, paying more for quality can actually save trouble, time, and money in the long run.
components.chinese: 例如，一個大學生可能真的需要一部好的手提電腦去寫論文、做研究，以及上網課；而一位上班族也可能需要一部可靠的手機和電腦來維持工作效率。在這些情況下，多花一點錢買高質素的產品，長遠來說反而可能省下更多麻煩、時間，甚至金錢。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a university student may need a good laptop for essays,
research, and online classes, while an office worker may need a dependable phone
and computer to stay productive. In those cases, paying more for quality can
actually save trouble, time, and money in the long run.
[例如，一個大學生可能真的需要一部好的手提電腦去寫論文、做研究，以及上網課；而一
位上班族也可能需要一部可靠的手機和電腦來維持工作效率。在這些情況下，多花一點錢
買高質素的產品，長遠來說反而可能省下更多麻煩、時間，甚至金錢。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people often do spend too much on electronic devices when they confuse want with need. However, when a device genuinely supports work or daily life, spending money on it is not wasteful at all. The issue in mind is not the technology itself, but the mindset behind the purchase.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，當人們把「想要」誤當成「需要」時，他們往往的確會在電子產品上花太多錢。不過，當一部裝置真的能支援工作或日常生活時，花錢在它身上其實一點也不算浪費。總結來說，問題不在科技本身，而在購買背後的心態。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people often do spend too much on electronic
devices when they confuse want with need. However, when a device genuinely
supports work or daily life, spending money on it is not wasteful at all. The
issue in mind is not the technology itself, but the mindset behind the purchase.
[總結來說，我會說，當人們把「想要」誤當成「需要」時，他們往往的確會在電子產品上花太
多錢。不過，當一部裝置真的能支援工作或日常生活時，花錢在它身上其實一點也不算浪
費。總結來說，問題不在科技本身，而在購買背後的心態。]

question.english: In what ways can electronic devices make our lives harder?
question.chinese: 電子設備可以在哪些方面令我們的生活變得更困難？

source_lines.text: Topic: In what ways can electronic devices make our lives harder?
電子設備可以在哪些方面令我們的生活變得更困難？

components.english: I think electronic devices can make our lives harder in quite a few ways, especially by distracting us and making it difficult to switch off.
components.chinese: 我認為，電子設備其實可以在不少方面令生活變得更困難，尤其是它們很容易令人分心，也令人很難真正放鬆下來。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think electronic devices can make our lives harder in quite a few ways,
especially by distracting us and making it difficult to switch off.
[我認為，電子設備其實可以在不少方面令生活變得更困難，尤其是它們很容易令人分心，
也令人很難真正放鬆下來。]

components.english: On the one hand, devices are meant to save time, but on the other hand, they can quietly steal it. Phones keep buzzing, screens keep flashing, and our attention gets pulled in ten different directions. As a result, people often find it harder to focus, harder to rest, and harder to be fully present. In that sense, a device can be a bit of a double-edged sword: it puts the world at our fingertips, but it also makes peace and quiet harder to come by.
components.chinese: 一方面，電子設備本來是要替人節省時間；但另一方面，它們也可能悄悄把時間偷走。手機不停震動、螢幕不停亮起，而我們的注意力就這樣被拉向四方八面。結果，人們往往更難專注、更難休息，也更難真正活在當下。從這個角度來看，電子設備有點像一把雙刃劍：它把世界送到我們指尖之上，卻也令平靜和安寧變得更不容易得到。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: On the one hand, devices are meant to save time, but on the
other hand, they can quietly steal it. Phones keep buzzing, screens keep flashing,
and our attention gets pulled in ten different directions. As a result, people
often find it harder to focus, harder to rest, and harder to be fully present. In
that sense, a device can be a bit of a double-edged sword: it puts the world at our
fingertips, but it also makes peace and quiet harder to come by.
[一方面，電子設備本來是要替人節省時間；但另一方面，它們也可能悄悄把時間偷走。手
機不停震動、螢幕不停亮起，而我們的注意力就這樣被拉向四方八面。結果，人們往往更難
專注、更難休息，也更難真正活在當下。從這個角度來看，電子設備有點像一把雙刃劍：它
把世界送到我們指尖之上，卻也令平靜和安寧變得更不容易得到。]

components.english: For example, someone may sit down to do one important task on a laptop, but then end up checking messages, scrolling through social media, replying to emails, and forgetting what they were supposed to do in the first place. Before they know it, an hour has slipped through their fingers.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人本來只是坐下來用手提電腦完成一項重要工作，但最後卻可能先去看訊息、滑社交媒體、回覆電郵，然後連自己原本要做甚麼都差點忘了。不知不覺之間，一小時就已經從指縫間溜走。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone may sit down to do one important task on a
laptop, but then end up checking messages, scrolling through social media,
replying to emails, and forgetting what they were supposed to do in the first
place. Before they know it, an hour has slipped through their fingers.
[例如，一個人本來只是坐下來用手提電腦完成一項重要工作，但最後卻可能先去看訊息、
滑社交媒體、回覆電郵，然後連自己原本要做甚麼都差點忘了。不知不覺之間，一小時就已
經從指縫間溜走。]

components.english: So, one major way electronic devices make life harder is by turning attention into a battleground and making it harder for people to stay focused and calm.
components.chinese: 所以，電子設備令生活變得更困難的一個主要方式，就是把人的注意力變成一個拉鋸戰，令專注和內心平靜都更難維持。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major way electronic devices make life harder is by
turning attention into a battleground and making it harder for people to stay
focused and calm.
[所以，電子設備令生活變得更困難的一個主要方式，就是把人的注意力變成一個拉鋸戰，
令專注和內心平靜都更難維持。]

components.english: On top of that, electronic devices can also make people more dependent, more stressed, and sometimes less able to function smoothly without them.
components.chinese: 除此之外，電子設備也會令人變得更依賴、更有壓力，有時甚至令人在沒有它們的情況下更難順利生活。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, electronic devices can also make people more
dependent, more stressed, and sometimes less able to function smoothly without
them.
[除此之外，電子設備也會令人變得更依賴、更有壓力，有時甚至令人在沒有它們的情況下
更難順利生活。]

components.english: The problem is that convenience can become a crutch. When people rely too heavily on their phones for directions, reminders, communication, shopping, and even entertainment, a dead battery or a bad connection can throw the whole day off balance. There is also the pressure of always being reachable. With messages, emails, and notifications following people everywhere, work can creep into private life and rest can begin to feel like a luxury. In other words, devices do not just sit in our pockets; sometimes they end up running the show.
components.chinese: 問題在於，便利有時會慢慢變成拐杖。當人們過度依賴手機去導航、提醒自己、溝通、購物，甚至娛樂時，只要手機沒電，或者網絡不好，整天的節奏都可能被打亂。除此之外，還有一種「隨時都要聯絡得到」的壓力。當訊息、電郵和通知無處不在時，工作就會慢慢滲進私人生活，而休息反而開始像一種奢侈。換句話說，電子設備不只是安靜地放在我們口袋裡；有時它們反而像是在主導整場生活。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The problem is that convenience can become a crutch. When
people rely too heavily on their phones for directions, reminders,
communication, shopping, and even entertainment, a dead battery or a bad
connection can throw the whole day off balance. There is also the pressure of
always being reachable. With messages, emails, and notifications following
people everywhere, work can creep into private life and rest can begin to feel
like a luxury. In other words, devices do not just sit in our pockets; sometimes
they end up running the show.
[問題在於，便利有時會慢慢變成拐杖。當人們過度依賴手機去導航、提醒自己、溝通、購物
，甚至娛樂時，只要手機沒電，或者網絡不好，整天的節奏都可能被打亂。除此之外，還有一
種「隨時都要聯絡得到」的壓力。當訊息、電郵和通知無處不在時，工作就會慢慢滲進私人生
活，而休息反而開始像一種奢侈。換句話說，電子設備不只是安靜地放在我們口袋裡；有時
它們反而像是在主導整場生活。]

components.english: For instance, if someone uses their phone as an alarm clock, diary, map, wallet, and way of keeping in touch, losing it for even half a day can feel like losing a small part of their life. They may not know where to go, how to pay, or whom to contact. That kind of dependence can be quite unsettling.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人把手機當作鬧鐘、記事本、地圖、錢包，以及聯絡別人的主要工具，那麼即使只是丟失半天，都可能令人覺得像失去了一小部分生活。他可能不知道要怎樣去目的地、怎樣付款，或者怎樣聯絡別人。這種依賴感其實相當令人不安。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if someone uses their phone as an alarm clock, diary,
map, wallet, and way of keeping in touch, losing it for even half a day can feel
like losing a small part of their life. They may not know where to go, how to
pay, or whom to contact. That kind of dependence can be quite unsettling.
[例如，如果一個人把手機當作鬧鐘、記事本、地圖、錢包，以及聯絡別人的主要工具，那麼
即使只是丟失半天，都可能令人覺得像失去了一小部分生活。他可能不知道要怎樣去目的
地、怎樣付款，或者怎樣聯絡別人。這種依賴感其實相當令人不安。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say electronic devices make life harder when they begin to control our attention, blur the line between work and rest, and make us too dependent on constant connection. They are incredibly useful, of course, but if we are not careful, the tools that are meant to serve us can slowly start to master us.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，當電子設備開始控制我們的注意力、模糊工作與休息之間的界線，並令我們過度依賴持續連線時，它們就會令生活變得更困難。當然，它們非常有用；但如果我們不夠留神，那些本來應該為我們服務的工具，就可能慢慢反過來主導我們。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say electronic devices make life harder when they
begin to control our attention, blur the line between work and rest, and make
us too dependent on constant connection. They are incredibly useful, of course,
but if we are not careful, the tools that are meant to serve us can slowly start to
master us.
[總結來說，我會說，當電子設備開始控制我們的注意力、模糊工作與休息之間的界線，並
令我們過度依賴持續連線時，它們就會令生活變得更困難。當然，它們非常有用；但如果我
們不夠留神，那些本來應該為我們服務的工具，就可能慢慢反過來主導我們。]

question.english: What would the world be like without computers?
question.chinese: 如果世界上沒有電腦，會是怎樣的？

source_lines.text: Topic: What would the world be like without computers?
如果世界上沒有電腦，會是怎樣的？

components.english: I think the world would be far slower, less connected, and much less efficient, because computers now sit at the heart of modern life.
components.chinese: 我認為，如果沒有電腦，世界會變得慢得多、連結少得多，而且效率也會大幅下降，因為電腦現在其實已經處於現代生活的核心。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the world would be far slower, less connected, and much less
efficient, because computers now sit at the heart of modern life.
[我認為，如果沒有電腦，世界會變得慢得多、連結少得多，而且效率也會大幅下降，因為電
腦現在其實已經處於現代生活的核心。]

components.english: From communication and education to banking, transport, and healthcare, computers are a bit like the nervous system of the modern world. Without them, many things that now happen at the touch of a button would become time-consuming and clumsy again. People could still live, of course, but daily life would feel heavier, rougher, and far less streamlined. In some ways, it would be like trying to run today’s world with yesterday’s tools.
components.chinese: 從溝通、教育，到銀行、交通和醫療，電腦其實有點像現代世界的神經系統。要是沒有它們，很多現在按一個按鈕就能完成的事情，都會重新變得費時又笨重。當然，人還是可以生活下去，但日常生活會變得更吃力、更粗糙，也遠遠沒有現在那麼順暢。在某程度上，那就像是想用昨天的工具去運作今天的世界。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: From communication and education to banking, transport, and
healthcare, computers are a bit like the nervous system of the modern world.
Without them, many things that now happen at the touch of a button would
become time-consuming and clumsy again. People could still live, of course, but
daily life would feel heavier, rougher, and far less streamlined. In some ways, it
would be like trying to run today’s world with yesterday’s tools.
[從溝通、教育，到銀行、交通和醫療，電腦其實有點像現代世界的神經系統。要是沒有它們
，很多現在按一個按鈕就能完成的事情，都會重新變得費時又笨重。當然，人還是可以生活
下去，但日常生活會變得更吃力、更粗糙，也遠遠沒有現在那麼順暢。在某程度上，那就像
是想用昨天的工具去運作今天的世界。]

components.english: For example, a student would have to spend much more time looking for information in physical libraries, an office worker would deal with endless paperwork, and even simple things like booking tickets or sending documents would take far longer. Tasks that now feel almost effortless could easily become a real headache.
components.chinese: 例如，一個學生可能要花更多時間到實體圖書館找資料；上班族則要處理沒完沒了的紙本文件；甚至像訂票或寄送文件這些簡單事情，也都會花上長得多的時間。那些現在幾乎不費吹灰之力就能完成的事，很容易就會變成令人頭痛的麻煩。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a student would have to spend much more time
looking for information in physical libraries, an office worker would deal with
endless paperwork, and even simple things like booking tickets or sending
documents would take far longer. Tasks that now feel almost effortless could
easily become a real headache.
[例如，一個學生可能要花更多時間到實體圖書館找資料；上班族則要處理沒完沒了的紙本
文件；甚至像訂票或寄送文件這些簡單事情，也都會花上長得多的時間。那些現在幾乎不費
吹灰之力就能完成的事，很容易就會變成令人頭痛的麻煩。]

components.english: So, overall, I would say a world without computers would still function, but it would move at a much slower pace and feel far less convenient than the one we know today.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我會說沒有電腦的世界仍然可以運作，但它的節奏會慢得多，也會比我們今天熟悉的世界不方便得多。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I would say a world without computers would still
function, but it would move at a much slower pace and feel far less convenient
than the one we know today.
[所以，整體來說，我會說沒有電腦的世界仍然可以運作，但它的節奏會慢得多，也會比我
們今天熟悉的世界不方便得多。]

components.english: That said, I do think such a world might also feel simpler, quieter, and perhaps a little more human in some respects.
components.chinese: 不過，我的確也認為，這樣的世界在某些方面可能會顯得更簡單、更安靜，甚至更有人味。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think such a world might also feel simpler, quieter, and
perhaps a little more human in some respects.
[不過，我的確也認為，這樣的世界在某些方面可能會顯得更簡單、更安靜，甚至更有人

components.english: Without computers, people might spend less time staring at screens, scrolling endlessly, or being pulled in ten different directions by notifications. Face-to-face conversation could play a bigger role again, and life might feel a little less rushed and less digitally crowded. In other words, without computers, we might lose a great deal of convenience, but we might also get back a little breathing space.
components.chinese: 如果沒有電腦，人們或許會少花一些時間盯著螢幕、無止境地滑動內容，或者被通知訊息拉向四面八方。面對面的交流可能會重新變得更重要，而生活也可能顯得沒有那麼急促、沒有那麼被數碼資訊塞得滿滿的。換句話說，沒有電腦，我們固然會失去大量便利，但也可能重新拿回一點喘息空間。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Without computers, people might spend less time staring at
screens, scrolling endlessly, or being pulled in ten different directions by
notifications. Face-to-face conversation could play a bigger role again, and life
might feel a little less rushed and less digitally crowded. In other words, without
computers, we might lose a great deal of convenience, but we might also get
back a little breathing space.
[如果沒有電腦，人們或許會少花一些時間盯著螢幕、無止境地滑動內容，或者被通知訊息
拉向四面八方。面對面的交流可能會重新變得更重要，而生活也可能顯得沒有那麼急促、沒
有那麼被數碼資訊塞得滿滿的。換句話說，沒有電腦，我們固然會失去大量便利，但也可能
重新拿回一點喘息空間。]

components.english: For instance, instead of messaging each other all day, people might write letters, make more phone calls, or meet in person more often. That kind of life might be less convenient, but it could also feel warmer and more grounded, almost as if the world had turned the volume down.
components.chinese: 例如，人們可能不會整天互傳訊息，而是會寫信、打更多電話，或者更常親身見面。那樣的生活也許沒那麼方便，但同時也可能顯得更溫暖、更踏實，幾乎像是整個世界把音量調低了一樣。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, instead of messaging each other all day, people might
write letters, make more phone calls, or meet in person more often. That kind of
life might be less convenient, but it could also feel warmer and more grounded,
almost as if the world had turned the volume down.
[例如，人們可能不會整天互傳訊息，而是會寫信、打更多電話，或者更常親身見面。那樣的
生活也許沒那麼方便，但同時也可能顯得更溫暖、更踏實，幾乎像是整個世界把音量調低了
一樣。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say a world without computers would probably be less advanced and less efficient, but not entirely worse in every way. It might lose some speed, but gain a little more quiet; lose some convenience, but regain a little more genuine human contact.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說沒有電腦的世界很可能會較不先進、效率也較低，但也不代表每一方面都一定更差。它可能會失去速度，卻多一點安靜；少一點便利，卻多一點真實的人際接觸。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say a world without computers would probably be
less advanced and less efficient, but not entirely worse in every way. It might lose
some speed, but gain a little more quiet; lose some convenience, but regain a
little more genuine human contact.
[總結來說，我會說沒有電腦的世界很可能會較不先進、效率也較低，但也不代表每一方面
都一定更差。它可能會失去速度，卻多一點安靜；少一點便利，卻多一點真實的人際接觸。]

question.english: Should children be taught to use computers at school?
question.chinese: 孩子是否應該在學校學習使用電腦？

source_lines.text: Topic: Should children be taught to use computers at school?
孩子是否應該在學校學習使用電腦？

components.english: Yes, definitely, I think children should be taught to use computers at school, because computer skills are no longer optional; they are part of everyday life.
components.chinese: 是的，我絕對認為孩子應該在學校學習使用電腦，因為電腦技能已經不再是可有可無的東西，而是日常生活的一部分。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, definitely, I think children should be taught to use computers at
school, because computer skills are no longer optional; they are part of everyday
life.
[是的，我絕對認為孩子應該在學校學習使用電腦，因為電腦技能已經不再是可有可無的東
西，而是日常生活的一部分。]

components.english: From homework to future jobs, digital tools are everywhere, so it makes sense for schools to help children learn the ropes early. More importantly, school is probably the best place to teach them not just how to use a computer, but how to use it well — for research, communication, problem-solving, and online safety. If children are surrounded by technology outside school but never taught how to handle it properly, they may end up knowing how to tap and scroll without really understanding what they are doing.
components.chinese: 從功課到未來工作，數碼工具幾乎無處不在，所以由學校幫助孩子及早熟習這些技能，其實很合理。更重要的是，學校大概是教他們不只是「會用電腦」，而是「好好地用電腦」的最佳地方，包括做資料搜集、溝通、解決問題，以及保護網上安全。如果孩子在校外天天接觸科技，卻從來沒有人正式教他們如何正確使用，那他們最後可能只會點按和滑動，卻未必真正明白自己在做甚麼。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: From homework to future jobs, digital tools are everywhere, so
it makes sense for schools to help children learn the ropes early. More
importantly, school is probably the best place to teach them not just how to use
a computer, but how to use it well — for research, communication,
problem-solving, and online safety. If children are surrounded by technology
outside school but never taught how to handle it properly, they may end up
knowing how to tap and scroll without really understanding what they are
doing.
[從功課到未來工作，數碼工具幾乎無處不在，所以由學校幫助孩子及早熟習這些技能，其
實很合理。更重要的是，學校大概是教他們不只是「會用電腦」，而是「好好地用電腦」的最佳
地方，包括做資料搜集、溝通、解決問題，以及保護網上安全。如果孩子在校外天天接觸科
技，卻從來沒有人正式教他們如何正確使用，那他們最後可能只會點按和滑動，卻未必真正
明白自己在做甚麼。]

components.english: For example, a primary or secondary student may need to type assignments, prepare slides, look for reliable information online, or use digital learning platforms. If they have already been taught those skills at school, they are likely to feel more confident and independent rather than lost every time they face a screen.
components.chinese: 例如，小學或中學的學生可能需要打字交功課、做簡報、上網找可靠資料，或者使用數碼學習平台。如果這些技能早已在學校學過，他們面對電腦時通常就會更有信心，也更能獨立處理事情，而不是每次對着螢幕都手足無措。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a primary or secondary student may need to type
assignments, prepare slides, look for reliable information online, or use digital
learning platforms. If they have already been taught those skills at school, they
are likely to feel more confident and independent rather than lost every time
they face a screen.
[例如，小學或中學的學生可能需要打字交功課、做簡報、上網找可靠資料，或者使用數碼學
習平台。如果這些技能早已在學校學過，他們面對電腦時通常就會更有信心，也更能獨立處
理事情，而不是每次對着螢幕都手足無措。]

components.english: So yes, I’d say computer education should be part of school life, because it equips children with practical skills they will almost certainly need later on.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說電腦教育應該成為學校生活的一部分，因為它能讓孩子掌握一些幾乎肯定會在未來派上用場的實用技能。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I’d say computer education should be part of school life,
because it equips children with practical skills they will almost certainly need
later on.
[所以，是的，我會說電腦教育應該成為學校生活的一部分，因為它能讓孩子掌握一些幾乎
肯定會在未來派上用場的實用技能。]

components.english: That said, I do not think computers should dominate the classroom or replace every traditional way of learning.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為電腦應該主導整個課堂，或者取代所有傳統的學習方式。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think computers should dominate the classroom or
replace every traditional way of learning.
[不過，我也不認為電腦應該主導整個課堂，或者取代所有傳統的學習方式。]

components.english: To be honest, too much screen time can be a double-edged sword. It can make lessons more engaging, but it can also shorten attention spans or reduce face-to-face interaction if it is overused. In my view, technology should be a tool, not the teacher; a bridge to learning, not a barrier between students and real human contact. Children still need to write by hand, speak to one another, and learn how to think without a screen doing all the heavy lifting.
components.chinese: 老實說，過多的屏幕時間其實是一把雙刃劍。它可以令課堂更吸引，但如果用得太多，也可能縮短孩子的專注力，或者減少面對面互動的機會。在我看來，科技應該是一種工具，而不是主角；它應該是一座通往學習的橋樑，而不是隔開學生與真實人際接觸的屏障。孩子仍然需要學會手寫、彼此交談，以及在沒有螢幕代勞的情況下自己思考。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, too much screen time can be a double-edged
sword. It can make lessons more engaging, but it can also shorten attention
spans or reduce face-to-face interaction if it is overused. In my view, technology
should be a tool, not the teacher; a bridge to learning, not a barrier between
students and real human contact. Children still need to write by hand, speak to
one another, and learn how to think without a screen doing all the heavy lifting.
[老實說，過多的屏幕時間其實是一把雙刃劍。它可以令課堂更吸引，但如果用得太多，也可
能縮短孩子的專注力，或者減少面對面互動的機會。在我看來，科技應該是一種工具，而不
是主角；它應該是一座通往學習的橋樑，而不是隔開學生與真實人際接觸的屏障。孩子仍然
需要學會手寫、彼此交談，以及在沒有螢幕代勞的情況下自己思考。]

components.english: For instance, a class could use computers to research a topic or create a presentation, but still discuss ideas in groups, take some notes by hand, and learn directly from the teacher. That kind of balance would give children the best of both worlds.
components.chinese: 例如，一班學生可以用電腦去搜集資料或製作簡報，但同時仍然可以分組討論、手寫部分筆記，並直接向老師學習。這樣的平衡，才真正能讓孩子同時得到兩邊的好處。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a class could use computers to research a topic or
create a presentation, but still discuss ideas in groups, take some notes by hand,
and learn directly from the teacher. That kind of balance would give children
the best of both worlds.
[例如，一班學生可以用電腦去搜集資料或製作簡報，但同時仍然可以分組討論、手寫部分
筆記，並直接向老師學習。這樣的平衡，才真正能讓孩子同時得到兩邊的好處。]

components.english: Overall, I think children should absolutely be taught to use computers at school, but the approach needs to be balanced and age-appropriate. The real goal is not to let technology steal the show, but to let it support learning in a smart and sensible way.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我絕對認為孩子應該在學校學習使用電腦，但方式必須平衡，而且要符合年齡需要。真正的目標不是讓科技搶走課堂的焦點，而是讓它以聰明而恰當的方式幫助學習。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think children should absolutely be taught to use
computers at school, but the approach needs to be balanced and
age-appropriate. The real goal is not to let technology steal the show, but to let
it support learning in a smart and sensible way.
[總結來說，我絕對認為孩子應該在學校學習使用電腦，但方式必須平衡，而且要符合年齡
需要。真正的目標不是讓科技搶走課堂的焦點，而是讓它以聰明而恰當的方式幫助學習。]

question.english: Do you think traditional performances are important?
question.chinese: 你認為傳統表演重要嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think traditional performances are important?
你認為傳統表演重要嗎？

components.english: Yes, absolutely. I think traditional performances are very important because they preserve cultural identity and keep history alive in a way that books alone cannot.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對重要。我認為傳統表演非常重要，因為它們能保存文化身份，也能以一種單靠書本無法做到的方式，讓歷史真正活起來。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely. I think traditional performances are very important
because they preserve cultural identity and keep history alive in a way that
books alone cannot.
[是的，絕對重要。我認為傳統表演非常重要，因為它們能保存文化身份，也能以一種單靠
書本無法做到的方式，讓歷史真正活起來。]

components.english: Traditional performances are not just old-fashioned entertainment. They carry stories, values, language, music, gestures, and ways of seeing the world that have been passed down over generations. In that sense, they are a bit like living archives: they do not simply tell people about the past; they let people feel it. If those performances disappear, a culture can lose not only its memory, but also part of its soul.
components.chinese: 傳統表演並不只是老派的娛樂而已。它們承載著故事、價值觀、語言、音樂、動作，以及一代一代流傳下來的世界觀。從這個角度來看，它們有點像活生生的文化檔案：它們不只是告訴人們過去發生過甚麼，而是讓人真正感受到那段過去。如果這些表演消失了，一種文化失去的就不只是記憶，還可能是靈魂的一部分。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Traditional performances are not just old-fashioned
entertainment. They carry stories, values, language, music, gestures, and ways of
seeing the world that have been passed down over generations. In that sense,
they are a bit like living archives: they do not simply tell people about the past;
they let people feel it. If those performances disappear, a culture can lose not
only its memory, but also part of its soul.
[傳統表演並不只是老派的娛樂而已。它們承載著故事、價值觀、語言、音樂、動作，以及一
代一代流傳下來的世界觀。從這個角度來看，它們有點像活生生的文化檔案：它們不只是告
訴人們過去發生過甚麼，而是讓人真正感受到那段過去。如果這些表演消失了，一種文化失
去的就不只是記憶，還可能是靈魂的一部分。]

components.english: For example, when people watch a traditional opera, folk dance, or festival performance, they are not only watching a show; they are also coming into contact with the customs and emotions of earlier generations. That kind of experience can strike a chord in a way that a textbook rarely does.
components.chinese: 例如，當人們觀看傳統戲曲、民間舞蹈，或者節慶表演時，他們不只是在看一場演出；他們同時也在接觸早期世代的習俗和情感。這種體驗往往能真正打動人，而這正是課本很少能做到的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, when people watch a traditional opera, folk dance, or
festival performance, they are not only watching a show; they are also coming
into contact with the customs and emotions of earlier generations. That kind of
experience can strike a chord in a way that a textbook rarely does.
[例如，當人們觀看傳統戲曲、民間舞蹈，或者節慶表演時，他們不只是在看一場演出；他們
同時也在接觸早期世代的習俗和情感。這種體驗往往能真正打動人，而這正是課本很少能
做到的。]

components.english: So yes, I would say traditional performances matter a great deal, because they help a culture remember who it is and where it comes from.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說傳統表演非常重要，因為它們能幫助一種文化記得自己是誰，也記得自己從哪裡而來。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say traditional performances matter a great deal,
because they help a culture remember who it is and where it comes from.
[所以，是的，我會說傳統表演非常重要，因為它們能幫助一種文化記得自己是誰，也記得
自己從哪裡而來。]

components.english: Another reason they are important is that they bring people together and create a sense of shared belonging.
components.chinese: 它們之所以重要，另一個原因是它們能把人們聚在一起，並創造一種共同的歸屬感。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason they are important is that they bring people together
and create a sense of shared belonging.
[它們之所以重要，另一個原因是它們能把人們聚在一起，並創造一種共同的歸屬感。]

components.english: In today’s world, life can be so fast, fragmented, and screen-driven that people often lose touch with local traditions and with one another. Traditional performances can act as a cultural meeting point. They give communities something to gather around, celebrate, and pass on. To me, that is priceless. A tradition survives not by sitting in a glass case, but by being lived, watched, heard, and felt.
components.chinese: 在今天這個世界，生活節奏可以快得驚人，也很碎片化，而且很多事情都被螢幕主導，所以人們很容易和本地傳統失去連結，也和彼此失去連結。傳統表演正好可以成為一個文化上的相聚點。它們讓社群有一樣東西可以一起圍繞、一起慶祝，也一起傳承下去。對我來說，這是非常珍貴的。一項傳統要活下去，並不是靠被鎖在玻璃櫃裡，而是靠被真正活出來、看見、聽見，也感受到。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In today’s world, life can be so fast, fragmented, and
screen-driven that people often lose touch with local traditions and with one
another. Traditional performances can act as a cultural meeting point. They give
communities something to gather around, celebrate, and pass on. To me, that is
priceless. A tradition survives not by sitting in a glass case, but by being lived,
watched, heard, and felt.
[在今天這個世界，生活節奏可以快得驚人，也很碎片化，而且很多事情都被螢幕主導，所
以人們很容易和本地傳統失去連結，也和彼此失去連結。傳統表演正好可以成為一個文化
上的相聚點。它們讓社群有一樣東西可以一起圍繞、一起慶祝，也一起傳承下去。對我來說
，這是非常珍貴的。一項傳統要活下去，並不是靠被鎖在玻璃櫃裡，而是靠被真正活出來、
看見、聽見，也感受到。]

components.english: For instance, when families attend a traditional performance during a festival, children, parents, and grandparents can all share the same experience, even if they take away different meanings from it. That is how culture continues to breathe rather than gather dust.
components.chinese: 例如，當一家人一起在節日時去看一場傳統表演時，小孩、父母和祖父母其實都能共享同一段經驗，即使他們從中感受到的意義未必完全一樣。文化也正是透過這種方式繼續呼吸，而不是慢慢蒙塵。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, when families attend a traditional performance during
a festival, children, parents, and grandparents can all share the same experience,
even if they take away different meanings from it. That is how culture continues
to breathe rather than gather dust.
[例如，當一家人一起在節日時去看一場傳統表演時，小孩、父母和祖父母其實都能共享同
一段經驗，即使他們從中感受到的意義未必完全一樣。文化也正是透過這種方式繼續呼吸，
而不是慢慢蒙塵。]

components.english: Overall, I definitely think traditional performances are important. That said, they should not be preserved as museum pieces only; they also need to be presented in ways that keep them meaningful and relevant to younger generations.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我絕對認為傳統表演很重要。不過，它們不應該只是像博物館展品一樣被保存起來；它們同時也需要以能讓年輕一代覺得有意義、也有關聯的方式被呈現。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I definitely think traditional performances are
important. That said, they should not be preserved as museum pieces only; they
also need to be presented in ways that keep them meaningful and relevant to
younger generations.
[總結來說，我絕對認為傳統表演很重要。不過，它們不應該只是像博物館展品一樣被保存
起來；它們同時也需要以能讓年輕一代覺得有意義、也有關聯的方式被呈現。]

question.english: What do you think is the difference between watching a live performance and watching it on TV?
question.chinese: 你認為現場看表演和在電視上看，有甚麼分別？

source_lines.text: Topic: What do you think is the difference between watching a live performance
and watching it on TV?
你認為現場看表演和在電視上看，有甚麼分別？

components.english: I think the biggest difference is that a live performance feels far more immediate and immersive, whereas watching it on TV feels more distant and controlled.
components.chinese: 我認為，最大的分別在於：現場表演會令人更有置身其中的感覺，而在電視上看則相對較有距離感，也更像是經過安排和控制的觀看體驗。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the biggest difference is that a live performance feels far more
immediate and immersive, whereas watching it on TV feels more distant and
controlled.
[我認為，最大的分別在於：現場表演會令人更有置身其中的感覺，而在電視上看則相對較
有距離感，也更像是經過安排和控制的觀看體驗。]

components.english: When you watch a performance live, you are not just seeing it; you are stepping into the atmosphere. You can hear the crowd, feel the tension or excitement in the air, and react in real time along with everyone else. In that sense, a live show has a pulse of its own. By contrast, TV gives you the performance through a screen, so even if the picture is clear, some of the magic can get flattened out. It is a bit like standing in the rain versus watching rain through a window: both are real, but they do not feel the same.
components.chinese: 當你現場看表演時，你不只是「看見」它，而是整個人走進了那種氛圍裡。你可以聽到觀眾的反應，感受到空氣中的緊張或興奮，也能和其他人一起即時作出反應。從這個角度來看，現場演出本身就像有自己的脈搏。相比之下，電視是透過螢幕把表演傳遞給你，所以即使畫面很清晰，有些魅力還是會被壓平一點。這有點像你親自站在雨中，和隔著窗看雨的分別：兩者都是真的，但感受並不一樣。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When you watch a performance live, you are not just seeing it;
you are stepping into the atmosphere. You can hear the crowd, feel the tension
or excitement in the air, and react in real time along with everyone else. In that
sense, a live show has a pulse of its own. By contrast, TV gives you the
performance through a screen, so even if the picture is clear, some of the magic
can get flattened out. It is a bit like standing in the rain versus watching rain
through a window: both are real, but they do not feel the same.
[當你現場看表演時，你不只是「看見」它，而是整個人走進了那種氛圍裡。你可以聽到觀眾
的反應，感受到空氣中的緊張或興奮，也能和其他人一起即時作出反應。從這個角度來看，
現場演出本身就像有自己的脈搏。相比之下，電視是透過螢幕把表演傳遞給你，所以即使畫
面很清晰，有些魅力還是會被壓平一點。這有點像你親自站在雨中，和隔著窗看雨的分別：
兩者都是真的，但感受並不一樣。]

components.english: For example, if you go to a live concert, the cheering, the lighting, and even the vibration of the music can hit you all at once. That shared energy often becomes part of the memory. On TV, you can still enjoy the songs, of course, but you are more of an observer than a participant.
components.chinese: 例如，如果你去看一場現場演唱會，歡呼聲、燈光效果，甚至音樂震動的感覺，都可能一下子同時衝過來。那種大家一起投入的能量，往往會成為記憶的一部分。當然，在電視上你仍然可以欣賞歌曲，但那時你比較像一位旁觀者，而不是參與其中的人。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if you go to a live concert, the cheering, the lighting,
and even the vibration of the music can hit you all at once. That shared energy
often becomes part of the memory. On TV, you can still enjoy the songs, of
course, but you are more of an observer than a participant.
[例如，如果你去看一場現場演唱會，歡呼聲、燈光效果，甚至音樂震動的感覺，都可能一下
子同時衝過來。那種大家一起投入的能量，往往會成為記憶的一部分。當然，在電視上你仍
然可以欣賞歌曲，但那時你比較像一位旁觀者，而不是參與其中的人。]

components.english: So, to me, the main difference is that live performances usually feel more vivid, emotional, and unforgettable, because they pull you into the moment rather than simply showing it to you.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，最主要的分別就是：現場表演通常會更加鮮明、更有情感，也更令人難忘，因為它是把你拉進那個時刻裡，而不只是把那個時刻展示給你看。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, the main difference is that live performances usually
feel more vivid, emotional, and unforgettable, because they pull you into the
moment rather than simply showing it to you.
[所以，在我看來，最主要的分別就是：現場表演通常會更加鮮明、更有情感，也更令人難忘
，因為它是把你拉進那個時刻裡，而不只是把那個時刻展示給你看。]

components.english: That said, watching a performance on TV has its own advantages, because it is more convenient, more comfortable, and sometimes even easier to follow.
components.chinese: 不過，在電視上看表演也有它自己的好處，因為那樣更方便、更舒服，而且有時候甚至更容易看清楚整個表演。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, watching a performance on TV has its own advantages,
because it is more convenient, more comfortable, and sometimes even easier to
follow.
[不過，在電視上看表演也有它自己的好處，因為那樣更方便、更舒服，而且有時候甚至更
容易看清楚整個表演。]

components.english: On TV, people can watch from the comfort of home, avoid the crowd, and often get a better visual angle than they would from a seat far away in a venue. The cameras do some of the work for you: they zoom in, switch angles, and bring details to the surface. So while TV may lose some of the atmosphere, it can make the performance more accessible and less exhausting. In other words, live performance gives you the electricity of the moment, while TV gives you ease and clarity.
components.chinese: 在電視上看時，人們可以舒舒服服地留在家裡，不用擠人群，而且很多時候還能比在場館裡坐得很遠時看得更清楚。鏡頭某程度上會替你做好一部分工作：它會拉近畫面、切換角度，並把一些細節帶到你眼前。所以，雖然電視可能少了一點現場氣氛，但它的確能令表演變得更容易接觸，也沒那麼累人。換句話說，現場表演給你的是當下的電流感，而電視給你的是輕鬆和清晰。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: On TV, people can watch from the comfort of home, avoid the
crowd, and often get a better visual angle than they would from a seat far away
in a venue. The cameras do some of the work for you: they zoom in, switch
angles, and bring details to the surface. So while TV may lose some of the
atmosphere, it can make the performance more accessible and less exhausting. In
other words, live performance gives you the electricity of the moment, while TV
gives you ease and clarity.
[在電視上看時，人們可以舒舒服服地留在家裡，不用擠人群，而且很多時候還能比在場館
裡坐得很遠時看得更清楚。鏡頭某程度上會替你做好一部分工作：它會拉近畫面、切換角度
，並把一些細節帶到你眼前。所以，雖然電視可能少了一點現場氣氛，但它的確能令表演變
得更容易接觸，也沒那麼累人。換句話說，現場表演給你的是當下的電流感，而電視給你的
是輕鬆和清晰。]

components.english: For instance, if someone is watching a live theatre show from the back row, they may miss facial expressions or tiny details. On TV, however, a close-up can bring those details front and centre. In the same way, older people or families may prefer watching at home simply because it is easier and more practical.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人在劇院後排看現場演出，他可能會錯過演員的表情，或者一些細微的動作。不過，在電視上，一個特寫鏡頭就可以把那些細節清楚地送到眼前。同樣地，長者或家庭觀眾也可能會更喜歡在家裡看，因為那樣確實更輕鬆，也更實際。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if someone is watching a live theatre show from the
back row, they may miss facial expressions or tiny details. On TV, however, a
close-up can bring those details front and centre. In the same way, older people
or families may prefer watching at home simply because it is easier and more
practical.
[例如，如果一個人在劇院後排看現場演出，他可能會錯過演員的表情，或者一些細微的動
作。不過，在電視上，一個特寫鏡頭就可以把那些細節清楚地送到眼前。同樣地，長者或家
庭觀眾也可能會更喜歡在家裡看，因為那樣確實更輕鬆，也更實際。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the difference comes down to experience versus convenience. A live performance usually feels more alive and emotionally charged, while TV makes it easier, clearer, and more accessible. Neither is worthless, but if you want the full atmosphere, live performance is hard to beat.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說兩者的分別主要在於「體驗感」和「方便程度」的不同。現場表演通常更有生命力，也更容易帶動情緒；而電視則讓觀看變得更輕鬆、更清晰，也更容易接觸。兩者都不是沒有價值，只不過如果你想感受最完整的氣氛，現場表演通常還是很難被取代。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the difference comes down to experience versus
convenience. A live performance usually feels more alive and emotionally
charged, while TV makes it easier, clearer, and more accessible. Neither is
worthless, but if you want the full atmosphere, live performance is hard to beat.
[總結來說，我會說兩者的分別主要在於「體驗感」和「方便程度」的不同。現場表演通常更有
生命力，也更容易帶動情緒；而電視則讓觀看變得更輕鬆、更清晰，也更容易接觸。兩者都
不是沒有價值，只不過如果你想感受最完整的氣氛，現場表演通常還是很難被取代。]

question.english: How do you think watching a dance performance or a stage play influences children?
question.chinese: 你認為觀看舞蹈表演或舞台劇會怎樣影響孩子？

source_lines.text: Topic: How do you think watching a dance performance or a stage play
influences children?
[你認為觀看舞蹈表演或舞台劇會怎樣影響孩子？]

components.english: I think watching a dance performance or a stage play can influence children in a very positive way, especially by developing their imagination and emotional awareness.
components.chinese: 我認為，觀看舞蹈表演或舞台劇能對孩子帶來很正面的影響，尤其是在培養想像力和情感感受力這兩方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think watching a dance performance or a stage play can influence
children in a very positive way, especially by developing their imagination and
emotional awareness.
[我認為，觀看舞蹈表演或舞台劇能對孩子帶來很正面的影響，尤其是在培養想像力和情感
感受力這兩方面。]

components.english: The main reason is that live performance brings stories, feelings, and human expression to life right in front of a child’s eyes. Unlike information on a page, or even something on a screen, a performance feels immediate and alive. Children are not just following words; they are reading faces, movements, music, rhythm, and atmosphere all at once. In that sense, it opens a window onto emotion. A dance piece can show sadness, joy, tension, or hope without saying a word, while a stage play can help children step into someone else’s shoes and see the world from another angle.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現場表演會把故事、情感，以及人的表達方式直接活生生地帶到孩子眼前。和書本上的資訊，甚至螢幕上的畫面相比，表演給人的感覺更即時，也更有生命力。孩子不只是跟著文字走，他們同時也在讀表情、動作、音樂、節奏，以及整體氣氛。從這個角度來看，它就像替情感打開了一扇窗。舞蹈作品可以在一句話也不說的情況下表現悲傷、快樂、緊張，或者希望；而舞台劇則能幫助孩子走進別人的處境，從另一個角度去看世界。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that live performance brings stories, feelings,
and human expression to life right in front of a child’s eyes. Unlike information
on a page, or even something on a screen, a performance feels immediate and
alive. Children are not just following words; they are reading faces, movements,
music, rhythm, and atmosphere all at once. In that sense, it opens a window
onto emotion. A dance piece can show sadness, joy, tension, or hope without
saying a word, while a stage play can help children step into someone else’s shoes
and see the world from another angle.
[主要原因是，現場表演會把故事、情感，以及人的表達方式直接活生生地帶到孩子眼前。
和書本上的資訊，甚至螢幕上的畫面相比，表演給人的感覺更即時，也更有生命力。孩子不
只是跟著文字走，他們同時也在讀表情、動作、音樂、節奏，以及整體氣氛。從這個角度來看
，它就像替情感打開了一扇窗。舞蹈作品可以在一句話也不說的情況下表現悲傷、快樂、緊
張，或者希望；而舞台劇則能幫助孩子走進別人的處境，從另一個角度去看世界。]

components.english: For example, if a child watches a stage play about friendship or family, they may come away thinking more deeply about how other people feel. In the same way, if they watch a dance performance, they may start to notice how movement alone can tell a story. Sometimes children even go home and imitate the characters or the movements, which shows that the performance has really sparked something in them.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子看了一齣關於友情或家庭的舞台劇，他可能之後會更深入地思考別人的感受。同樣地，如果他看了一場舞蹈表演，他也可能會開始留意原來單靠動作也能講故事。有時孩子回家後甚至會模仿角色或舞步，這就顯示那場表演的確在他們心裡點燃了一些東西。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child watches a stage play about friendship or
family, they may come away thinking more deeply about how other people feel.
In the same way, if they watch a dance performance, they may start to notice
how movement alone can tell a story. Sometimes children even go home and
imitate the characters or the movements, which shows that the performance has
really sparked something in them.
[例如，如果一個孩子看了一齣關於友情或家庭的舞台劇，他可能之後會更深入地思考別人
的感受。同樣地，如果他看了一場舞蹈表演，他也可能會開始留意原來單靠動作也能講故
事。有時孩子回家後甚至會模仿角色或舞步，這就顯示那場表演的確在他們心裡點燃了一
些東西。]

components.english: So, in my view, one major influence is that live performances feed children’s imagination and help them become more emotionally sensitive.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，其中一個主要影響就是，現場表演能滋養孩子的想像力，也能幫助他們變得更有情感感受力。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in my view, one major influence is that live performances feed
children’s imagination and help them become more emotionally sensitive.
[所以，在我看來，其中一個主要影響就是，現場表演能滋養孩子的想像力，也能幫助他們
變得更有情感感受力。]

components.english: On top of that, I think these performances can also shape children’s behaviour and personal development by improving their concentration, cultural awareness, and even confidence.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為這類表演能透過提升專注力、文化意識，甚至自信心，去影響孩子的行為和個人成長。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think these performances can also shape children’s
behaviour and personal development by improving their concentration, cultural
awareness, and even confidence.
[除此之外，我也認為這類表演能透過提升專注力、文化意識，甚至自信心，去影響孩子的
行為和個人成長。]

components.english: Watching a live performance usually asks children to slow down, sit through a full experience, and pay attention from beginning to end. That is quite valuable nowadays, because many children are used to fast, bite-sized entertainment. A stage play or dance performance teaches them to stay with a story instead of flicking from one thing to the next. It can also plant the seed of cultural appreciation, because children begin to see that art is not just decoration; it is a way of expressing ideas, traditions, and identity. That said, the effect is not automatic. If the performance is too long, too abstract, or far beyond their age level, it may simply go over their heads.
components.chinese: 觀看現場表演通常會要求孩子慢下來，完整地經歷一場演出，並且從頭到尾保持注意力。這在今天其實很有價值，因為很多孩子已經習慣了節奏很快、片段式的娛樂。舞台劇或舞蹈表演會教他們跟著一個完整故事走，而不是不停從一件事跳到另一件事。它也能種下文化欣賞的種子，因為孩子會開始明白，藝術不只是表面的裝飾；它其實是一種表達思想、傳統和身份認同的方式。不過，這種影響並不是自動發生的。如果表演太長、太抽象，或者遠遠超出他們的年齡理解範圍，那就可能只是讓他們看得一頭霧水。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Watching a live performance usually asks children to slow
down, sit through a full experience, and pay attention from beginning to end.
That is quite valuable nowadays, because many children are used to fast,
bite-sized entertainment. A stage play or dance performance teaches them to
stay with a story instead of flicking from one thing to the next. It can also plant
the seed of cultural appreciation, because children begin to see that art is not
just decoration; it is a way of expressing ideas, traditions, and identity. That
said, the effect is not automatic. If the performance is too long, too abstract, or
far beyond their age level, it may simply go over their heads.
[觀看現場表演通常會要求孩子慢下來，完整地經歷一場演出，並且從頭到尾保持注意力。
這在今天其實很有價值，因為很多孩子已經習慣了節奏很快、片段式的娛樂。舞台劇或舞蹈
表演會教他們跟著一個完整故事走，而不是不停從一件事跳到另一件事。它也能種下文化
欣賞的種子，因為孩子會開始明白，藝術不只是表面的裝飾；它其實是一種表達思想、傳統
和身份認同的方式。不過，這種影響並不是自動發生的。如果表演太長、太抽象，或者遠遠
超出他們的年齡理解範圍，那就可能只是讓他們看得一頭霧水。]

components.english: For instance, if children watch a lively, well-paced play designed for young audiences, they are much more likely to stay engaged, talk about it afterwards, and even become interested in acting or dancing themselves. By contrast, if the performance is too heavy or overly complex, they may switch off halfway through and remember very little.
components.chinese: 例如，如果孩子看的是一齣節奏明快、為年幼觀眾設計的舞台劇，他們通常更容易投入，之後也更可能會討論內容，甚至對演戲或跳舞產生興趣。相比之下，如果表演太沉重，或者過於複雜，他們就可能看到一半已經失去興趣，最後也記不住多少東西。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if children watch a lively, well-paced play designed for
young audiences, they are much more likely to stay engaged, talk about it
afterwards, and even become interested in acting or dancing themselves. By
contrast, if the performance is too heavy or overly complex, they may switch off
halfway through and remember very little.
[例如，如果孩子看的是一齣節奏明快、為年幼觀眾設計的舞台劇，他們通常更容易投入，
之後也更可能會討論內容，甚至對演戲或跳舞產生興趣。相比之下，如果表演太沉重，或者
過於複雜，他們就可能看到一半已經失去興趣，最後也記不住多少東西。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say watching dance performances and stage plays can influence children in rich and lasting ways, because these experiences can stretch the imagination, deepen empathy, and sharpen attention. If the performance speaks their language, even quietly, it can stay with them for a very long time.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說觀看舞蹈表演和舞台劇能以豐富而長遠的方式影響孩子，因為這些經驗可以延展他們的想像力、加深同理心，並且提升專注力。只要那場表演能用孩子聽得懂的方式和他們對話，即使很安靜地發生，也可能在他們心裡停留很久。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say watching dance performances and stage plays
can influence children in rich and lasting ways, because these experiences can
stretch the imagination, deepen empathy, and sharpen attention. If the
performance speaks their language, even quietly, it can stay with them for a very
long time.
[總結來說，我會說觀看舞蹈表演和舞台劇能以豐富而長遠的方式影響孩子，因為這些經驗
可以延展他們的想像力、加深同理心，並且提升專注力。只要那場表演能用孩子聽得懂的方
式和他們對話，即使很安靜地發生，也可能在他們心裡停留很久。]

question.english: Why should people be concerned about the environment?
question.chinese: 為甚麼人們應該關心環境？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why should people be concerned about the environment?
為甚麼人們應該關心環境？

components.english: I think people should be concerned about the environment because it is not some distant issue; it is the foundation of our health, safety, and quality of life.
components.chinese: 我認為，人們應該關心環境，因為這並不是甚麼遙遠的議題；它其實是我們健康、安全，以及生活質素的基礎。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think people should be concerned about the environment because it is
not some distant issue; it is the foundation of our health, safety, and quality of
life.
[我認為，人們應該關心環境，因為這並不是甚麼遙遠的議題；它其實是我們健康、安全，以
及生活質素的基礎。]

components.english: The air we breathe, the water we drink, the food we eat, and even the climate we live in all depend on a healthy environment. If that foundation starts to crack, the effects do not stay in the background for long. They show up in dirty air, extreme weather, rising health problems, and growing pressure on daily life. In other words, harming the environment is a bit like sawing away at the branch we are sitting on.
components.chinese: 我們呼吸的空氣、喝的水、吃的食物，甚至我們所處的氣候，其實都依賴一個健康的環境。如果這個基礎開始出現裂痕，後果就不會長期只停留在背景之中。它很快就會以空氣污染、極端天氣、健康問題增加，以及日常生活壓力上升的形式顯現出來。換句話說，破壞環境其實有點像一邊坐在樹枝上，一邊把那根樹枝鋸斷。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The air we breathe, the water we drink, the food we eat, and
even the climate we live in all depend on a healthy environment. If that
foundation starts to crack, the effects do not stay in the background for long.
They show up in dirty air, extreme weather, rising health problems, and
growing pressure on daily life. In other words, harming the environment is a bit
like sawing away at the branch we are sitting on.
[我們呼吸的空氣、喝的水、吃的食物，甚至我們所處的氣候，其實都依賴一個健康的環境。
如果這個基礎開始出現裂痕，後果就不會長期只停留在背景之中。它很快就會以空氣污染、
極端天氣、健康問題增加，以及日常生活壓力上升的形式顯現出來。換句話說，破壞環境其
實有點像一邊坐在樹枝上，一邊把那根樹枝鋸斷。]

components.english: For example, if rivers are polluted or green spaces disappear, local people may face dirtier water, hotter neighbourhoods, and a lower quality of life. Likewise, when cities suffer from severe air pollution, it is often children, the elderly, and people with health conditions who pay the highest price.
components.chinese: 例如，如果河流受到污染，或者綠化空間消失，當地人就可能要面對更骯髒的水、更炎熱的社區，以及更差的生活質素。同樣地，當城市出現嚴重空氣污染時，往往是小朋友、長者，以及本身有健康問題的人要付出最高代價。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if rivers are polluted or green spaces disappear, local
people may face dirtier water, hotter neighbourhoods, and a lower quality of
life. Likewise, when cities suffer from severe air pollution, it is often children,
the elderly, and people with health conditions who pay the highest price.
[例如，如果河流受到污染，或者綠化空間消失，當地人就可能要面對更骯髒的水、更炎熱
的社區，以及更差的生活質素。同樣地，當城市出現嚴重空氣污染時，往往是小朋友、長者，
以及本身有健康問題的人要付出最高代價。]

components.english: So, people should care about the environment because protecting it is really a way of protecting ourselves.
components.chinese: 所以，人們應該關心環境，因為保護環境，其實就是在保護我們自己。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, people should care about the environment because protecting
it is really a way of protecting ourselves.
[所以，人們應該關心環境，因為保護環境，其實就是在保護我們自己。]

components.english: On top of that, I think people should care because environmental damage does not just affect us now; it also shapes the world we leave behind.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我認為人們之所以應該關心環境，還因為環境破壞不只影響現在，也會塑造我們留給下一代的世界。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think people should care because environmental
damage does not just affect us now; it also shapes the world we leave behind.
[除此之外，我認為人們之所以應該關心環境，還因為環境破壞不只影響現在，也會塑造我
們留給下一代的世界。]

components.english: If people keep treating nature as if it were endless, sooner or later the bill comes due. Future generations may inherit more floods, more heatwaves, fewer natural resources, and a world that is harder to live in. That is why environmental responsibility matters: it is not only about convenience today, but also about fairness tomorrow. We did not create the planet, so we should not behave as though we are free to burn through it.
components.chinese: 如果人們一直把自然當成取之不盡、用之不竭的東西，遲早都要為此付出代價。未來世代可能會繼承更多水災、更多熱浪、更少自然資源，以及一個更難生活的世界。這正是環境責任之所以重要的原因：它不只是關乎今天的便利，也關乎明天的公平。我們並沒有創造這個地球，所以我們不應該表現得好像自己可以毫無節制地把它耗盡一樣。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If people keep treating nature as if it were endless, sooner or
later the bill comes due. Future generations may inherit more floods, more
heatwaves, fewer natural resources, and a world that is harder to live in. That is
why environmental responsibility matters: it is not only about convenience
today, but also about fairness tomorrow. We did not create the planet, so we
should not behave as though we are free to burn through it.
[如果人們一直把自然當成取之不盡、用之不竭的東西，遲早都要為此付出代價。未來世代
可能會繼承更多水災、更多熱浪、更少自然資源，以及一個更難生活的世界。這正是環境責
任之所以重要的原因：它不只是關乎今天的便利，也關乎明天的公平。我們並沒有創造這個
地球，所以我們不應該表現得好像自己可以毫無節制地把它耗盡一樣。]

components.english: For instance, if a community cuts down trees, wastes water, and relies heavily on disposable products year after year, the damage may seem small at first. However, over time, those choices pile up, and the next generation may be left to clean up a mess they did not create.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個社區年復一年地砍伐樹木、浪費用水，並且嚴重依賴即棄產品，起初那些傷害看起來也許不大。不過，隨著時間過去，那些選擇會一點一滴累積起來，而下一代可能就要替一個不是他們造成的爛攤子收拾善後。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a community cuts down trees, wastes water, and
relies heavily on disposable products year after year, the damage may seem small
at first. However, over time, those choices pile up, and the next generation may
be left to clean up a mess they did not create.
[例如，如果一個社區年復一年地砍伐樹木、浪費用水，並且嚴重依賴即棄產品，起初那些
傷害看起來也許不大。不過，隨著時間過去，那些選擇會一點一滴累積起來，而下一代可能
就要替一個不是他們造成的爛攤子收拾善後。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people should be concerned about the environment because our lives depend on it today, and our choices will echo far beyond our own lifetime.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人們應該關心環境，因為我們今天的生活本來就依賴它，而我們所作的選擇，也會在我們的一生之後繼續產生回響。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people should be concerned about the
environment because our lives depend on it today, and our choices will echo far
beyond our own lifetime.
[總結來說，我會說人們應該關心環境，因為我們今天的生活本來就依賴它，而我們所作的
選擇，也會在我們的一生之後繼續產生回響。]

question.english: Is water pollution a problem in your country?
question.chinese: 你認為你國家的水污染是一個問題嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is water pollution a problem in your country?
你認為你國家的水污染是一個問題嗎？

components.english: Yes, I would say water pollution is still a problem in my country, or at least in places like Hong Kong, even if it may not always be visible on the surface.
components.chinese: 是的，我會說水污染在我的地方仍然是一個問題，至少像香港這類地方是如此，雖然它未必總是表面上看得那麼明顯。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I would say water pollution is still a problem in my country, or at
least in places like Hong Kong, even if it may not always be visible on the
surface.
[是的，我會說水污染在我的地方仍然是一個問題，至少像香港這類地方是如此，雖然它未
必總是表面上看得那麼明顯。]

components.english: The main reason is that water can be polluted in all sorts of ways, from plastic waste and litter to industrial discharge, sewage, and polluted runoff after heavy rain. Sometimes the water may not look terrible at first glance, but that does not mean it is clean. In many cases, the problem is hidden beneath the surface. It can harm marine life, affect water quality, and damage the wider environment bit by bit. So, to me, it is not something we can just brush under the carpet.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，水可以透過很多不同方式受到污染，例如塑膠垃圾、廢棄物、工業排放、污水，以及大雨後被沖入河道或海裡的污染物流。很多時候，水面看起來未必很糟，但那並不代表它真的乾淨。很多問題其實是藏在表面之下的。它會一點一滴地傷害海洋生物、影響水質，並破壞整體環境。所以對我來說，這並不是一個可以輕輕帶過、假裝不存在的問題。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that water can be polluted in all sorts of
ways, from plastic waste and litter to industrial discharge, sewage, and polluted
runoff after heavy rain. Sometimes the water may not look terrible at first
glance, but that does not mean it is clean. In many cases, the problem is hidden
beneath the surface. It can harm marine life, affect water quality, and damage
the wider environment bit by bit. So, to me, it is not something we can just
brush under the carpet.
[主要原因是，水可以透過很多不同方式受到污染，例如塑膠垃圾、廢棄物、工業排放、污水
，以及大雨後被沖入河道或海裡的污染物流。很多時候，水面看起來未必很糟，但那並不代
表它真的乾淨。很多問題其實是藏在表面之下的。它會一點一滴地傷害海洋生物、影響水質
，並破壞整體環境。所以對我來說，這並不是一個可以輕輕帶過、假裝不存在的問題。]

components.english: For example, if beaches, rivers, or coastal areas are filled with rubbish or contaminated water, that can affect fish, damage ecosystems, and even make people think twice about swimming or spending time there. In a city like Hong Kong, where the harbour and coastline are such a visible part of daily life, pollution really hits close to home.
components.chinese: 例如，如果沙灘、河流，或者沿海地區充滿垃圾或受污染的水體，那不但會影響魚類，也會破壞生態系統，甚至令人對游泳或到那裡消遣都卻步。在像香港這樣的城市，維港和海岸線本來就是日常生活中非常鮮明的一部分，所以污染問題其實是相當貼身、相當真實的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if beaches, rivers, or coastal areas are filled with
rubbish or contaminated water, that can affect fish, damage ecosystems, and
even make people think twice about swimming or spending time there. In a city
like Hong Kong, where the harbour and coastline are such a visible part of daily
life, pollution really hits close to home.
[例如，如果沙灘、河流，或者沿海地區充滿垃圾或受污染的水體，那不但會影響魚類，也會
破壞生態系統，甚至令人對游泳或到那裡消遣都卻步。在像香港這樣的城市，維港和海岸線
本來就是日常生活中非常鮮明的一部分，所以污染問題其實是相當貼身、相當真實的。]

components.english: So yes, I do think water pollution is a genuine problem, because once water quality goes downhill, the effects can spread much further than people expect.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為水污染是一個真實存在的問題，因為一旦水質走下坡，它帶來的影響往往會比人們想像中更廣、更深。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think water pollution is a genuine problem, because
once water quality goes downhill, the effects can spread much further than
people expect.
[所以，是的，我的確認為水污染是一個真實存在的問題，因為一旦水質走下坡，它帶來的影
響往往會比人們想像中更廣、更深。]

components.english: That said, I would also say the situation is not hopeless, because there is much more public awareness now than there used to be.
components.chinese: 不過，我也會說情況並不是毫無希望，因為現在大眾對這個問題的關注，已經比以前高得多。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would also say the situation is not hopeless, because there
is much more public awareness now than there used to be.
[不過，我也會說情況並不是毫無希望，因為現在大眾對這個問題的關注，已經比以前高得

components.english: Governments, schools, and environmental groups are putting more emphasis on cleaning up beaches, reducing waste, and protecting waterways. So, while pollution is still a real issue, it is not as if people are simply turning a blind eye to it. In fact, the more seriously society takes it, the better the chances of stopping the problem from snowballing.
components.chinese: 政府、學校，以及環保團體都愈來愈重視清潔海灘、減少廢物，以及保護水道。所以，雖然污染依然是一個真實問題，但也不是說大家完全視而不見。事實上，社會愈認真看待它，就愈有機會防止問題愈滾愈大。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Governments, schools, and environmental groups are putting
more emphasis on cleaning up beaches, reducing waste, and protecting
waterways. So, while pollution is still a real issue, it is not as if people are simply
turning a blind eye to it. In fact, the more seriously society takes it, the better
the chances of stopping the problem from snowballing.
[政府、學校，以及環保團體都愈來愈重視清潔海灘、減少廢物，以及保護水道。所以，雖然
污染依然是一個真實問題，但也不是說大家完全視而不見。事實上，社會愈認真看待它，就
愈有機會防止問題愈滾愈大。]

components.english: For instance, when schools organise beach clean-ups or campaigns to reduce plastic use, children grow up with a stronger sense of environmental responsibility. Small actions like that may seem modest, but they can create a ripple effect over time.
components.chinese: 例如，當學校舉辦淨灘活動，或者推行減少使用塑膠的宣傳時，孩子就會在成長過程中建立更強的環保意識。這些小行動看起來也許不算驚天動地，但長遠來說，它們是可以產生連鎖效應的。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, when schools organise beach clean-ups or campaigns
to reduce plastic use, children grow up with a stronger sense of environmental
responsibility. Small actions like that may seem modest, but they can create a
ripple effect over time.
[例如，當學校舉辦淨灘活動，或者推行減少使用塑膠的宣傳時，孩子就會在成長過程中建
立更強的環保意識。這些小行動看起來也許不算驚天動地，但長遠來說，它們是可以產生連
鎖效應的。]

components.english: Overall, I would say water pollution is still a problem in my country, but it is one that people are becoming more aware of and more willing to tackle. It is not a lost cause, but it is definitely not something we can afford to take lightly.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說水污染在我的地方仍然是一個問題，不過人們對它的意識正在提高，也愈來愈願意正面處理。這不是一個已經失控到無法挽回的問題，但它絕對是一個不能掉以輕心的問題。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say water pollution is still a problem in my
country, but it is one that people are becoming more aware of and more willing
to tackle. It is not a lost cause, but it is definitely not something we can afford to
take lightly.
[總結來說，我會說水污染在我的地方仍然是一個問題，不過人們對它的意識正在提高，也
愈來愈願意正面處理。這不是一個已經失控到無法挽回的問題，但它絕對是一個不能掉以
輕心的問題。]

question.english: What are some of the main environmental problems in your country?
question.chinese: 你所在的地方有哪些主要環境問題？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are some of the main environmental problems in your country?
你所在的地方有哪些主要環境問題？

components.english: Off the top of my head, I’d say the main environmental problems in Hong Kong are air pollution, too much waste, and the growing impact of climate change.
components.chinese: 如果要我馬上說，我會覺得香港主要的環境問題是空氣污染、廢物過多，以及氣候變化帶來愈來愈明顯的影響。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Off the top of my head, I’d say the main environmental problems in
Hong Kong are air pollution, too much waste, and the growing impact of
climate change.
[如果要我馬上說，我會覺得香港主要的環境問題是空氣污染、廢物過多，以及氣候變化帶
來愈來愈明顯的影響。]

components.english: The first two are probably the most obvious in daily life. Hong Kong is such a dense city that when the air is bad or rubbish keeps building up, people feel it almost straight away. The government still describes Hong Kong as facing both street-level air pollution and regional smog, and food waste remains the biggest type of waste sent to landfills here.
components.chinese: 前面兩個問題，大概是日常生活中最容易感受到的。香港是一個很密集的城市，所以一旦空氣變差，或者垃圾愈積愈多，人們幾乎很快就會感受到。政府目前仍指出，香港同時面對路邊空氣污染和區域性煙霧，而廚餘仍然是送往堆填區的最大類別廢物。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The first two are probably the most obvious in daily life. Hong
Kong is such a dense city that when the air is bad or rubbish keeps building up,
people feel it almost straight away. The government still describes Hong Kong as
facing both street-level air pollution and regional smog, and food waste remains
the biggest type of waste sent to landfills here.
[前面兩個問題，大概是日常生活中最容易感受到的。香港是一個很密集的城市，所以一旦
空氣變差，或者垃圾愈積愈多，人們幾乎很快就會感受到。政府目前仍指出，香港同時面對
路邊空氣污染和區域性煙霧，而廚餘仍然是送往堆填區的最大類別廢物。]

components.english: For example, on a day with heavy traffic, the air can feel a bit stale and oppressive, especially on busy roads. And with how common takeaway food is in Hong Kong, it is not hard to imagine how packaging, leftovers, and all the little bits of daily waste can pile up faster than people realise.
components.chinese: 例如，在交通繁忙的日子裡，空氣有時真的會令人覺得有點焗、有點悶，尤其是在車多的路段。再加上香港外賣文化很普遍，所以包裝、剩食，以及那些日常零零碎碎的垃圾，其實比人想像中更快就會堆積起來。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, on a day with heavy traffic, the air can feel a bit stale
and oppressive, especially on busy roads. And with how common takeaway food
is in Hong Kong, it is not hard to imagine how packaging, leftovers, and all the
little bits of daily waste can pile up faster than people realise.
[例如，在交通繁忙的日子裡，空氣有時真的會令人覺得有點焗、有點悶，尤其是在車多的
路段。再加上香港外賣文化很普遍，所以包裝、剩食，以及那些日常零零碎碎的垃圾，其實
比人想像中更快就會堆積起來。]

components.english: So yes, if I had to name the most noticeable everyday problems, air pollution and waste would be right up there.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要我說最明顯、最貼近日常生活的環境問題，我會首先想到空氣污染和廢物問題。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, if I had to name the most noticeable everyday problems,
