air pollution and waste would be right up there.
[所以，如果要我說最明顯、最貼近日常生活的環境問題，我會首先想到空氣污染和廢物問

components.english: Another big issue, in my view, is climate change, because it no longer feels like some distant problem in the future.
components.chinese: 在我看來，另一個很大的問題就是氣候變化，因為它已經不再像是未來很遙遠的事情。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another big issue, in my view, is climate change, because it no longer
feels like some distant problem in the future.
[在我看來，另一個很大的問題就是氣候變化，因為它已經不再像是未來很遙遠的事情。]

components.english: Hong Kong has been getting warmer, with more hot nights and more frequent heavy rain than before. That is why climate change feels much more real now. It is not just something people read about in the news; it is something they can feel on their skin and see outside their window.
components.chinese: 香港一直在變暖，熱夜比以前更多，暴雨也比以前更頻繁。正因如此，氣候變化現在令人感覺真實得多。它不再只是新聞裡看到的東西，而是人們可以在皮膚上感受到、在窗外看得到的事情。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Hong Kong has been getting warmer, with more hot nights
and more frequent heavy rain than before. That is why climate change feels
much more real now. It is not just something people read about in the news; it is
something they can feel on their skin and see outside their window.

到的事情。]

components.english: For instance, after a spell of extreme heat or a really heavy downpour, people can feel very clearly that environmental problems are not abstract at all. They affect how comfortable the city feels, how easily people get around, and sometimes even how safe daily life feels.
components.chinese: 例如，在一段極端炎熱天氣，或者一場很大的暴雨之後，人們就會很清楚地感受到，環境問題一點也不抽象。它會影響整座城市的舒適感、影響人們出行是否順利，有時甚至連日常生活的安全感也會受到影響。

components.english: Overall, I’d say Hong Kong’s main environmental problems are the ones that quietly creep into everyday life: dirty air, too much waste, and a hotter, harsher climate. Of course, people are more aware of these issues now, but they are still hard to ignore.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港主要的環境問題，正正就是那些會慢慢滲進日常生活的問題：不夠乾淨的空氣、過多的廢物，以及愈來愈炎熱、愈來愈嚴峻的氣候。當然，現在大家對這些問題的意識更高了，但它們仍然很難被忽視。

but they are still hard to ignore.

乾淨的空氣、過多的廢物，以及愈來愈炎熱、愈來愈嚴峻的氣候。當然，現在大家對這些問
題的意識更高了，但它們仍然很難被忽視。]

question.english: What are some of the causes of water pollution?
question.chinese: 造成水污染的一些原因是甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are some of the causes of water pollution?
造成水污染的一些原因是甚麼？

components.english: I think some of the biggest causes of water pollution are industrial waste and agricultural runoff.
components.chinese: 我認為，造成水污染其中一些最大的原因，就是工業廢物和農業徑流。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think some of the biggest causes of water pollution are industrial
waste and agricultural runoff.
[我認為，造成水污染其中一些最大的原因，就是工業廢物和農業徑流。]

components.english: Water pollution rarely appears out of thin air. In many cases, it is the result of human activity. Factories may release chemicals, dirty water, or toxic waste into rivers and lakes, while farms often use fertilisers and pesticides that are washed into nearby water sources when it rains. Once those substances get into the system, they can spread like ink in water and damage both wildlife and water quality.
components.chinese: 水污染很少是無緣無故出現的。在很多情況下，它其實是人類活動造成的結果。工廠可能會把化學物質、污水，或者有毒廢料排進河流和湖泊，而農場則常常使用肥料和農藥，這些東西在下雨時又會被沖進附近水源。一旦這些物質進入整個水系，它們就可能像墨水滴進水裡一樣擴散開來，破壞野生生物和水質。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Water pollution rarely appears out of thin air. In many cases, it
is the result of human activity. Factories may release chemicals, dirty water, or
toxic waste into rivers and lakes, while farms often use fertilisers and pesticides
that are washed into nearby water sources when it rains. Once those substances
get into the system, they can spread like ink in water and damage both wildlife
and water quality.
[水污染很少是無緣無故出現的。在很多情況下，它其實是人類活動造成的結果。工廠可能
會把化學物質、污水，或者有毒廢料排進河流和湖泊，而農場則常常使用肥料和農藥，這些
東西在下雨時又會被沖進附近水源。一旦這些物質進入整個水系，它們就可能像墨水滴進
水裡一樣擴散開來，破壞野生生物和水質。]

components.english: For example, if a factory near a river fails to treat its waste properly, the river may become unsafe for fish and even for nearby communities. In the same way, if farmland is heavily sprayed with chemicals, rainwater can carry those pollutants straight into streams or reservoirs.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一間靠近河流的工廠沒有妥善處理廢水，那條河就可能變得不適合魚類生存，甚至對附近居民也構成風險。同樣地，如果農地大量噴灑化學物質，雨水就可能把那些污染物直接帶進溪流或水塘。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a factory near a river fails to treat its waste properly,
the river may become unsafe for fish and even for nearby communities. In the
same way, if farmland is heavily sprayed with chemicals, rainwater can carry
those pollutants straight into streams or reservoirs.
[例如，如果一間靠近河流的工廠沒有妥善處理廢水，那條河就可能變得不適合魚類生存，
甚至對附近居民也構成風險。同樣地，如果農地大量噴灑化學物質，雨水就可能把那些污染
物直接帶進溪流或水塘。]

components.english: So, one major source of water pollution is the way industries and farms release harmful substances into the environment.
components.chinese: 所以，水污染其中一個主要來源，就是工業和農業把有害物質排進環境之中的方式。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major source of water pollution is the way industries and
farms release harmful substances into the environment.
[所以，水污染其中一個主要來源，就是工業和農業把有害物質排進環境之中的方式。]

components.english: Another important cause is everyday human waste, especially sewage, plastic rubbish, and poor waste disposal.
components.chinese: 另一個重要原因，就是日常人類廢物，特別是污水、塑膠垃圾，以及不良的廢物處理方式。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another important cause is everyday human waste, especially sewage,
plastic rubbish, and poor waste disposal.
[另一個重要原因，就是日常人類廢物，特別是污水、塑膠垃圾，以及不良的廢物處理方

components.english: Cities and towns produce huge amounts of waste, and if that waste is not managed properly, waterways often end up paying the price. Untreated sewage can contaminate rivers, while plastic bottles, bags, and packaging may find their way into drains, canals, and eventually the sea. In other words, what begins as carelessness on land can easily become pollution in the water. A river does not become dirty overnight; it is often polluted bit by bit, drop by drop.
components.chinese: 城市和鄉鎮每天都會產生大量廢物，而如果這些廢物沒有被妥善處理，最後付出代價的往往就是水道。未經處理的污水會污染河流，而塑膠瓶、膠袋和包裝物則可能流入排水道、渠道，最後進入大海。換句話說，陸地上的疏忽大意，很容易就會變成水中的污染。河流不是一夜之間變髒的；很多時候，它是被一點一滴、慢慢污染出來的。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Cities and towns produce huge amounts of waste, and if that
waste is not managed properly, waterways often end up paying the price.
Untreated sewage can contaminate rivers, while plastic bottles, bags, and
packaging may find their way into drains, canals, and eventually the sea. In
other words, what begins as carelessness on land can easily become pollution in
the water. A river does not become dirty overnight; it is often polluted bit by
bit, drop by drop.
[城市和鄉鎮每天都會產生大量廢物，而如果這些廢物沒有被妥善處理，最後付出代價的往
往就是水道。未經處理的污水會污染河流，而塑膠瓶、膠袋和包裝物則可能流入排水道、渠
道，最後進入大海。換句話說，陸地上的疏忽大意，很容易就會變成水中的污染。河流不是
一夜之間變髒的；很多時候，它是被一點一滴、慢慢污染出來的。]

components.english: For instance, when people throw rubbish on the street, rain can wash it into drains, and those drains often lead directly to rivers or the sea. Likewise, if a city does not have an effective sewage treatment system, dirty water can flow straight into natural water bodies.
components.chinese: 例如，當人們把垃圾隨手丟在街上，雨水就可能把它沖進排水系統，而那些排水系統最後往往會通往河流或大海。同樣地，如果一座城市沒有有效的污水處理系統，污水就可能直接流進天然水域。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, when people throw rubbish on the street, rain can
wash it into drains, and those drains often lead directly to rivers or the sea.
Likewise, if a city does not have an effective sewage treatment system, dirty
water can flow straight into natural water bodies.
[例如，當人們把垃圾隨手丟在街上，雨水就可能把它沖進排水系統，而那些排水系統最後
往往會通往河流或大海。同樣地，如果一座城市沒有有效的污水處理系統，污水就可能直接
流進天然水域。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say water pollution is mainly caused by human behaviour, whether through industry, farming, or everyday waste. That said, the problem is man-made, which means it can also be reduced if people and governments take it seriously.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說水污染主要是由人類行為造成的，不論是工業、農業，還是日常廢物處理。不過，正因為這是人為造成的問題，所以只要政府和市民真正重視，它其實也是可以被減輕的。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say water pollution is mainly caused by human
behaviour, whether through industry, farming, or everyday waste. That said, the
problem is man-made, which means it can also be reduced if people and
governments take it seriously.
[總結來說，我會說水污染主要是由人類行為造成的，不論是工業、農業，還是日常廢物處
理。不過，正因為這是人為造成的問題，所以只要政府和市民真正重視，它其實也是可以被
減輕的。]

question.english: Do you think problems with the cleanliness of water will improve in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為未來水質清潔方面的問題會改善嗎？

source_lines.text: Do you think problems with the cleanliness of water will improve in the future?
你認為未來水質清潔方面的問題會改善嗎？

components.english: I think they probably will improve to some extent, but not everywhere and certainly not overnight.
components.chinese: 我認為，這些問題在某個程度上大概會有所改善，不過未必每個地方都一樣，而且肯定不會一夜之間就解決。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think they probably will improve to some extent, but not everywhere
and certainly not overnight.
[我認為，這些問題在某個程度上大概會有所改善，不過未必每個地方都一樣，而且肯定不
會一夜之間就解決。]

components.english: The main reason is that people are becoming more aware that clean water is not a luxury; it is a basic necessity. As a result, governments, scientists, and environmental groups are likely to put more effort into water treatment, pollution control, and better infrastructure. Technology is also moving in the right direction, so there may be more efficient ways to detect contamination and clean water before it reaches the public. In that sense, I do think the tide could turn, at least gradually.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，人們越來越明白，乾淨的水並不是奢侈品，而是最基本的生活需要。因此，政府、科學家，以及環保團體都很可能會在淨水技術、污染控制，以及基建改善方面投放更多心力。科技本身也正朝著較好的方向發展，所以未來可能會有更有效的方法去偵測污染，並在水到達市民手上之前先把它處理好。從這個角度來看，我的確認為情況有可能慢慢好轉。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that people are becoming more aware that
clean water is not a luxury; it is a basic necessity. As a result, governments,
scientists, and environmental groups are likely to put more effort into water
treatment, pollution control, and better infrastructure. Technology is also
moving in the right direction, so there may be more efficient ways to detect
contamination and clean water before it reaches the public. In that sense, I do
think the tide could turn, at least gradually.
[主要原因是，人們越來越明白，乾淨的水並不是奢侈品，而是最基本的生活需要。因此，政
府、科學家，以及環保團體都很可能會在淨水技術、污染控制，以及基建改善方面投放更多
心力。科技本身也正朝著較好的方向發展，所以未來可能會有更有效的方法去偵測污染，並
在水到達市民手上之前先把它處理好。從這個角度來看，我的確認為情況有可能慢慢好

components.english: For example, if cities invest in better sewage treatment systems, stricter rules for factories, and smarter ways to monitor rivers and reservoirs, water quality is quite likely to improve over time. Even small changes, such as reducing plastic waste or stopping illegal dumping, can help clear the water little by little.
components.chinese: 例如，如果城市願意投放資源去改善污水處理系統、對工廠實施更嚴格的規管，並用更智能的方法監察河流和水塘，水質其實很有可能隨著時間慢慢改善。即使只是一些小改變，例如減少塑膠垃圾，或者制止非法傾倒廢物，也能一點一滴地令水變乾淨。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if cities invest in better sewage treatment systems,
stricter rules for factories, and smarter ways to monitor rivers and reservoirs,
water quality is quite likely to improve over time. Even small changes, such as
reducing plastic waste or stopping illegal dumping, can help clear the water
little by little.
[例如，如果城市願意投放資源去改善污水處理系統、對工廠實施更嚴格的規管，並用更智
能的方法監察河流和水塘，水質其實很有可能隨著時間慢慢改善。即使只是一些小改變，例
如減少塑膠垃圾，或者制止非法傾倒廢物，也能一點一滴地令水變乾淨。]

components.english: So yes, I do think there is reason to be hopeful, because with enough effort, cleaner water is definitely not out of reach.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我的確認為這方面是有理由樂觀的，因為只要投入足夠努力，較潔淨的水源並不是遙不可及。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I do think there is reason to be hopeful, because with
enough effort, cleaner water is definitely not out of reach.
[所以，是的，我的確認為這方面是有理由樂觀的，因為只要投入足夠努力，較潔淨的水源
並不是遙不可及。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the problem will simply disappear, because water pollution is tied to some very deep and stubborn issues.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為這個問題會自動消失，因為水污染其實和一些很深層、也很難處理的問題連在一起。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the problem will simply disappear, because
water pollution is tied to some very deep and stubborn issues.
[不過，我也不認為這個問題會自動消失，因為水污染其實和一些很深層、也很難處理的問
題連在一起。]

components.english: In many places, rapid urban growth, industrial waste, weak regulation, and even climate change all muddy the waters, both literally and figuratively. Some governments may have the technology but not the political will, while others may want to act but lack the money or infrastructure. On top of that, once a river, lake, or coastline is badly polluted, cleaning it up can be a long haul rather than a quick fix. So, although progress is possible, it may come in fits and starts rather than in one smooth line.
components.chinese: 在很多地方，城市快速發展、工業廢料、監管不足，甚至氣候變化，都會令問題變得更複雜，某程度上可說是真的把水「攪濁」了。有些政府也許有技術，卻未必有足夠的政治意志；而另一些地方則可能想做，卻缺乏資金或基建。再加上，一旦一條河流、湖泊，或者海岸線已經受到嚴重污染，要把它清理好，往往是一場長仗，而不是快速見效的解決方法。所以，雖然進步是可能的，但這種進步未必會很順，反而可能是一段一段慢慢推進。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In many places, rapid urban growth, industrial waste, weak
regulation, and even climate change all muddy the waters, both literally and
figuratively. Some governments may have the technology but not the political
will, while others may want to act but lack the money or infrastructure. On top
of that, once a river, lake, or coastline is badly polluted, cleaning it up can be a
long haul rather than a quick fix. So, although progress is possible, it may come
in fits and starts rather than in one smooth line.
[在很多地方，城市快速發展、工業廢料、監管不足，甚至氣候變化，都會令問題變得更複雜
，某程度上可說是真的把水「攪濁」了。有些政府也許有技術，卻未必有足夠的政治意志；而
另一些地方則可能想做，卻缺乏資金或基建。再加上，一旦一條河流、湖泊，或者海岸線已
經受到嚴重污染，要把它清理好，往往是一場長仗，而不是快速見效的解決方法。所以，雖
然進步是可能的，但這種進步未必會很順，反而可能是一段一段慢慢推進。]

components.english: For instance, a wealthy city may be able to upgrade its water systems fairly quickly, whereas a poorer area may still struggle with polluted rivers, unsafe drinking water, or poor sanitation for years. In that case, the future may look brighter for some communities, but much murkier for others.
components.chinese: 例如，一個富裕城市也許能相對快速地升級它的供水和淨水系統；相反，較貧困的地區卻可能仍然要多年面對河流污染、飲用水不安全，或者衛生設施不足的問題。在這種情況下，未來對某些社區來說也許會比較光明，但對另一些地方來說，前景仍然可能相當模糊。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a wealthy city may be able to upgrade its water
systems fairly quickly, whereas a poorer area may still struggle with polluted
rivers, unsafe drinking water, or poor sanitation for years. In that case, the
future may look brighter for some communities, but much murkier for others.
[例如，一個富裕城市也許能相對快速地升級它的供水和淨水系統；相反，較貧困的地區卻
可能仍然要多年面對河流污染、飲用水不安全，或者衛生設施不足的問題。在這種情況下，
未來對某些社區來說也許會比較光明，但對另一些地方來說，前景仍然可能相當模糊。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say water cleanliness problems will probably improve in the future, but only if people treat them as urgent rather than optional. Progress is possible, but clean water will not fall into our lap; it has to be protected, invested in, and taken seriously.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，未來水質清潔方面的問題大概是有機會改善的，但前提是人們必須把它當成迫切問題，而不是可做可不做的選項。進步是可能的，不過乾淨的水並不會自己掉到我們手上；它必須被保護、被投放資源，也必須被認真看待。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say water cleanliness problems will probably
improve in the future, but only if people treat them as urgent rather than
optional. Progress is possible, but clean water will not fall into our lap; it has to
be protected, invested in, and taken seriously.
[總結來說，我會說，未來水質清潔方面的問題大概是有機會改善的，但前提是人們必須把
它當成迫切問題，而不是可做可不做的選項。進步是可能的，不過乾淨的水並不會自己掉到
我們手上；它必須被保護、被投放資源，也必須被認真看待。]

question.english: How can people protect the environment?
question.chinese: 人們可以怎樣保護環境？

source_lines.text: Topic: How can people protect the environment?
[人們可以怎樣保護環境？]

components.english: I think people can protect the environment mainly by changing their everyday habits, because small actions, repeated day after day, can add up to a big difference.
components.chinese: 我認為，人們保護環境最主要的方法，就是改變日常生活習慣，因為一些細小的行動，只要日復一日地持續下去，最後就能累積成很大的改變。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think people can protect the environment mainly by changing their
everyday habits, because small actions, repeated day after day, can add up to a
big difference.
[我認為，人們保護環境最主要的方法，就是改變日常生活習慣，因為一些細小的行動，只
要日復一日地持續下去，最後就能累積成很大的改變。]

components.english: A lot of environmental damage comes not from one dramatic act, but from ordinary waste: using too much electricity, throwing away too much plastic, wasting water, or relying too heavily on cars. So if people learn to live a little more lightly, the pressure on the environment can be reduced quite a lot. In that sense, protecting the planet is not always about grand gestures; very often, it begins at home, in the kitchen, at the socket, or on the daily commute.
components.chinese: 很多環境破壞其實不是來自某一個特別戲劇性的行為，而是來自日常的浪費，例如用太多電、丟掉太多塑膠、浪費食水，或者過度依賴汽車。所以，如果人們學會讓自己的生活方式「輕一點」，環境所承受的壓力其實就可以減少很多。從這個角度來說，保護地球未必總是甚麼轟轟烈烈的大舉動；很多時候，它其實是從家裡、廚房、插座旁邊，或者每天上班上學的路上開始的。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: A lot of environmental damage comes not from one dramatic
act, but from ordinary waste: using too much electricity, throwing away too
much plastic, wasting water, or relying too heavily on cars. So if people learn to
live a little more lightly, the pressure on the environment can be reduced quite a
lot. In that sense, protecting the planet is not always about grand gestures; very
often, it begins at home, in the kitchen, at the socket, or on the daily commute.
[很多環境破壞其實不是來自某一個特別戲劇性的行為，而是來自日常的浪費，例如用太多
電、丟掉太多塑膠、浪費食水，或者過度依賴汽車。所以，如果人們學會讓自己的生活方式
「輕一點」，環境所承受的壓力其實就可以減少很多。從這個角度來說，保護地球未必總是甚
麼轟轟烈烈的大舉動；很多時候，它其實是從家裡、廚房、插座旁邊，或者每天上班上學的
路上開始的。]

components.english: For example, people can bring their own bags and bottles, turn off lights and air conditioning when they are not needed, take public transport, and avoid buying things with excessive packaging. None of those actions sounds world-changing by itself, but when millions of people do them, the effect can snowball.
components.chinese: 例如，人們可以自備購物袋和水樽、在不需要時關掉燈和冷氣、乘搭公共交通，以及避免購買包裝過多的產品。這些行動單獨看起來都不像能改變世界的大事，但當數以百萬計的人都這樣做時，效果就會像雪球一樣愈滾愈大。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, people can bring their own bags and bottles, turn off
lights and air conditioning when they are not needed, take public transport,
and avoid buying things with excessive packaging. None of those actions sounds
world-changing by itself, but when millions of people do them, the effect can
snowball.
[例如，人們可以自備購物袋和水樽、在不需要時關掉燈和冷氣、乘搭公共交通，以及避免
購買包裝過多的產品。這些行動單獨看起來都不像能改變世界的大事，但當數以百萬計的
人都這樣做時，效果就會像雪球一樣愈滾愈大。]

components.english: So, from my point of view, one of the most practical ways to protect the environment is to build greener habits into everyday life rather than treat sustainability as something distant or abstract.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，保護環境其中一個最實際的方法，就是把更環保的習慣融入日常生活，而不是把可持續發展看成遙遠又抽象的概念。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, from my point of view, one of the most practical ways to
protect the environment is to build greener habits into everyday life rather than
treat sustainability as something distant or abstract.
[所以，在我看來，保護環境其中一個最實際的方法，就是把更環保的習慣融入日常生活，
而不是把可持續發展看成遙遠又抽象的概念。]

components.english: On top of that, people can protect the environment by making wiser choices as consumers and citizens, not just as individuals at home.
components.chinese: 除此之外，人們也可以透過作為消費者和公民時作出更明智的選擇，來保護環境，而不只是停留在家中的個人層面。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, people can protect the environment by making wiser
choices as consumers and citizens, not just as individuals at home.
[除此之外，人們也可以透過作為消費者和公民時作出更明智的選擇，來保護環境，而不只
是停留在家中的個人層面。]

components.english: The reason is that environmental problems are bigger than any one person, so personal responsibility matters, but collective pressure matters too. People can support businesses that take sustainability seriously, join recycling or clean-up activities, and back policies that encourage cleaner transport and greener cities. In other words, if everyday habits are the first step, shared action is the next. One person can plant a seed, but a whole community can change the landscape.
components.chinese: 原因是，環境問題本身比任何一個人都大，所以個人責任固然重要，但集體力量同樣重要。人們可以支持真正重視可持續發展的企業、參與回收或清潔活動，並支持鼓勵更潔淨交通和更環保城市的政策。換句話說，如果日常習慣是第一步，那麼共同參與就是下一步。一個人可以種下一顆種子，但整個社區卻可以改變整片風景。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The reason is that environmental problems are bigger than
any one person, so personal responsibility matters, but collective pressure
matters too. People can support businesses that take sustainability seriously, join
recycling or clean-up activities, and back policies that encourage cleaner
transport and greener cities. In other words, if everyday habits are the first step,
shared action is the next. One person can plant a seed, but a whole community
can change the landscape.
[原因是，環境問題本身比任何一個人都大，所以個人責任固然重要，但集體力量同樣重
要。人們可以支持真正重視可持續發展的企業、參與回收或清潔活動，並支持鼓勵更潔淨交
通和更環保城市的政策。換句話說，如果日常習慣是第一步，那麼共同參與就是下一步。一
個人可以種下一顆種子，但整個社區卻可以改變整片風景。]

components.english: For instance, if people choose products that last longer instead of buying cheap items again and again, they reduce waste at the source. In the same way, if local residents support tree-planting projects or campaigns for cleaner public transport, they help create an environment that is easier for everyone to live in and easier for the planet to breathe in.
components.chinese: 例如，如果人們選擇較耐用的產品，而不是不斷重複購買廉價又短命的東西，他們其實就是從源頭減少浪費。同樣地，如果當地居民支持植樹計劃，或者支持更潔淨公共交通的倡議，他們就是在幫助建立一個對所有人都更宜居、也讓地球更能「呼吸」的環境。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if people choose products that last longer instead of
buying cheap items again and again, they reduce waste at the source. In the same
way, if local residents support tree-planting projects or campaigns for cleaner
public transport, they help create an environment that is easier for everyone to
live in and easier for the planet to breathe in.
[例如，如果人們選擇較耐用的產品，而不是不斷重複購買廉價又短命的東西，他們其實就
是從源頭減少浪費。同樣地，如果當地居民支持植樹計劃，或者支持更潔淨公共交通的倡議
，他們就是在幫助建立一個對所有人都更宜居、也讓地球更能「呼吸」的環境。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people can protect the environment by combining personal discipline with collective effort. In other words，the earth is not saved by one perfect person, but by many ordinary people making better choices, bit by bit and day by day.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人們可以透過把個人自律和集體努力結合起來，去保護環境。地球並不是靠某一個完美的人拯救的，而是靠很多普通人一點一滴、一天一天地作出更好的選擇。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people can protect the environment by
combining personal discipline with collective effort. In other words，the earth is
not saved by one perfect person, but by many ordinary people making better
choices, bit by bit and day by day.
[總結來說，我會說人們可以透過把個人自律和集體努力結合起來，去保護環境。地球並不
是靠某一個完美的人拯救的，而是靠很多普通人一點一滴、一天一天地作出更好的選擇。]

question.english: Do you think money should be spent on protecting animals?
question.chinese: 你認為應該花錢保護動物嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think money should be spent on protecting animals?
你認為應該花錢保護動物嗎？

components.english: Yes, I definitely think money should be spent on protecting animals, especially endangered species and the habitats they depend on.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為應該花錢保護動物，尤其是瀕危物種，以及牠們所依賴的棲息地。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I definitely think money should be spent on protecting animals,
especially endangered species and the habitats they depend on.
[是的，我的確認為應該花錢保護動物，尤其是瀕危物種，以及牠們所依賴的棲息地。]

components.english: The main reason is that animals are not just there to make the world look nicer. They are part of the balance of nature, and once that balance is broken, the damage can spread further than people expect. In a way, nature is a bit like a woven net: if you keep cutting threads, sooner or later the whole thing starts to give way. So, protecting animals is not only about kindness; it is also about protecting ecosystems that support human life as well.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，動物的存在不只是為了讓世界看起來更美。牠們其實是大自然平衡的一部分，而一旦這種平衡被打破，帶來的損害往往會比人們想像中更廣。某程度上，大自然有點像一張編織起來的網：如果你不停把其中的線剪斷，遲早整張網都會開始鬆掉、甚至崩塌。所以，保護動物不只是出於善意；同時也是在保護那些支撐人類生活的生態系統。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that animals are not just there to make the
world look nicer. They are part of the balance of nature, and once that balance is
broken, the damage can spread further than people expect. In a way, nature is a
bit like a woven net: if you keep cutting threads, sooner or later the whole thing
starts to give way. So, protecting animals is not only about kindness; it is also
about protecting ecosystems that support human life as well.
[主要原因是，動物的存在不只是為了讓世界看起來更美。牠們其實是大自然平衡的一部分
，而一旦這種平衡被打破，帶來的損害往往會比人們想像中更廣。某程度上，大自然有點像
一張編織起來的網：如果你不停把其中的線剪斷，遲早整張網都會開始鬆掉、甚至崩塌。所
以，保護動物不只是出於善意；同時也是在保護那些支撐人類生活的生態系統。]

components.english: For example, if certain animals disappear from an ecosystem, it can affect the food chain, plant life, and even farming in the long run. Bees are a simple example. They may look small, but they play a huge role in pollination. If we lose them, the effects would ripple far beyond the natural world.
components.chinese: 例如，如果某些動物從一個生態系統中消失，就可能影響食物鏈、植物生長，甚至長遠來說也會影響農業。蜜蜂就是一個很簡單的例子。牠們看起來很小，但在授粉方面扮演著非常重要的角色。如果失去牠們，影響就不只停留在自然界裡，而是會一圈一圈擴散出去。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if certain animals disappear from an ecosystem, it can
affect the food chain, plant life, and even farming in the long run. Bees are a
simple example. They may look small, but they play a huge role in pollination. If
we lose them, the effects would ripple far beyond the natural world.
[例如，如果某些動物從一個生態系統中消失，就可能影響食物鏈、植物生長，甚至長遠來
說也會影響農業。蜜蜂就是一個很簡單的例子。牠們看起來很小，但在授粉方面扮演著非常
重要的角色。如果失去牠們，影響就不只停留在自然界裡，而是會一圈一圈擴散出去。]

components.english: So yes, I would say spending money on animal protection is worthwhile, because it helps preserve both biodiversity and the wider natural system.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說，把資源投放在保護動物上是值得的，因為這能保護生物多樣性，也能維持更大的自然系統。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say spending money on animal protection is
worthwhile, because it helps preserve both biodiversity and the wider natural
system.
[所以，是的，我會說，把資源投放在保護動物上是值得的，因為這能保護生物多樣性，也能
維持更大的自然系統。]

components.english: That said, I do not think money should be thrown around blindly; it should be spent in a practical and well-targeted way.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為這些錢應該隨便亂花；資源必須用得實際，而且有針對性。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think money should be thrown around blindly; it
should be spent in a practical and well-targeted way.
[不過，我也不認為這些錢應該隨便亂花；資源必須用得實際，而且有針對性。]

components.english: To be honest, simply pouring money into a problem does not always solve it. What really matters is how the money is used. In my view, it is better to spend it on habitat protection, anti-poaching efforts, rescue centres, and public education rather than on flashy campaigns that look good on paper but do very little in real life. In other words, it is not just about spending more; it is about spending wisely.
components.chinese: 老實說，單純把很多錢倒進一個問題裡，未必就能解決事情。真正重要的是，那些錢是怎樣被使用的。在我看來，把資源投放在保護棲息地、打擊偷獵、設立救援中心，以及公眾教育上，會比花在一些表面上很好看、但實際作用很有限的宣傳活動更有效。換句話說，重點不只是花更多錢，而是要花得更明智。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, simply pouring money into a problem does not
always solve it. What really matters is how the money is used. In my view, it is
better to spend it on habitat protection, anti-poaching efforts, rescue centres,
and public education rather than on flashy campaigns that look good on paper
but do very little in real life. In other words, it is not just about spending more;
it is about spending wisely.
[老實說，單純把很多錢倒進一個問題裡，未必就能解決事情。真正重要的是，那些錢是怎
樣被使用的。在我看來，把資源投放在保護棲息地、打擊偷獵、設立救援中心，以及公眾教
育上，會比花在一些表面上很好看、但實際作用很有限的宣傳活動更有效。換句話說，重點
不只是花更多錢，而是要花得更明智。]

components.english: For instance, if a government protects wetlands, forests, or coastal areas, it is not only saving one animal here and there; it is protecting an entire home for many species at once. That usually gives more bang for the buck than short-term actions that only treat the symptom rather than the cause.
components.chinese: 例如，如果政府願意保護濕地、森林，或者沿海地區，那就不只是救了一兩隻動物而已；而是在同一時間保護了很多物種共同生活的家。這樣做通常比一些只處理表面問題、而不是根源的短期行動，更能發揮資源的效果。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a government protects wetlands, forests, or coastal
areas, it is not only saving one animal here and there; it is protecting an entire
home for many species at once. That usually gives more bang for the buck than
short-term actions that only treat the symptom rather than the cause.
[例如，如果政府願意保護濕地、森林，或者沿海地區，那就不只是救了一兩隻動物而已；而
是在同一時間保護了很多物種共同生活的家。這樣做通常比一些只處理表面問題、而不是
根源的短期行動，更能發揮資源的效果。]

components.english: Overall, I do think money should be spent on protecting animals, because once a species disappears, bringing it back is incredibly difficult, if not impossible. In the end, the real issue is not simply whether we spend money, but whether we spend it where it can genuinely make a difference.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為應該花錢保護動物，但關鍵不只是有沒有花，而是有沒有花在真正能改變事情的地方。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I do think money should be spent on protecting
animals, because once a species disappears, bringing it back is incredibly
difficult, if not impossible. In the end, the real issue is not simply whether we
spend money, but whether we spend it where it can genuinely make a difference.
[總結來說，我認為應該花錢保護動物，但關鍵不只是有沒有花，而是有沒有花在真正能改
變事情的地方。]

question.english: Do you think more should be done to protect natural scenic spots in your country?
question.chinese: 你認為在你的國家，是否應該做更多事情去保護自然風景區？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think more should be done to protect natural scenic spots in your
country?
你認為在你的國家，是否應該做更多事情去保護自然風景區？

components.english: Yes, absolutely, I think much more should be done, because natural scenic spots are not just beautiful places to look at; they are part of a country’s identity and a refuge from urban life.
components.chinese: 是的，我絕對認為應該做更多，因為自然風景區不只是讓人欣賞的美麗地方；它們也是一個地方身份的一部分，同時是人們逃離城市生活的一個喘息空間。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, absolutely, I think much more should be done, because natural
scenic spots are not just beautiful places to look at; they are part of a country’s
identity and a refuge from urban life.
[是的，我絕對認為應該做更多，因為自然風景區不只是讓人欣賞的美麗地方；它們也是一
個地方身份的一部分，同時是人們逃離城市生活的一個喘息空間。]

components.english: The main reason is that once these places are damaged, they are incredibly hard to restore. A mountain trail, a coastline, or a wetland may look timeless, but in reality they can be surprisingly fragile. If they are overdeveloped, overcrowded, or polluted, the damage can build up quietly and then hit all at once. In that sense, protecting them is a bit like protecting the lungs of a city: people may not always notice them, but they would certainly feel the loss if they were gone.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，這些地方一旦被破壞，往往非常難恢復。一條山徑、一片海岸線，或者一個濕地，看起來好像歷久不變，但其實它們可能比人想像中脆弱得多。如果它們被過度開發、人流過多，或者受到污染，損害可能會悄悄累積，然後一下子爆發出來。從這個角度來看，保護這些地方有點像保護一座城市的肺部：人們平時未必總會留意它們，但一旦失去，就一定會感受到那種缺口。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that once these places are damaged, they are
incredibly hard to restore. A mountain trail, a coastline, or a wetland may look
timeless, but in reality they can be surprisingly fragile. If they are
overdeveloped, overcrowded, or polluted, the damage can build up quietly and
then hit all at once. In that sense, protecting them is a bit like protecting the
lungs of a city: people may not always notice them, but they would certainly feel
the loss if they were gone.
[主要原因是，這些地方一旦被破壞，往往非常難恢復。一條山徑、一片海岸線，或者一個濕
地，看起來好像歷久不變，但其實它們可能比人想像中脆弱得多。如果它們被過度開發、人
流過多，或者受到污染，損害可能會悄悄累積，然後一下子爆發出來。從這個角度來看，保
護這些地方有點像保護一座城市的肺部：人們平時未必總會留意它們，但一旦失去，就一定
會感受到那種缺口。]

components.english: For example, in places with popular hiking trails, beaches, or country parks, too many visitors can leave behind rubbish, erode the land, and disturb wildlife. A place that once felt peaceful and unspoilt can slowly lose its charm and start to feel worn down. If that happens, people are not only losing a view; they are losing a whole experience.
components.chinese: 例如，在一些受歡迎的行山路線、海灘，或者郊野公園裡，過多遊客可能會留下垃圾、令土地受侵蝕，並干擾野生動物。一個原本寧靜、未被破壞的地方，可能會慢慢失去魅力，開始顯得殘舊而疲憊。如果真的變成那樣，人們失去的就不只是一道風景，而是整種體驗。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in places with popular hiking trails, beaches, or
country parks, too many visitors can leave behind rubbish, erode the land, and
disturb wildlife. A place that once felt peaceful and unspoilt can slowly lose its
charm and start to feel worn down. If that happens, people are not only losing a
view; they are losing a whole experience.
[例如，在一些受歡迎的行山路線、海灘，或者郊野公園裡，過多遊客可能會留下垃圾、令土
地受侵蝕，並干擾野生動物。一個原本寧靜、未被破壞的地方，可能會慢慢失去魅力，開始
顯得殘舊而疲憊。如果真的變成那樣，人們失去的就不只是一道風景，而是整種體驗。]

components.english: So yes, I strongly believe more should be done, because protecting natural scenic spots means protecting beauty, biodiversity, and a healthier way of life at the same time.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我很認為應該做更多保護工作，因為保護自然風景區，其實同時是在保護美景、生態多樣性，以及一種更健康的生活方式。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I strongly believe more should be done, because protecting
natural scenic spots means protecting beauty, biodiversity, and a healthier way
of life at the same time.
[所以，是的，我很認為應該做更多保護工作，因為保護自然風景區，其實同時是在保護美
景、生態多樣性，以及一種更健康的生活方式。]

components.english: That said, I do not think protection should mean shutting people out completely; it should mean managing these places wisely.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為保護就等於完全把人拒於門外；更理想的做法，應該是更有智慧地管理這些地方。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think protection should mean shutting people out
completely; it should mean managing these places wisely.
[不過，我也不認為保護就等於完全把人拒於門外；更理想的做法，應該是更有智慧地管理
這些地方。]

components.english: To be honest, nature should not be treated like something locked behind glass. People should still be able to visit, enjoy, and connect with it. The real issue is how to strike a balance between access and preservation. That could mean stricter rules on littering, better visitor limits, more environmental education, or better planning around tourism. In other words, the goal is not to turn natural areas into museums, but to stop us loving them to death.
components.chinese: 老實說，大自然不應該被當成只能鎖在玻璃後面欣賞的東西。人們仍然應該可以去接觸、享受，並和它建立連結。真正的問題，在於如何在開放使用和保護保存之間取得平衡。那可能包括更嚴格的防止亂拋垃圾規則、更好的人流限制、更多環保教育，或者對旅遊發展作更好的規劃。換句話說，目標不是把自然地區變成博物館，而是避免我們用一種「愛到把它毀掉」的方式去對待它。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, nature should not be treated like something
locked behind glass. People should still be able to visit, enjoy, and connect with
it. The real issue is how to strike a balance between access and preservation.
That could mean stricter rules on littering, better visitor limits, more
environmental education, or better planning around tourism. In other words,
the goal is not to turn natural areas into museums, but to stop us loving them to
death.
[老實說，大自然不應該被當成只能鎖在玻璃後面欣賞的東西。人們仍然應該可以去接觸、
享受，並和它建立連結。真正的問題，在於如何在開放使用和保護保存之間取得平衡。那可
能包括更嚴格的防止亂拋垃圾規則、更好的人流限制、更多環保教育，或者對旅遊發展作更
好的規劃。換句話說，目標不是把自然地區變成博物館，而是避免我們用一種「愛到把它毀
掉」的方式去對待它。]

components.english: For instance, if a scenic island or nature reserve becomes trendy on social media, visitor numbers can shoot through the roof almost overnight. In that case, it makes sense to improve facilities, control numbers during peak periods, and teach people how to behave responsibly there, rather than simply letting the place take the hit.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個風景優美的島嶼或自然保護區因社交媒體而忽然爆紅，遊客數量可能一夜之間飆升。在這種情況下，更合理的做法就是改善配套設施、在高峰時段控制人流，並教育人們如何負責任地使用那個地方，而不是任由那片地方硬生生承受衝擊。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a scenic island or nature reserve becomes trendy on
social media, visitor numbers can shoot through the roof almost overnight. In
that case, it makes sense to improve facilities, control numbers during peak
periods, and teach people how to behave responsibly there, rather than simply
letting the place take the hit.
[例如，如果一個風景優美的島嶼或自然保護區因社交媒體而忽然爆紅，遊客數量可能一夜
之間飆升。在這種情況下，更合理的做法就是改善配套設施、在高峰時段控制人流，並教育
人們如何負責任地使用那個地方，而不是任由那片地方硬生生承受衝擊。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say more protection is definitely needed, but it should be smart protection rather than blunt restriction. The best approach is to keep these places open enough to inspire people, but protected enough to survive for generations.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說更多保護措施絕對有需要，但那應該是有智慧的保護，而不是生硬的限制。最好的做法，是讓這些地方既保留足夠的開放性去打動人，也同時受到足夠的保護，能夠一代一代地保存下去。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say more protection is definitely needed, but it
should be smart protection rather than blunt restriction. The best approach is
to keep these places open enough to inspire people, but protected enough to
survive for generations.
[總結來說，我會說更多保護措施絕對有需要，但那應該是有智慧的保護，而不是生硬的限
制。最好的做法，是讓這些地方既保留足夠的開放性去打動人，也同時受到足夠的保護，能
夠一代一代地保存下去。]

question.english: What can individuals do to try and ensure water is kept clean?
question.chinese: 個人可以做些甚麼來盡量確保水源保持清潔？

source_lines.text: Topic: What can individuals do to try and ensure water is kept clean?
個人可以做些甚麼來盡量確保水源保持清潔？

components.english: I think individuals can do quite a lot to help keep water clean, even if they cannot solve the whole problem on their own.
components.chinese: 我認為，個人其實可以做很多事情去幫助保持水源清潔，即使他們未必能夠單靠自己解決整個問題。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think individuals can do quite a lot to help keep water clean, even if
they cannot solve the whole problem on their own.
[我認為，個人其實可以做很多事情去幫助保持水源清潔，即使他們未必能夠單靠自己解決
整個問題。]

components.english: The main point is that water pollution does not only come from large factories or major accidents; it also grows out of everyday habits. If people litter, waste water, pour oil or chemicals down the drain, or rely too heavily on single-use plastic, the damage adds up over time. So, in my view, one of the most important things individuals can do is be more careful and responsible in daily life. Keeping water clean often begins with small choices at home, not just grand policies from above.
components.chinese: 重點在於，水污染不只是來自大型工廠或嚴重事故；它其實也會由日常習慣一點一滴累積起來。如果人們亂拋垃圾、浪費食水、把食油或化學品倒進去水渠，或者過度依賴即棄塑膠，長遠下來，破壞就會慢慢累積。所以在我看來，個人最重要能做的事之一，就是在日常生活中更小心、更有責任感。保持水清潔，很多時候不是從甚麼宏大政策開始，而是從家中的小選擇開始。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main point is that water pollution does not only come from
large factories or major accidents; it also grows out of everyday habits. If people
litter, waste water, pour oil or chemicals down the drain, or rely too heavily on
single-use plastic, the damage adds up over time. So, in my view, one of the most
important things individuals can do is be more careful and responsible in daily
life. Keeping water clean often begins with small choices at home, not just grand
policies from above.
[重點在於，水污染不只是來自大型工廠或嚴重事故；它其實也會由日常習慣一點一滴累積
起來。如果人們亂拋垃圾、浪費食水、把食油或化學品倒進去水渠，或者過度依賴即棄塑膠
，長遠下來，破壞就會慢慢累積。所以在我看來，個人最重要能做的事之一，就是在日常生
活中更小心、更有責任感。保持水清潔，很多時候不是從甚麼宏大政策開始，而是從家中的
小選擇開始。]

components.english: For example, people can avoid throwing rubbish into rivers or the sea, cut down on plastic bottles and packaging, and dispose of used oil, cleaning products, or batteries properly instead of letting them end up in the water system. A family that forms those habits may seem small, but it is still one less source of pollution.
components.chinese: 例如，人們可以避免把垃圾扔進河流或大海、減少使用塑膠樽和過度包裝，並且把用過的食油、清潔用品，或者電池妥善處理，而不是讓它們最終流入水系統。一個家庭養成這些習慣，看起來也許很小，但它始終已經少了一個污染源頭。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, people can avoid throwing rubbish into rivers or the
sea, cut down on plastic bottles and packaging, and dispose of used oil, cleaning
products, or batteries properly instead of letting them end up in the water
system. A family that forms those habits may seem small, but it is still one less
source of pollution.
[例如，人們可以避免把垃圾扔進河流或大海、減少使用塑膠樽和過度包裝，並且把用過的
食油、清潔用品，或者電池妥善處理，而不是讓它們最終流入水系統。一個家庭養成這些習
慣，看起來也許很小，但它始終已經少了一個污染源頭。]

components.english: So, I would say individual responsibility really matters, because cleaner water often starts with cleaner habits.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說個人的責任其實很重要，因為更乾淨的水，往往就是從更乾淨的生活習慣開始的。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I would say individual responsibility really matters, because
cleaner water often starts with cleaner habits.
[所以，我會說個人的責任其實很重要，因為更乾淨的水，往往就是從更乾淨的生活習慣開
始的。]

components.english: On top of that, individuals can also make a difference by influencing other people and supporting community action.
components.chinese: 除此之外，個人也可以透過影響身邊的人，以及支持社區行動，去帶來改變。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, individuals can also make a difference by influencing
other people and supporting community action.
[除此之外，個人也可以透過影響身邊的人，以及支持社區行動，去帶來改變。]

components.english: To be honest, environmental protection works best when it spreads from person to person. If someone joins a beach clean-up, teaches children not to treat drains like a dumping ground, or speaks up when they see careless behaviour, that can create a ripple effect. In other words, one person’s action may be small by itself, but it can still help shift attitudes in the right direction. After all, every drop counts.
components.chinese: 老實說，環境保護最有效的時候，往往就是它能夠由一個人傳到另一個人的時候。如果有人參加淨灘活動、教孩子不要把排水渠當成垃圾桶，或者看到不負責任的行為時願意出聲提醒，那其實都可以產生連鎖效應。換句話說，一個人的行動本身也許不算大，但它仍然可以幫助整體觀念朝正確方向改變。畢竟，每一滴都很重要。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, environmental protection works best when it
spreads from person to person. If someone joins a beach clean-up, teaches
children not to treat drains like a dumping ground, or speaks up when they see
careless behaviour, that can create a ripple effect. In other words, one person’s
action may be small by itself, but it can still help shift attitudes in the right
direction. After all, every drop counts.
[老實說，環境保護最有效的時候，往往就是它能夠由一個人傳到另一個人的時候。如果有
人參加淨灘活動、教孩子不要把排水渠當成垃圾桶，或者看到不負責任的行為時願意出聲
提醒，那其實都可以產生連鎖效應。換句話說，一個人的行動本身也許不算大，但它仍然可
以幫助整體觀念朝正確方向改變。畢竟，每一滴都很重要。]

components.english: For instance, if a student takes part in a school clean-up campaign and then encourages their family to use less plastic and waste less water at home, the effect goes beyond that one event. What starts as a small action can gradually snowball into a more responsible way of living.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個學生參加了學校的清潔活動，之後又鼓勵家人減少使用塑膠、在家節約用水，那影響其實就不只停留在那一次活動上。原本只是一個小行動，慢慢也可以滾動成一種更有責任感的生活方式。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a student takes part in a school clean-up campaign
and then encourages their family to use less plastic and waste less water at home,
the effect goes beyond that one event. What starts as a small action can gradually
snowball into a more responsible way of living.
[例如，如果一個學生參加了學校的清潔活動，之後又鼓勵家人減少使用塑膠、在家節約用
水，那影響其實就不只停留在那一次活動上。原本只是一個小行動，慢慢也可以滾動成一種
更有責任感的生活方式。]

components.english: Overall, I think individuals can help keep water clean by changing their own habits and encouraging others to do the same. They may not be able to turn the whole situation around single-handedly, but they can absolutely be part of the solution rather than part of the problem.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為個人可以透過改變自己的習慣，以及鼓勵別人一起做，來幫助保持水源清潔。他們未必能夠一手扭轉整個局面，但他們絕對可以成為問題變好而不是變壞的一部分。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think individuals can help keep water clean by
changing their own habits and encouraging others to do the same. They may
not be able to turn the whole situation around single-handedly, but they can
absolutely be part of the solution rather than part of the problem.
[總結來說，我認為個人可以透過改變自己的習慣，以及鼓勵別人一起做，來幫助保持水源
清潔。他們未必能夠一手扭轉整個局面，但他們絕對可以成為問題變好而不是變壞的一部

question.english: What rewards can children get from school?
question.chinese: 孩子可以從學校得到甚麼回報？

source_lines.text: Topic: What rewards can children get from school?
孩子可以從學校得到甚麼回報？

components.english: I think the most obvious rewards children get from school are knowledge, practical skills, and qualifications that can open doors later in life.
components.chinese: 我認為，孩子從學校得到最明顯的回報，就是知識、實用技能，以及將來能替他們打開機會之門的學歷和資格。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the most obvious rewards children get from school are
knowledge, practical skills, and qualifications that can open doors later in life.
[我認為，孩子從學校得到最明顯的回報，就是知識、實用技能，以及將來能替他們打開機
會之門的學歷和資格。]

components.english: School gives children the building blocks they need for life. They learn how to read, write, count, solve problems, and think more clearly. On top of that, they develop study habits, discipline, and the ability to work towards long-term goals. In that sense, school is not just a place where children collect facts; it is where they lay the foundations for the rest of their lives.
components.chinese: 學校會給孩子人生所需要的基本磚塊。他們在那裡學會閱讀、書寫、計算、解難，以及更清晰地思考。除此之外，他們也會培養學習習慣、自律能力，以及朝長遠目標努力的本事。從這個角度來看，學校不只是一個讓孩子收集知識的地方；它更是一個讓他們為未來人生打地基的地方。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: School gives children the building blocks they need for life.
They learn how to read, write, count, solve problems, and think more clearly.
On top of that, they develop study habits, discipline, and the ability to work
towards long-term goals. In that sense, school is not just a place where children
collect facts; it is where they lay the foundations for the rest of their lives.
[學校會給孩子人生所需要的基本磚塊。他們在那裡學會閱讀、書寫、計算、解難，以及更清
晰地思考。除此之外，他們也會培養學習習慣、自律能力，以及朝長遠目標努力的本事。從
這個角度來看，學校不只是一個讓孩子收集知識的地方；它更是一個讓他們為未來人生打
地基的地方。]

components.english: For example, a child who learns good reading and writing skills at school is much more likely to do well in future studies and eventually have better career options. Even something as basic as learning how to manage homework can teach them responsibility and time management.
components.chinese: 例如，一個在學校裡學好閱讀和寫作能力的孩子，將來在進一步學習時往往會更容易表現出色，之後也更可能擁有較好的職業選擇。即使只是像管理功課這麼基本的事情，其實也能教會他們責任感和時間管理。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a child who learns good reading and writing skills at
school is much more likely to do well in future studies and eventually have
better career options. Even something as basic as learning how to manage
homework can teach them responsibility and time management.
[例如，一個在學校裡學好閱讀和寫作能力的孩子，將來在進一步學習時往往會更容易表現
出色，之後也更可能擁有較好的職業選擇。即使只是像管理功課這麼基本的事情，其實也能
教會他們責任感和時間管理。]

components.english: So, one major reward children get from school is a strong academic and practical foundation that can serve them for years to come.
components.chinese: 所以，孩子從學校得到的一個重要回報，就是一個扎實的學術和實際能力基礎，而這種基礎能在往後很多年裡持續幫助他們。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major reward children get from school is a strong
academic and practical foundation that can serve them for years to come.
[所以，孩子從學校得到的一個重要回報，就是一個扎實的學術和實際能力基礎，而這種基
礎能在往後很多年裡持續幫助他們。]

components.english: Another equally important reward is personal growth, including confidence, friendships, social skills, and a sense of achievement.
components.chinese: 另一個同樣重要的回報，就是個人成長，包括自信、友誼、社交能力，以及成就感。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another equally important reward is personal growth, including
confidence, friendships, social skills, and a sense of achievement.
[另一個同樣重要的回報，就是個人成長，包括自信、友誼、社交能力，以及成就感。]

components.english: To me, school is also a training ground for life. Children learn how to speak up, cooperate, handle disagreement, and bounce back when things do not go their way. They also begin to discover what they are good at, whether that is sport, music, leadership, art, or simply helping others. A prize or a high mark may feel good in the moment, but the deeper reward is often the confidence that quietly takes root over time.
components.chinese: 在我看來，學校同時也是人生的訓練場。孩子會在那裡學會如何表達自己、如何合作、如何處理分歧，以及當事情不如預期時如何重新站起來。他們也會開始發現自己的長處，無論那是運動、音樂、領導能力、藝術，還是單純地幫助別人。一個獎項或高分在當下當然會令人開心，但更深層的回報，往往是那份隨時間靜靜扎根的自信。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To me, school is also a training ground for life. Children learn
how to speak up, cooperate, handle disagreement, and bounce back when things
do not go their way. They also begin to discover what they are good at, whether
that is sport, music, leadership, art, or simply helping others. A prize or a high
mark may feel good in the moment, but the deeper reward is often the
confidence that quietly takes root over time.
[在我看來，學校同時也是人生的訓練場。孩子會在那裡學會如何表達自己、如何合作、如
何處理分歧，以及當事情不如預期時如何重新站起來。他們也會開始發現自己的長處，無論
那是運動、音樂、領導能力、藝術，還是單純地幫助別人。一個獎項或高分在當下當然會令
人開心，但更深層的回報，往往是那份隨時間靜靜扎根的自信。]

components.english: For instance, a shy child may join a group project or give a class presentation and gradually realise that they are more capable than they thought. In the same way, children who make close friends at school often gain emotional support and a stronger sense of belonging.
components.chinese: 例如，一個本來很害羞的孩子，可能透過參與小組作業或在班上作簡報，慢慢發現自己其實比想像中更有能力。同樣地，在學校交到親密朋友的孩子，往往也能得到情感上的支持，以及更強的歸屬感。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a shy child may join a group project or give a class
presentation and gradually realise that they are more capable than they thought.
In the same way, children who make close friends at school often gain emotional
support and a stronger sense of belonging.

以及更強的歸屬感。]

components.english: Overall, I would say the rewards children get from school are not only academic. They also gain character, confidence, and life skills, and in many ways those rewards are worth just as much as the grades on a report card.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說孩子從學校得到的回報並不只是學業上的。他們同時也會獲得品格、自信，以及生活技能，而在很多方面，這些回報其實和成績表上的分數一樣珍貴。

not only academic. They also gain character, confidence, and life skills, and in
many ways those rewards are worth just as much as the grades on a report card.

信，以及生活技能，而在很多方面，這些回報其實和成績表上的分數一樣珍貴。]

question.english: Should parents push their children to get prizes?
question.chinese: 父母應不應該逼孩子去拿獎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Should parents push their children to get prizes?
父母應不應該逼孩子去拿獎？

components.english: Personally, I would say parents should encourage their children to do well, but they should not push them too hard just to win prizes.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我會說父母應該鼓勵孩子努力表現，但不應該為了拿獎而把他們逼得太緊。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I would say parents should encourage their children to do
well, but they should not push them too hard just to win prizes.
[就我個人而言，我會說父母應該鼓勵孩子努力表現，但不應該為了拿獎而把他們逼得太

components.english: The main reason is that a prize can be motivating, but too much pressure can easily backfire. If children feel that winning is the only thing that matters, they may become anxious, discouraged, or afraid of failure. In that case, achievement stops being a source of pride and starts to feel like a burden. After all, childhood should not feel like a never-ending race. I think it is far healthier for parents to focus on effort, growth, and enjoyment rather than treating every activity like a contest that has to be won.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，獎項的確可以帶來動力，但壓力太大往往很容易適得其反。如果孩子覺得贏是唯一重要的事，他們就可能變得焦慮、洩氣，或者很害怕失敗。到了那一步，成就感就不再是值得驕傲的來源，反而會變成一種負擔。畢竟，童年不應該像一場永無止境的競賽。我認為，父母把重點放在努力、成長和樂趣上，會比把每件事都當成非贏不可的比賽健康得多。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that a prize can be motivating, but too much
pressure can easily backfire. If children feel that winning is the only thing that
matters, they may become anxious, discouraged, or afraid of failure. In that case,
achievement stops being a source of pride and starts to feel like a burden. After
all, childhood should not feel like a never-ending race. I think it is far healthier
for parents to focus on effort, growth, and enjoyment rather than treating every
activity like a contest that has to be won.
[主要原因是，獎項的確可以帶來動力，但壓力太大往往很容易適得其反。如果孩子覺得贏
是唯一重要的事，他們就可能變得焦慮、洩氣，或者很害怕失敗。到了那一步，成就感就不
再是值得驕傲的來源，反而會變成一種負擔。畢竟，童年不應該像一場永無止境的競賽。我
認為，父母把重點放在努力、成長和樂趣上，會比把每件事都當成非贏不可的比賽健康得

components.english: For example, if a child is learning the piano, it is fine for parents to encourage them to practise and prepare for a competition. However, if the parents keep comparing them with other children or make them feel that second place is somehow a failure, the child may end up hating music altogether. In that situation, the prize may be won, but something more important may be lost.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子正在學鋼琴，父母鼓勵他練習，或者為比賽作準備，這其實沒有問題。不過，如果父母不停把他和其他孩子比較，或者令他覺得拿第二名就算失敗，那麼這個孩子最後可能會連音樂本身都討厭起來。在那種情況下，也許獎是拿到了，但更重要的東西反而失去了。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child is learning the piano, it is fine for parents to
encourage them to practise and prepare for a competition. However, if the
parents keep comparing them with other children or make them feel that second
place is somehow a failure, the child may end up hating music altogether. In that
situation, the prize may be won, but something more important may be lost.
[例如，如果一個孩子正在學鋼琴，父母鼓勵他練習，或者為比賽作準備，這其實沒有問題。
不過，如果父母不停把他和其他孩子比較，或者令他覺得拿第二名就算失敗，那麼這個孩子
最後可能會連音樂本身都討厭起來。在那種情況下，也許獎是拿到了，但更重要的東西反而
失去了。]

components.english: So, in my view, parents should inspire their children rather than drive them too hard, because confidence grows better in support than in pressure.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，父母應該啟發孩子，而不是把他們逼得太狠，因為自信在支持之下會比在壓力之下長得更好。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in my view, parents should inspire their children rather than
drive them too hard, because confidence grows better in support than in
pressure.
[所以，在我看來，父母應該啟發孩子，而不是把他們逼得太狠，因為自信在支持之下會比
在壓力之下長得更好。]

components.english: That said, I do think a gentle push can sometimes be helpful, because children do not always recognise their own potential.
components.chinese: 不過，我的確認為，適度的推動有時是有幫助的，因為孩子未必總能看見自己的潛力。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think a gentle push can sometimes be helpful, because
children do not always recognise their own potential.
[不過，我的確認為，適度的推動有時是有幫助的，因為孩子未必總能看見自己的潛力。]

components.english: Some children give up at the first hurdle or shy away from challenges simply because they lack confidence. In those cases, a parent’s encouragement can act like a small nudge in the right direction. The key difference, though, is between guiding and forcing. A gentle push can be a springboard, whereas constant pressure can feel like a weight around a child’s neck. So yes, parents should help children raise their game, but they should not make prizes the measure of their worth.
components.chinese: 有些孩子一遇到困難就很快放棄，或者因為缺乏自信而逃避挑戰。在這些情況下，父母的鼓勵就像在正確方向上輕輕推一把。不過，關鍵分別就在於「引導」和「強迫」之間。溫和的推動可以成為踏腳石，但持續不斷的壓力卻可能像掛在孩子脖子上的重物一樣。所以，父母的確可以幫助孩子提升表現，但不應把獎項變成衡量孩子價值的標準。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Some children give up at the first hurdle or shy away from
challenges simply because they lack confidence. In those cases, a parent’s
encouragement can act like a small nudge in the right direction. The key
difference, though, is between guiding and forcing. A gentle push can be a
springboard, whereas constant pressure can feel like a weight around a child’s
neck. So yes, parents should help children raise their game, but they should not
make prizes the measure of their worth.
[有些孩子一遇到困難就很快放棄，或者因為缺乏自信而逃避挑戰。在這些情況下，父母的
鼓勵就像在正確方向上輕輕推一把。不過，關鍵分別就在於「引導」和「強迫」之間。溫和的推
動可以成為踏腳石，但持續不斷的壓力卻可能像掛在孩子脖子上的重物一樣。所以，父母的
確可以幫助孩子提升表現，但不應把獎項變成衡量孩子價值的標準。]

components.english: For instance, a child may be nervous about joining a speech competition and say they want to quit before even trying. In that case, a parent who encourages them to give it a go may actually be doing them a favour. Even if the child does not win, they may still gain courage, experience, and a sense of progress, which can matter far more in the long run.
components.chinese: 例如，一個孩子可能因為害怕而不敢參加演講比賽，甚至在開始之前就想放棄。在這種情況下，父母鼓勵他先試一次，其實反而是在幫他。即使最後沒有得獎，他仍然可能得到勇氣、經驗，以及一種自己正在進步的感覺，而從長遠來看，這些往往比獎項本身更重要。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a child may be nervous about joining a speech
competition and say they want to quit before even trying. In that case, a parent
who encourages them to give it a go may actually be doing them a favour. Even if
the child does not win, they may still gain courage, experience, and a sense of
progress, which can matter far more in the long run.
[例如，一個孩子可能因為害怕而不敢參加演講比賽，甚至在開始之前就想放棄。在這種情
況下，父母鼓勵他先試一次，其實反而是在幫他。即使最後沒有得獎，他仍然可能得到勇
氣、經驗，以及一種自己正在進步的感覺，而從長遠來看，這些往往比獎項本身更重要。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say parents should encourage their children to aim high, but not push them in a way that turns success into stress. A prize should be the icing on the cake, not the whole cake.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說父母應該鼓勵孩子追求更高目標，但不應該用一種把成功變成壓力的方式去逼他們。獎項應該只是錦上添花，而不應該變成整件事情的全部。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say parents should encourage their children to aim
high, but not push them in a way that turns success into stress. A prize should
be the icing on the cake, not the whole cake.
[總結來說，我會說父母應該鼓勵孩子追求更高目標，但不應該用一種把成功變成壓力的方
式去逼他們。獎項應該只是錦上添花，而不應該變成整件事情的全部。]

question.english: Is it good for children to compete for prizes at school?
question.chinese: 在學校裡，讓孩子為獎項而競爭是好事嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is it good for children to compete for prizes at school?
[在學校裡，讓孩子為獎項而競爭是好事嗎？]

components.english: Yes, I think it can be good for children to compete for prizes at school, provided that the competition is healthy and well handled.
components.chinese: 是的，我認為讓孩子在學校裡為獎項而競爭可以是好事，只要那種競爭是健康的，而且處理得當。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I think it can be good for children to compete for prizes at school,
provided that the competition is healthy and well handled.
[是的，我認為讓孩子在學校裡為獎項而競爭可以是好事，只要那種競爭是健康的，而且處
理得當。]

components.english: The main reason is that prizes can give children a clear goal to work towards. They often encourage students to put in more effort, stay focused, and push themselves a little further than they otherwise would. In that sense, a prize can act like a small spark: it can light a fire under a child and make them realise what they are capable of. On top of that, competition can teach useful life lessons, such as discipline, persistence, and how to cope when things do not go your way.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，獎項可以為孩子提供一個清楚的目標去努力。它往往會鼓勵學生付出更多心力、保持專注，並且比平時再多推自己一步。從這個角度來看，獎項就像一點小火花：它可以點燃孩子的動力，讓他們意識到自己其實做得到甚麼。除此之外，競爭也能教會孩子一些很有用的人生道理，例如紀律、堅持，以及當事情不如自己所願時應該如何面對。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that prizes can give children a clear goal to
work towards. They often encourage students to put in more effort, stay
focused, and push themselves a little further than they otherwise would. In that
sense, a prize can act like a small spark: it can light a fire under a child and make
them realise what they are capable of. On top of that, competition can teach
useful life lessons, such as discipline, persistence, and how to cope when things
do not go your way.
[主要原因是，獎項可以為孩子提供一個清楚的目標去努力。它往往會鼓勵學生付出更多心
力、保持專注，並且比平時再多推自己一步。從這個角度來看，獎項就像一點小火花：它可
以點燃孩子的動力，讓他們意識到自己其實做得到甚麼。除此之外，競爭也能教會孩子一些
很有用的人生道理，例如紀律、堅持，以及當事情不如自己所願時應該如何面對。]

components.english: For example, if a school holds a reading award, a science competition, or a sports prize, some children may become much more motivated to practise, prepare, and improve. Even if they do not win in the end, the process itself can still help them build confidence and a stronger work ethic.
components.chinese: 例如，如果學校設有閱讀獎、科學比賽，或者體育獎項，有些孩子就可能會因此更有動力去練習、準備，以及提升自己。即使最後沒有贏，整個過程本身也仍然能幫助他們建立自信，以及更強的做事態度。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a school holds a reading award, a science
competition, or a sports prize, some children may become much more motivated
to practise, prepare, and improve. Even if they do not win in the end, the
process itself can still help them build confidence and a stronger work ethic.
[例如，如果學校設有閱讀獎、科學比賽，或者體育獎項，有些孩子就可能會因此更有動力
去練習、準備，以及提升自己。即使最後沒有贏，整個過程本身也仍然能幫助他們建立自信
，以及更強的做事態度。]

components.english: So yes, I would say competition for prizes can be beneficial, because it can motivate children and help them grow through challenge.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說為獎項而競爭可以帶來好處，因為它能激勵孩子，也能讓他們透過挑戰而成長。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say competition for prizes can be beneficial,
because it can motivate children and help them grow through challenge.
[所以，是的，我會說為獎項而競爭可以帶來好處，因為它能激勵孩子，也能讓他們透過挑
戰而成長。]

components.english: That said, I do not think prize-based competition is always positive, especially if winning becomes the only thing that matters.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為以獎項為中心的競爭總是正面的，尤其是當「贏」變成唯一重要的事情時。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think prize-based competition is always positive,
especially if winning becomes the only thing that matters.
[不過，我並不認為以獎項為中心的競爭總是正面的，尤其是當「贏」變成唯一重要的事情

components.english: If schools place too much emphasis on prizes, some children may start to tie their self-worth to results. That is where things can go off the rails. Instead of enjoying learning, they may become anxious, overly competitive, or afraid of failure. Children who do not win may feel left out or begin to think they are simply not good enough, which can be quite damaging at a young age. A prize can be a useful push, but it should not become the whole story.
components.chinese: 如果學校過分強調獎項，有些孩子就可能會開始把自己的價值感和結果綁在一起。那樣一來，事情就很容易失去平衡。他們可能不再享受學習，反而變得焦慮、過度好勝，或者害怕失敗。那些沒有得獎的孩子，也可能會感到被排除在外，甚至開始覺得自己天生就不夠好，而這對年紀還小的孩子來說，其實是相當有傷害性的。獎項可以是一種推動力，但它不應該變成整件事的全部。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If schools place too much emphasis on prizes, some children
may start to tie their self-worth to results. That is where things can go off the
rails. Instead of enjoying learning, they may become anxious, overly competitive,
or afraid of failure. Children who do not win may feel left out or begin to think
they are simply not good enough, which can be quite damaging at a young age.
A prize can be a useful push, but it should not become the whole story.
[如果學校過分強調獎項，有些孩子就可能會開始把自己的價值感和結果綁在一起。那樣一
來，事情就很容易失去平衡。他們可能不再享受學習，反而變得焦慮、過度好勝，或者害怕
失敗。那些沒有得獎的孩子，也可能會感到被排除在外，甚至開始覺得自己天生就不夠好，
而這對年紀還小的孩子來說，其實是相當有傷害性的。獎項可以是一種推動力，但它不應該
變成整件事的全部。]

components.english: For instance, if one student wins prizes again and again while others are rarely recognised, the classroom can start to feel less like a place of learning and more like a race with only a few winners. In that kind of environment, children may compare themselves too much instead of learning at their own pace.
components.chinese: 例如，如果同一個學生一次又一次得獎，而其他人卻很少被看見，那麼課室就可能慢慢不再像一個學習的地方，反而更像一場只有少數人能贏的比賽。在那種環境裡，孩子可能會過度和別人比較，而不是按照自己的步伐去學習。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if one student wins prizes again and again while
others are rarely recognised, the classroom can start to feel less like a place of
learning and more like a race with only a few winners. In that kind of
environment, children may compare themselves too much instead of learning at
their own pace.
[例如，如果同一個學生一次又一次得獎，而其他人卻很少被看見，那麼課室就可能慢慢不
再像一個學習的地方，反而更像一場只有少數人能贏的比賽。在那種環境裡，孩子可能會過
度和別人比較，而不是按照自己的步伐去學習。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say it is good for children to compete for prizes at school in moderation, as long as the focus is not only on winning but also on effort, progress, and enjoyment. The healthiest competition is the kind that lifts children up rather than weighs them down.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說讓孩子在學校裡為獎項而競爭是可以的，但要有分寸，而且重點不應只放在輸贏上，也要放在努力、進步，以及學習的樂趣上。最健康的競爭，是那種能把孩子托高，而不是把他們壓垮的競爭。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say it is good for children to compete for prizes at
school in moderation, as long as the focus is not only on winning but also on
effort, progress, and enjoyment. The healthiest competition is the kind that lifts
children up rather than weighs them down.
[總結來說，我會說讓孩子在學校裡為獎項而競爭是可以的，但要有分寸，而且重點不應只
放在輸贏上，也要放在努力、進步，以及學習的樂趣上。最健康的競爭，是那種能把孩子托
高，而不是把他們壓垮的競爭。]

question.english: What kinds of rewards can companies offer to their outstanding employees?
question.chinese: 公司可以向表現出色的員工提供哪些獎勵？

source_lines.text: What kinds of rewards can companies offer to their outstanding employees?
公司可以向表現出色的員工提供哪些獎勵？

components.english: I think companies can reward outstanding employees in several ways, such as giving bonuses, promotions, extra leave, or better career opportunities.
components.chinese: 我認為，公司其實可以用幾種不同方式去獎勵表現出色的員工，例如發放獎金、提供升職機會、額外假期，或者更好的職涯發展機會。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think companies can reward outstanding employees in several ways,
such as giving bonuses, promotions, extra leave, or better career opportunities.
[我認為，公司其實可以用幾種不同方式去獎勵表現出色的員工，例如發放獎金、提供升職
機會、額外假期，或者更好的職涯發展機會。]

components.english: The reason is quite simple: when someone has worked really hard and delivered strong results, they usually want to feel that their effort has been seen and valued. A reward is not just a nice gesture; it is a signal. It tells employees, “Your work matters.” In many cases, financial rewards like bonuses are important because, after all, a pat on the back is nice, but it does not pay the bills. At the same time, things like promotion or paid leave can also mean a lot, because they show trust and appreciation in a more lasting way.
components.chinese: 原因其實很簡單：當一個人真的很努力，而且做出了很好的成績時，他通常都希望自己的付出有被看見、有被重視。獎勵不只是一個好看的姿態，它其實是一種訊號，告訴員工：「你的工作是有價值的。」很多時候，金錢上的獎勵，例如獎金，確實很重要，因為說到底，口頭稱讚雖然令人開心，但不能拿來交帳單。與此同時，升職或者有薪假期之類的獎勵，也很有分量，因為它們能以更長遠的方式表達信任和欣賞。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is quite simple: when someone has worked really
hard and delivered strong results, they usually want to feel that their effort has
been seen and valued. A reward is not just a nice gesture; it is a signal. It tells
employees, “Your work matters.” In many cases, financial rewards like bonuses
are important because, after all, a pat on the back is nice, but it does not pay the
bills. At the same time, things like promotion or paid leave can also mean a lot,
because they show trust and appreciation in a more lasting way.
[原因其實很簡單：當一個人真的很努力，而且做出了很好的成績時，他通常都希望自己的
付出有被看見、有被重視。獎勵不只是一個好看的姿態，它其實是一種訊號，告訴員工：「你
的工作是有價值的。」很多時候，金錢上的獎勵，例如獎金，確實很重要，因為說到底，口頭
稱讚雖然令人開心，但不能拿來交帳單。與此同時，升職或者有薪假期之類的獎勵，也很有
分量，因為它們能以更長遠的方式表達信任和欣賞。]

components.english: For example, if a sales employee performs exceptionally well for a whole year, the company might give them a year-end bonus, recognise them publicly at a staff event, or even move them into a more senior role. That kind of reward does more than make them feel good for one day; it can light a fire under them and motivate them to keep raising their game.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一位銷售員工整年表現都非常突出，公司可能會給他年終獎金、在員工活動中公開表揚他，甚至把他晉升到更高級的職位。這類獎勵不只是讓他開心一天而已；它其實能點燃他的動力，令他更有心繼續把表現提升上去。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a sales employee performs exceptionally well for a
whole year, the company might give them a year-end bonus, recognise them
publicly at a staff event, or even move them into a more senior role. That kind of
reward does more than make them feel good for one day; it can light a fire under
them and motivate them to keep raising their game.
[例如，如果一位銷售員工整年表現都非常突出，公司可能會給他年終獎金、在員工活動中
公開表揚他，甚至把他晉升到更高級的職位。這類獎勵不只是讓他開心一天而已；它其實能
點燃他的動力，令他更有心繼續把表現提升上去。]

components.english: So, I’d say practical rewards like money, promotion, and time off are some of the most effective ways to recognise outstanding employees.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說，像金錢獎勵、升職機會，以及額外休假這些較實際的回報，都是表揚優秀員工最有效的方法之一。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I’d say practical rewards like money, promotion, and time off
are some of the most effective ways to recognise outstanding employees.
[所以，我會說，像金錢獎勵、升職機會，以及額外休假這些較實際的回報，都是表揚優秀員
工最有效的方法之一。]

components.english: That said, I do not think rewards always have to be financial, because many employees also value recognition, trust, and personal growth.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為獎勵一定要是金錢上的，因為很多員工同樣很重視認可、信任，以及個人成長。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think rewards always have to be financial, because
many employees also value recognition, trust, and personal growth.
[不過，我也不認為獎勵一定要是金錢上的，因為很多員工同樣很重視認可、信任，以及個
人成長。]

components.english: Sometimes the most meaningful reward is not just more money, but a stronger sense that the company believes in you. For some people, being given more responsibility, flexible working arrangements, training opportunities, or the chance to lead a project can mean just as much as cash. A good reward should do two things at once: recognise past effort and open the door to future growth. In that sense, the best companies do not just hand out rewards; they invest in people.
components.chinese: 有時候，最有意義的獎勵不只是多一點錢，而是更強烈地感受到公司真的相信你、重視你。對某些人來說，獲得更多責任、彈性的工作安排、培訓機會，或者領導項目的機會，其實和現金一樣有分量。一個好的獎勵，最好能同時做到兩件事：一方面肯定過去的努力，另一方面打開未來成長的大門。從這個角度來說，最好的公司不只是發獎勵，而是在投資人才。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Sometimes the most meaningful reward is not just more
money, but a stronger sense that the company believes in you. For some people,
being given more responsibility, flexible working arrangements, training
opportunities, or the chance to lead a project can mean just as much as cash. A
good reward should do two things at once: recognise past effort and open the
door to future growth. In that sense, the best companies do not just hand out
rewards; they invest in people.
[有時候，最有意義的獎勵不只是多一點錢，而是更強烈地感受到公司真的相信你、重視
你。對某些人來說，獲得更多責任、彈性的工作安排、培訓機會，或者領導項目的機會，其實
和現金一樣有分量。一個好的獎勵，最好能同時做到兩件事：一方面肯定過去的努力，另一
方面打開未來成長的大門。從這個角度來說，最好的公司不只是發獎勵，而是在投資人

components.english: For instance, if a young employee shows strong potential, a company might sponsor a professional course, assign them a mentor, or let them take charge of an important task. That may not feel flashy on the surface, but in the long run it can be worth its weight in gold.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一位年輕員工展現出很強的潛力，公司可以資助他讀專業課程、安排一位導師帶領他，或者讓他負責一項重要任務。這些獎勵表面上未必特別耀眼，但從長遠來看，往往非常有價值。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a young employee shows strong potential, a
company might sponsor a professional course, assign them a mentor, or let them
take charge of an important task. That may not feel flashy on the surface, but in
the long run it can be worth its weight in gold.
[例如，如果一位年輕員工展現出很強的潛力，公司可以資助他讀專業課程、安排一位導師
帶領他，或者讓他負責一項重要任務。這些獎勵表面上未必特別耀眼，但從長遠來看，往往
非常有價值。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say companies can reward outstanding employees in both material and non-material ways, from bonuses and promotions to trust and development opportunities. In the end, the best reward is one that not only says “well done,” but also gives people a reason to stay, grow, and keep doing their best.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，公司可以用物質和非物質兩方面去獎勵優秀員工，從獎金和升職，到信任和發展機會都算在內。說到底，最好的獎勵不只是說一句「你做得很好」，而是能讓員工更願意留下來、繼續成長，並持續做到最好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say companies can reward outstanding employees in
both material and non-material ways, from bonuses and promotions to trust
and development opportunities. In the end, the best reward is one that not only
says “well done,” but also gives people a reason to stay, grow, and keep doing
their best.
[總結來說，我會說，公司可以用物質和非物質兩方面去獎勵優秀員工，從獎金和升職，到
信任和發展機會都算在內。說到底，最好的獎勵不只是說一句「你做得很好」，而是能讓員
工更願意留下來、繼續成長，並持續做到最好。]

question.english: Is it good to have competition?
question.chinese: 你認為競爭是好事嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Is it good to have competition?
你認為競爭是好事嗎？

components.english: Yes, generally speaking, I think competition can be a good thing, because it pushes people to improve and stops them from becoming too comfortable.
components.chinese: 是的，一般來說，我認為競爭可以是一件好事，因為它會推動人進步，也能防止人變得太安於現狀。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, generally speaking, I think competition can be a good thing,
because it pushes people to improve and stops them from becoming too
comfortable.
[是的，一般來說，我認為競爭可以是一件好事，因為它會推動人進步，也能防止人變得太
安於現狀。]

components.english: When people know they are being compared with others, they often work harder, think more creatively, and raise their game. In that sense, competition can act like a spark: it wakes people up and gives them a reason to sharpen their skills. Whether in school, sport, or business, a bit of healthy rivalry can bring out focus, ambition, and real growth.
components.chinese: 當人們知道自己會被拿來和別人比較時，他們往往會更努力、更有創意，也更願意提升自己的表現。從這個角度來看，競爭有點像一束火花：它會把人喚醒，並給人一個磨練能力的理由。無論是在學校、運動，還是商業世界裡，適度而健康的競爭都能激發專注、上進心，以及真正的成長。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When people know they are being compared with others, they
often work harder, think more creatively, and raise their game. In that sense,
competition can act like a spark: it wakes people up and gives them a reason to
sharpen their skills. Whether in school, sport, or business, a bit of healthy
rivalry can bring out focus, ambition, and real growth.
[當人們知道自己會被拿來和別人比較時，他們往往會更努力、更有創意，也更願意提升自
己的表現。從這個角度來看，競爭有點像一束火花：它會把人喚醒，並給人一個磨練能力的
理由。無論是在學校、運動，還是商業世界裡，適度而健康的競爭都能激發專注、上進心，以
及真正的成長。]

components.english: For example, in a classroom, if students know there is a scholarship or a top ranking to aim for, they may study more seriously and manage their time better. In the same way, in sport, strong opponents often push athletes to train harder than they otherwise would.
components.chinese: 例如，在課室裡，如果學生知道自己有獎學金或者名次可以爭取，他們可能就會更認真讀書，也更懂得管理時間。同樣地，在體育比賽中，強勁的對手往往會迫使運動員比平時更努力地訓練。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in a classroom, if students know there is a scholarship
or a top ranking to aim for, they may study more seriously and manage their
time better. In the same way, in sport, strong opponents often push athletes to
train harder than they otherwise would.
[例如，在課室裡，如果學生知道自己有獎學金或者名次可以爭取，他們可能就會更認真讀
書，也更懂得管理時間。同樣地，在體育比賽中，強勁的對手往往會迫使運動員比平時更努
力地訓練。]

components.english: So yes, I would say competition is often beneficial, because it can bring out the best in people when it is handled in the right spirit.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說競爭很多時候是有好處的，因為只要方向正確，它的確能把人最好的一面激發出來。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say competition is often beneficial, because it can
bring out the best in people when it is handled in the right spirit.
[所以，是的，我會說競爭很多時候是有好處的，因為只要方向正確，它的確能把人最好的
一面激發出來。]

components.english: That said, competition can also become harmful if it turns toxic or excessive.
components.chinese: 不過，如果競爭變得有毒，或者過度激烈，它也可能帶來傷害。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, competition can also become harmful if it turns toxic or
excessive.
[不過，如果競爭變得有毒，或者過度激烈，它也可能帶來傷害。]

components.english: If people become obsessed with winning at all costs, competition stops being a ladder and starts becoming a trap. Instead of learning and improving, they may compare themselves constantly, feel burnt out, or even cut corners just to stay ahead. In other words, competition is a double-edged sword: it can lift people up, but it can also wear them down if it goes too far.
components.chinese: 如果人們變得只在乎不惜一切去贏，那麼競爭就不再是一道讓人向上爬的梯子，反而會變成一個陷阱。人們不但不會專注於學習和進步，反而可能會不停和別人比較、感到筋疲力盡，甚至為了領先而走捷徑。換句話說，競爭是一把雙刃劍：它可以把人推高，但如果走得太遠，也可能把人磨得很累。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If people become obsessed with winning at all costs,
competition stops being a ladder and starts becoming a trap. Instead of learning
and improving, they may compare themselves constantly, feel burnt out, or even
cut corners just to stay ahead. In other words, competition is a double-edged
sword: it can lift people up, but it can also wear them down if it goes too far.
[如果人們變得只在乎不惜一切去贏，那麼競爭就不再是一道讓人向上爬的梯子，反而會變
成一個陷阱。人們不但不會專注於學習和進步，反而可能會不停和別人比較、感到筋疲力盡
，甚至為了領先而走捷徑。換句話說，競爭是一把雙刃劍：它可以把人推高，但如果走得太
遠，也可能把人磨得很累。]

components.english: For instance, in some schools or workplaces, people are put under so much pressure to come first that they lose sleep, lose confidence, and lose sight of why they started in the first place. At that point, competition is no longer healthy; it is simply draining.
components.chinese: 例如，在某些學校或工作環境裡，人們承受著必須拿第一的巨大壓力，結果失去睡眠、失去自信，甚至忘記自己當初為甚麼出發。到了那個地步，競爭就不再健康，而只是單純地消耗人。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, in some schools or workplaces, people are put under so
much pressure to come first that they lose sleep, lose confidence, and lose sight
of why they started in the first place. At that point, competition is no longer
healthy; it is simply draining.
[例如，在某些學校或工作環境裡，人們承受著必須拿第一的巨大壓力，結果失去睡眠、失
去自信，甚至忘記自己當初為甚麼出發。到了那個地步，競爭就不再健康，而只是單純地消
耗人。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say competition is good in moderation, because it can motivate people and help them grow. The key is to keep it healthy, fair, and balanced, so that it pushes people forward rather than breaking them down.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說競爭在適度的情況下是好的，因為它能激勵人，也能幫助人成長。關鍵在於把它保持在健康、公平而平衡的狀態，讓它成為推人向前的力量，而不是把人壓垮的東西。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say competition is good in moderation, because it
can motivate people and help them grow. The key is to keep it healthy, fair, and
balanced, so that it pushes people forward rather than breaking them down.
[總結來說，我會說競爭在適度的情況下是好的，因為它能激勵人，也能幫助人成長。關鍵
在於把它保持在健康、公平而平衡的狀態，讓它成為推人向前的力量，而不是把人壓垮的東

question.english: What are the most common resolutions in your country?
question.chinese: 在你的地方，人們最常見的新年目標是甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the most common resolutions in your country?
在你的地方，人們最常見的新年目標是甚麼？

components.english: In a place like Hong Kong, I’d say the most common resolutions are usually to get healthier, save more money, and manage life a bit better overall.
components.chinese: 在像香港這樣的地方，我會說最常見的新年目標通常都是讓自己更健康、存更多錢，以及把生活整體上管理得更好一點。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In a place like Hong Kong, I’d say the most common resolutions are
usually to get healthier, save more money, and manage life a bit better overall.
[在像香港這樣的地方，我會說最常見的新年目標通常都是讓自己更健康、存更多錢，以及
把生活整體上管理得更好一點。]

components.english: The reason is quite straightforward: a lot of people live busy, stressful, and fast-paced lives, so when a new year begins, they want to turn over a new leaf. Health-related goals, such as exercising more, eating more carefully, or sleeping earlier, are extremely common. At the same time, because the cost of living can be high, many people also tell themselves that they should cut back on unnecessary spending and save more. In other words, a lot of resolutions are really about getting life back on track.
components.chinese: 原因其實很直接：很多人過著忙碌、壓力大，而且節奏很快的生活，所以當新一年開始時，他們自然會想重新開始、重新調整自己。和健康有關的目標，例如多做運動、吃得更小心，或者早點睡，都是非常常見的。同時，由於生活成本可能相當高，不少人也會提醒自己要減少不必要的開支，存更多錢。換句話說，很多新年目標其實都是在想辦法把生活重新拉回正軌。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is quite straightforward: a lot of people live busy,
stressful, and fast-paced lives, so when a new year begins, they want to turn over
a new leaf. Health-related goals, such as exercising more, eating more carefully,
or sleeping earlier, are extremely common. At the same time, because the cost of
living can be high, many people also tell themselves that they should cut back on
unnecessary spending and save more. In other words, a lot of resolutions are
really about getting life back on track.
[原因其實很直接：很多人過著忙碌、壓力大，而且節奏很快的生活，所以當新一年開始時，
他們自然會想重新開始、重新調整自己。和健康有關的目標，例如多做運動、吃得更小心，
或者早點睡，都是非常常見的。同時，由於生活成本可能相當高，不少人也會提醒自己要減
少不必要的開支，存更多錢。換句話說，很多新年目標其實都是在想辦法把生活重新拉回正

components.english: For example, someone may join a gym in January, promise to stop drinking so many sugary drinks, or start tracking their expenses instead of buying things on impulse. Those are the kind of resolutions people make almost every year, even if some of them do not last very long.
components.chinese: 例如，有些人可能會在一月去報健身室、下決心少喝含糖飲品，或者開始記錄自己的開支，而不是一時衝動就買東西。這些都是人們幾乎每年都會立下的目標，雖然其中有些目標未必能維持很久。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone may join a gym in January, promise to stop

必能維持很久。]

components.english: So, if I had to sum it up, the most common resolutions are usually about health, money, and self-discipline.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要我總結一下，我會說最常見的新年目標通常都圍繞著健康、金錢，以及自律。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to sum it up, the most common resolutions are
usually about health, money, and self-discipline.
[所以，如果要我總結一下，我會說最常見的新年目標通常都圍繞著健康、金錢，以及自

components.english: On top of that, quite a few people also make resolutions about personal growth and relationships.
components.chinese: 除此之外，也有不少人會定下一些和個人成長以及人際關係有關的新年目標。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, quite a few people also make resolutions about
personal growth and relationships.
[除此之外，也有不少人會定下一些和個人成長以及人際關係有關的新年目標。]

components.english: I think that is because people do not just want to look better or earn more; they also want to feel that they are moving forward in life. So some people decide to learn English, pick up a new skill, read more, or spend more time with family instead of being swallowed up by work all the time. In a busy city, that kind of balance can feel like gold dust.
components.chinese: 我認為這是因為，人們不只是想讓自己看起來更好，或者賺更多錢；他們也希望感覺到自己的人生是在向前走。所以，有些人會決定學英文、培養新技能、多看書，或者花更多時間陪家人，而不是整天都被工作吞沒。在一個忙碌的城市裡，這種平衡有時真的珍貴得像黃金一樣。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: I think that is because people do not just want to look better
or earn more; they also want to feel that they are moving forward in life. So
some people decide to learn English, pick up a new skill, read more, or spend
more time with family instead of being swallowed up by work all the time. In a
busy city, that kind of balance can feel like gold dust.
