一樣。]

components.english: For instance, a young employee might say they want to stop working late every night, take a course after work, or set aside one day a week for family time. Those goals may sound simple, but they reflect what many people are quietly hoping for: a healthier and more meaningful routine.
components.chinese: 例如，一位年輕上班族可能會說，他希望自己不要每晚都工作到很晚、下班後去讀一個課程，或者每星期預留一天陪家人。這些目標聽起來也許很簡單，但它們其實反映了很多人內心真正渴望的東西：一種更健康、也更有意義的生活節奏。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a young employee might say they want to stop
working late every night, take a course after work, or set aside one day a week
for family time. Those goals may sound simple, but they reflect what many
people are quietly hoping for: a healthier and more meaningful routine.

心真正渴望的東西：一種更健康、也更有意義的生活節奏。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say the most common resolutions in my place are to live more healthily, save more wisely, and improve life in some way. The details may vary from person to person, but the common thread is that people want to feel healthier, more secure, and a little more in control of their lives.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說在我的地方，最常見的新年目標就是活得更健康、花錢更有分寸，以及在某方面把生活過得更好。雖然每個人的具體內容都不一樣，但共同主線其實很清楚：人們都希望自己更健康、更有安全感，也對自己的人生多一點掌控。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say the most common resolutions in my place are to
live more healthily, save more wisely, and improve life in some way. The details
may vary from person to person, but the common thread is that people want to
feel healthier, more secure, and a little more in control of their lives.
[總結來說，我會說在我的地方，最常見的新年目標就是活得更健康、花錢更有分寸，以及
在某方面把生活過得更好。雖然每個人的具體內容都不一樣，但共同主線其實很清楚：人們
都希望自己更健康、更有安全感，也對自己的人生多一點掌控。]

question.english: How difficult would it be for you to save money this year?
question.chinese: 對你來說，今年要存錢會有多困難？

source_lines.text: Topic: How difficult would it be for you to save money this year?
對你來說，今年要存錢會有多困難？

components.english: I’d say it would be fairly difficult, though not impossible, mainly because the cost of living is high and money can slip through your fingers before you even notice it.
components.chinese: 我會說，這會是相當困難的，但也不是不可能，主要因為生活成本很高，而且錢很多時候還沒察覺就已經從手指縫裡溜走了。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say it would be fairly difficult, though not impossible, mainly because
the cost of living is high and money can slip through your fingers before you
even notice it.
[我會說，這會是相當困難的，但也不是不可能，主要因為生活成本很高，而且錢很多時候
還沒察覺就已經從手指縫裡溜走了。]

components.english: The main issue is that everyday expenses have a way of piling up. There is rent, transport, food, bills, and all the little extras that do not look serious on their own but add up surprisingly fast. In a year like this, saving money can feel a bit like swimming against the current. You are moving forward, but not without effort.
components.chinese: 主要問題是，日常開支很容易越積越多。有租金、交通、食物、帳單，還有各種看起來不算嚴重、但加起來卻快得驚人的零碎開支。在這樣的一年裡，存錢有時真的像逆流而上一樣。你不是不能前進，只是絕對不輕鬆。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main issue is that everyday expenses have a way of piling up.
There is rent, transport, food, bills, and all the little extras that do not look
serious on their own but add up surprisingly fast. In a year like this, saving
money can feel a bit like swimming against the current. You are moving
forward, but not without effort.
[主要問題是，日常開支很容易越積越多。有租金、交通、食物、帳單，還有各種看起來不算
嚴重、但加起來卻快得驚人的零碎開支。在這樣的一年裡，存錢有時真的像逆流而上一樣。
你不是不能前進，只是絕對不輕鬆。]

components.english: For example, even if I start the month with good intentions, one or two meals out, an unexpected purchase, a birthday gift, or a small emergency can eat into my budget quite quickly. Before long, the money I meant to save has been chipped away little by little.
components.chinese: 例如，即使我在月初是抱著很好的打算開始，一兩次外出吃飯、一件突如其來要買的東西、一份生日禮物，或者一個小小的突發開支，都可能很快侵蝕我的預算。沒多久，我原本打算存下來的錢，就會一點一點被削走。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, even if I start the month with good intentions, one or
two meals out, an unexpected purchase, a birthday gift, or a small emergency
can eat into my budget quite quickly. Before long, the money I meant to save has
been chipped away little by little.
[例如，即使我在月初是抱著很好的打算開始，一兩次外出吃飯、一件突如其來要買的東
西、一份生日禮物，或者一個小小的突發開支，都可能很快侵蝕我的預算。沒多久，我原本
打算存下來的錢，就會一點一點被削走。]

components.english: So, in realistic terms, I think saving money this year would take real discipline rather than just good intentions.
components.chinese: 所以，從現實角度來說，我認為今年要存到錢，靠的不會只是好意願，而是真正的自律。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in realistic terms, I think saving money this year would take
real discipline rather than just good intentions.
[所以，從現實角度來說，我認為今年要存到錢，靠的不會只是好意願，而是真正的自律。]

components.english: That said, I do think it would still be manageable if I planned carefully and treated saving as a priority rather than an afterthought.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為，只要我好好規劃，並把存錢當成優先事項而不是事後才想起的事，它仍然是可以做到的。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think it would still be manageable if I planned
carefully and treated saving as a priority rather than an afterthought.
[不過，我也認為，只要我好好規劃，並把存錢當成優先事項而不是事後才想起的事，它仍
然是可以做到的。]

components.english: To me, the key would be to make saving automatic and realistic. If I set aside a fixed amount as soon as I get paid, I would be much less likely to spend it on impulse. On top of that, small habits can go a long way, such as cutting back on unnecessary shopping, cooking more at home, or keeping an eye on where my money actually goes. In other words, I would need to mind the pennies if I wanted the pounds to look after themselves.
components.chinese: 對我來說，關鍵會在於讓存錢變得自動而且現實。如果我一出糧就先撥出一個固定金額，我就比較不容易因一時衝動把它花掉。除此之外，一些小習慣其實也很有幫助，例如減少不必要購物、多在家裡煮食，或者認真留意自己的錢究竟花到哪裡去了。換句話說，如果我希望大數目能照顧好自己，我就得先把小數目看緊。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To me, the key would be to make saving automatic and
realistic. If I set aside a fixed amount as soon as I get paid, I would be much less
likely to spend it on impulse. On top of that, small habits can go a long way,
such as cutting back on unnecessary shopping, cooking more at home, or
keeping an eye on where my money actually goes. In other words, I would need
to mind the pennies if I wanted the pounds to look after themselves.
[對我來說，關鍵會在於讓存錢變得自動而且現實。如果我一出糧就先撥出一個固定金額，
我就比較不容易因一時衝動把它花掉。除此之外，一些小習慣其實也很有幫助，例如減少不
必要購物、多在家裡煮食，或者認真留意自己的錢究竟花到哪裡去了。換句話說，如果我希
望大數目能照顧好自己，我就得先把小數目看緊。]

components.english: For instance, if I transfer part of my salary into a savings account straight away and set myself a modest monthly goal, saving would probably feel less overwhelming. It would not be a dramatic transformation overnight, but it would at least put me on the right track.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我一出糧就立刻把部分薪金轉到儲蓄戶口，並且替自己設定一個不算太激進的每月目標，那麼存錢這件事大概就不會顯得那麼令人吃不消。它不會一夜之間帶來戲劇性的改變，但至少能讓我走在正確的方向上。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if I transfer part of my salary into a savings account
straight away and set myself a modest monthly goal, saving would probably feel
less overwhelming. It would not be a dramatic transformation overnight, but it
would at least put me on the right track.
[例如，如果我一出糧就立刻把部分薪金轉到儲蓄戶口，並且替自己設定一個不算太激進的
每月目標，那麼存錢這件事大概就不會顯得那麼令人吃不消。它不會一夜之間帶來戲劇性
的改變，但至少能讓我走在正確的方向上。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say saving money this year would be challenging, but not out of reach. It would require consistency, restraint, and a bit of financial common sense, but if I kept my eye on the ball, I think I could make it work.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說今年存錢會有挑戰，但也不是遙不可及。它需要持續性、克制力，以及一點理財常識，不過只要我一直把焦點放對位置，我認為自己還是做得到的。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say saving money this year would be challenging,
but not out of reach. It would require consistency, restraint, and a bit of
financial common sense, but if I kept my eye on the ball, I think I could make it
work.
[總結來說，我會說今年存錢會有挑戰，但也不是遙不可及。它需要持續性、克制力，以及一
點理財常識，不過只要我一直把焦點放對位置，我認為自己還是做得到的。]

question.english: How difficult would it be for you to get fit this year?
question.chinese: 你覺得自己今年要變得更健康、更有體能，會有多困難？

source_lines.text: Topic: How difficult would it be for you to get fit this year?
你覺得自己今年要變得更健康、更有體能，會有多困難？

components.english: To be honest, I’d say it would be quite challenging for me, although not impossible.
components.chinese: 老實說，我會說這對我來說會有點困難，不過也不是做不到。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: To be honest, I’d say it would be quite challenging for me, although not
impossible.
[老實說，我會說這對我來說會有點困難，不過也不是做不到。]

components.english: The main reason is that getting fit is usually less about knowing what to do and more about doing it consistently. Most people already know the basics: exercise regularly, eat more carefully, and get enough sleep. The hard part is sticking to that when life gets busy. When work, study, or family responsibilities pile up, fitness can easily slide down the list, and before you know it, one missed workout turns into a whole week of excuses.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，變得健康有體能，很多時候不是因為不知道該做甚麼，而是因為很難持之以恆。大多數人其實都知道基本原則：規律運動、飲食小心一點、睡眠充足。真正困難的，是當生活一忙起來，還能不能一直做下去。當工作、學業，或者家庭責任一層一層壓上來時，健身這件事就很容易被放到後面，然後不知不覺間，一次沒做運動，就變成整整一星期都在找藉口。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that getting fit is usually less about knowing
what to do and more about doing it consistently. Most people already know the
basics: exercise regularly, eat more carefully, and get enough sleep. The hard part
is sticking to that when life gets busy. When work, study, or family
responsibilities pile up, fitness can easily slide down the list, and before you
know it, one missed workout turns into a whole week of excuses.
[主要原因是，變得健康有體能，很多時候不是因為不知道該做甚麼，而是因為很難持之以
恆。大多數人其實都知道基本原則：規律運動、飲食小心一點、睡眠充足。真正困難的，是當
生活一忙起來，還能不能一直做下去。當工作、學業，或者家庭責任一層一層壓上來時，健
身這件事就很容易被放到後面，然後不知不覺間，一次沒做運動，就變成整整一星期都在找
藉口。]

components.english: For example, after a long day, it is very easy to tell yourself that you are too tired to exercise and that you will start tomorrow instead. Then tomorrow comes, and the same thing happens again. It can become a slippery slope if you are not careful.
components.chinese: 例如，忙了一整天之後，人很容易對自己說「今天太累了，明天再開始吧」。可是到了明天，同樣的情況又再發生。如果不小心，這種狀態其實很容易一路滑下去。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, after a long day, it is very easy to tell yourself that you
are too tired to exercise and that you will start tomorrow instead. Then
tomorrow comes, and the same thing happens again. It can become a slippery
slope if you are not careful.
[例如，忙了一整天之後，人很容易對自己說「今天太累了，明天再開始吧」。可是到了明天，
同樣的情況又再發生。如果不小心，這種狀態其實很容易一路滑下去。]

components.english: So, I’d say the difficulty would lie mainly in staying disciplined and building a routine, rather than in fitness itself.
components.chinese: 所以，我會說真正困難的地方，主要不在於健身本身，而在於能不能保持自律，並且建立穩定的習慣。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, I’d say the difficulty would lie mainly in staying disciplined
and building a routine, rather than in fitness itself.
[所以，我會說真正困難的地方，主要不在於健身本身，而在於能不能保持自律，並且建立
穩定的習慣。]

components.english: That said, I also think it would be manageable if I took a realistic and steady approach instead of trying to change everything overnight.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為，如果我採取一種務實而穩定的方法，而不是一夜之間把所有事情都改掉，那這件事其實是可以做到的。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think it would be manageable if I took a realistic and
steady approach instead of trying to change everything overnight.
[不過，我也認為，如果我採取一種務實而穩定的方法，而不是一夜之間把所有事情都改掉
，那這件事其實是可以做到的。]

components.english: In my view, getting fit does not have to mean living in the gym or turning life upside down. It can start with smaller habits, such as walking more, eating a bit more cleanly, cutting back on junk food, and exercising a few times a week. The more realistic the plan is, the more likely it is to stick. In that sense, getting fit is a bit like steering a ship: even a small change in direction can take you somewhere much better over time.
components.chinese: 在我看來，變得健康有體能，並不代表一定要整天泡在健身室裡，或者把整個生活徹底翻過來。它其實可以從一些較小的習慣開始，例如多走路、吃得乾淨一點、少吃垃圾食物，以及每星期做幾次運動。計劃越現實，就越有可能持續下去。從這個角度來看，健身有點像在調整一艘船的方向：哪怕只是小小地轉一點，時間久了，也能把你帶到更好的地方。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In my view, getting fit does not have to mean living in the gym
or turning life upside down. It can start with smaller habits, such as walking
more, eating a bit more cleanly, cutting back on junk food, and exercising a few
times a week. The more realistic the plan is, the more likely it is to stick. In that
sense, getting fit is a bit like steering a ship: even a small change in direction can
take you somewhere much better over time.
[在我看來，變得健康有體能，並不代表一定要整天泡在健身室裡，或者把整個生活徹底翻
過來。它其實可以從一些較小的習慣開始，例如多走路、吃得乾淨一點、少吃垃圾食物，以
及每星期做幾次運動。計劃越現實，就越有可能持續下去。從這個角度來看，健身有點像在
調整一艘船的方向：哪怕只是小小地轉一點，時間久了，也能把你帶到更好的地方。]

components.english: For instance, if I started by going for a thirty-minute walk most days, doing a couple of home workouts each week, and cutting down on sugary drinks, I think I would already be moving in the right direction. It would not be dramatic, but it would be real progress.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我先從大多數日子都走三十分鐘路、每星期做幾次居家運動，以及減少喝含糖飲品開始，我覺得自己其實已經是在朝正確方向前進了。那種改變未必很戲劇化，但卻會是真正的進步。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if I started by going for a thirty-minute walk most
days, doing a couple of home workouts each week, and cutting down on sugary
drinks, I think I would already be moving in the right direction. It would not
be dramatic, but it would be real progress.
[例如，如果我先從大多數日子都走三十分鐘路、每星期做幾次居家運動，以及減少喝含糖
飲品開始，我覺得自己其實已經是在朝正確方向前進了。那種改變未必很戲劇化，但卻會是
真正的進步。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say it would be challenging but definitely doable. It would not exactly be a walk in the park, but it would not be like climbing a mountain either. As long as I stayed consistent, I think I could get noticeably fitter by the end of the year.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說這件事會有挑戰，但絕對做得到。它不算是毫不費力的事，但也不至於難如登天。只要我能保持穩定，我覺得到了今年年底，自己的狀態應該可以明顯變得更好。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say it would be challenging but definitely doable. It
would not exactly be a walk in the park, but it would not be like climbing a
mountain either. As long as I stayed consistent, I think I could get noticeably
fitter by the end of the year.
[總結來說，我會說這件事會有挑戰，但絕對做得到。它不算是毫不費力的事，但也不至於
難如登天。只要我能保持穩定，我覺得到了今年年底，自己的狀態應該可以明顯變得更

question.english: What do you think this year will be like for you?
question.chinese: 你覺得今年對你來說會是怎樣的一年？

source_lines.text: Topic: What do you think this year will be like for you?
[你覺得今年對你來說會是怎樣的一年？]

components.english: Personally, I think this year will be busy but rewarding for me, and I see it as a year of steady progress rather than dramatic change.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我覺得今年對我來說會是忙碌但有收穫的一年，而且我會把它看成是一段穩步前進的年份，而不是充滿戲劇性大轉變的一年。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think this year will be busy but rewarding for me, and I see
it as a year of steady progress rather than dramatic change.
[就我個人而言，我覺得今年對我來說會是忙碌但有收穫的一年，而且我會把它看成是一段
穩步前進的年份，而不是充滿戲劇性大轉變的一年。]

components.english: The main reason is that I have quite a few things I want to work on, such as improving my skills, becoming more disciplined, and managing my time better. I do not expect everything to change overnight, but I do feel that this could be a year in which I gradually get my act together and move in the right direction. In that sense, I think it will be more about building momentum than chasing instant results.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，我今年有不少想努力的事情，例如提升自己的能力、讓自己更有紀律，以及更好地管理時間。我並不期待所有事情一夜之間都改變，但我確實覺得，這可能會是我慢慢把生活整理好，並朝正確方向走的一年。從這個角度來看，我覺得今年更像是在建立動能，而不是急著追求立刻見效的結果。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that I have quite a few things I want to work
on, such as improving my skills, becoming more disciplined, and managing my
time better. I do not expect everything to change overnight, but I do feel that
this could be a year in which I gradually get my act together and move in the
right direction. In that sense, I think it will be more about building momentum
than chasing instant results.
[主要原因是，我今年有不少想努力的事情，例如提升自己的能力、讓自己更有紀律，以及
更好地管理時間。我並不期待所有事情一夜之間都改變，但我確實覺得，這可能會是我慢慢
把生活整理好，並朝正確方向走的一年。從這個角度來看，我覺得今年更像是在建立動能，
而不是急著追求立刻見效的結果。]

components.english: For example, I would like to keep improving my English, stay more consistent with my daily routine, and make better use of my time instead of letting the days slip through my fingers. If I can do those things well, even in small steps, I think I will feel much more satisfied by the end of the year.
components.chinese: 例如，我希望自己能持續提升英語、在日常生活中保持更穩定的節奏，並且更好地運用時間，而不是讓日子不知不覺地從指縫間溜走。如果我能把這些事情做好，哪怕只是以很小的步伐前進，我想到了年底時，我都會感到更滿足。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, I would like to keep improving my English, stay more
consistent with my daily routine, and make better use of my time instead of
letting the days slip through my fingers. If I can do those things well, even in
small steps, I think I will feel much more satisfied by the end of the year.
[例如，我希望自己能持續提升英語、在日常生活中保持更穩定的節奏，並且更好地運用時
間，而不是讓日子不知不覺地從指縫間溜走。如果我能把這些事情做好，哪怕只是以很小的
步伐前進，我想到了年底時，我都會感到更滿足。]

components.english: So, overall, I think this year will be a productive one for me, mainly because I want it to be shaped by growth, not just by busyness.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我覺得今年對我來說會是相當充實而有成果的一年，主要因為我希望它是由成長來定義，而不只是由忙碌來填滿。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I think this year will be a productive one for me,
mainly because I want it to be shaped by growth, not just by busyness.
[所以，整體來說，我覺得今年對我來說會是相當充實而有成果的一年，主要因為我希望它
是由成長來定義，而不只是由忙碌來填滿。]

components.english: That said, I do not think it will be an easy or perfectly smooth year, because life usually throws a few curveballs when you least expect them.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不覺得今年會是一帆風順、毫無波折的一年，因為人生往往會在你最意想不到的時候丟來幾個變數。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think it will be an easy or perfectly smooth year,
because life usually throws a few curveballs when you least expect them.
[不過，我並不覺得今年會是一帆風順、毫無波折的一年，因為人生往往會在你最意想不到
的時候丟來幾個變數。]

components.english: There will probably be moments when I feel tired, under pressure, or unsure of myself, especially when different responsibilities pile up at the same time. However, I think that is part of the process. A year does not have to be easy to be meaningful. Sometimes the difficult parts are exactly what sharpen you, steady you, and teach you what you are made of.
components.chinese: 我大概也會有一些時候感到疲累、有壓力，或者對自己不太確定，尤其是當不同責任同時堆上來的時候。不過，我覺得那也是過程的一部分。一年未必要輕鬆，才算有意義。有時候，正正是那些比較難熬的部分，才會磨練你、令你站穩，也讓你更知道自己是由甚麼做成的。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: There will probably be moments when I feel tired, under
pressure, or unsure of myself, especially when different responsibilities pile up
at the same time. However, I think that is part of the process. A year does not
have to be easy to be meaningful. Sometimes the difficult parts are exactly what
sharpen you, steady you, and teach you what you are made of.
[我大概也會有一些時候感到疲累、有壓力，或者對自己不太確定，尤其是當不同責任同時
堆上來的時候。不過，我覺得那也是過程的一部分。一年未必要輕鬆，才算有意義。有時候，
正正是那些比較難熬的部分，才會磨練你、令你站穩，也讓你更知道自己是由甚麼做成

components.english: For instance, if I face setbacks in study, work, or personal plans, I may feel discouraged at first. But I hope I will learn to take a step back, adjust my pace, and keep going instead of falling apart over every small setback. That, to me, would already count as real progress.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我在學業、工作，或者個人計劃上遇到挫折，我一開始可能也會感到灰心。不過，我希望自己能學會先退一步、調整節奏，然後繼續走下去，而不是被每一個小小的失誤打垮。對我來說，這本身就已經算是真正的進步。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if I face setbacks in study, work, or personal plans, I
may feel discouraged at first. But I hope I will learn to take a step back, adjust
my pace, and keep going instead of falling apart over every small setback. That,
to me, would already count as real progress.
[例如，如果我在學業、工作，或者個人計劃上遇到挫折，我一開始可能也會感到灰心。不過
，我希望自己能學會先退一步、調整節奏，然後繼續走下去，而不是被每一個小小的失誤打
垮。對我來說，這本身就已經算是真正的進步。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say this year will probably be challenging, stretching, and hopefully fulfilling. If I can stay grounded and keep moving forward, I think it could turn out to be one of those quiet years that changes you more than you realise at the time.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說今年大概會是一個有挑戰、有拉扯，但也希望能帶來滿足感的一年。如果我能保持踏實，並且一直向前走，我覺得它很可能會變成那種表面上安靜、但回頭看時才發現其實改變了你很多的一年。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say this year will probably be challenging,
stretching, and hopefully fulfilling. If I can stay grounded and keep moving
forward, I think it could turn out to be one of those quiet years that changes
you more than you realise at the time.
[總結來說，我會說今年大概會是一個有挑戰、有拉扯，但也希望能帶來滿足感的一年。如
果我能保持踏實，並且一直向前走，我覺得它很可能會變成那種表面上安靜、但回頭看時才
發現其實改變了你很多的一年。]

question.english: Do you personally think that resolutions help us achieve our goals?
question.chinese: 你個人認為，決心能幫助我們達成目標嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you personally think that resolutions help us achieve our goals?
你個人認為，決心能幫助我們達成目標嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do, at least to a certain extent, because resolutions can give people a clear sense of direction and a reason to start.
components.chinese: 是的，我認為可以，至少在某程度上是這樣，因為決心能給人一個清楚的方向，也給人一個開始行動的理由。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do, at least to a certain extent, because resolutions can give people
a clear sense of direction and a reason to start.
[是的，我認為可以，至少在某程度上是這樣，因為決心能給人一個清楚的方向，也給人一
個開始行動的理由。]

components.english: I think a resolution can be useful because it draws a line in the sand and says, “Right, this is what I want to change.” A lot of people drift when their goals stay vague, but once they make a clear resolution, the goal stops being just a passing thought and starts to feel more real. In that sense, a resolution is a bit like a compass: it does not walk the road for you, but it helps you stop wandering.
components.chinese: 我覺得，決心之所以有用，是因為它像是在地上劃出一條界線，告訴自己：「好，這就是我想改變的事情。」很多人之所以原地打轉，是因為目標一直都很模糊；但一旦他們立下明確的決心，那個目標就不再只是腦海中一閃而過的念頭，而會開始變得更真實。從這個角度來說，決心有點像指南針：它不會替你走路，但它能讓你不再迷失方向。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: I think a resolution can be useful because it draws a line in the
sand and says, “Right, this is what I want to change.” A lot of people drift when
their goals stay vague, but once they make a clear resolution, the goal stops
being just a passing thought and starts to feel more real. In that sense, a
resolution is a bit like a compass: it does not walk the road for you, but it helps
you stop wandering.
[我覺得，決心之所以有用，是因為它像是在地上劃出一條界線，告訴自己：「好，這就是我
想改變的事情。」很多人之所以原地打轉，是因為目標一直都很模糊；但一旦他們立下明確
的決心，那個目標就不再只是腦海中一閃而過的念頭，而會開始變得更真實。從這個角度來
說，決心有點像指南針：它不會替你走路，但它能讓你不再迷失方向。]

components.english: For example, if someone decides they want to get fitter and makes a resolution to exercise three times a week, that gives them something concrete to aim for. It is much better than simply saying, “I should probably be healthier one day,” and then leaving it up in the air.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人決定自己想變得更健康，並且立下每星期運動三次的決心，那他就有了一個具體可以努力的方向。這明顯比只說一句「我某天應該要健康一點」，然後把事情一直懸在半空中要好得多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone decides they want to get fitter and makes a
resolution to exercise three times a week, that gives them something concrete to
aim for. It is much better than simply saying, “I should probably be healthier
one day,” and then leaving it up in the air.
[例如，如果一個人決定自己想變得更健康，並且立下每星期運動三次的決心，那他就有了
一個具體可以努力的方向。這明顯比只說一句「我某天應該要健康一點」，然後把事情一直
懸在半空中要好得多。]

components.english: So yes, I would say resolutions can help, mainly because they turn vague wishes into clearer intentions.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說決心是有幫助的，主要因為它能把模糊的願望變成較清楚的目標意圖。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say resolutions can help, mainly because they turn
vague wishes into clearer intentions.
[所以，是的，我會說決心是有幫助的，主要因為它能把模糊的願望變成較清楚的目標意

components.english: That said, I do not think resolutions are enough on their own, because a good intention means very little if it is not backed up by action.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為單靠決心本身就足夠，因為如果沒有實際行動支持，再好的意圖也沒有太大作用。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think resolutions are enough on their own, because
a good intention means very little if it is not backed up by action.
[不過，我也不認為單靠決心本身就足夠，因為如果沒有實際行動支持，再好的意圖也沒有
太大作用。]

components.english: To be honest, many resolutions sound great in January and then quietly fade away by February. The problem is that people often make them too big, too vague, or too idealistic. A resolution can light the spark, but daily habits keep the fire burning. If someone does not build a routine, track their progress, or keep going when motivation dips, the resolution may end up being all talk and no follow-through.
components.chinese: 老實說，很多決心在一月的時候聽起來都很好，但到了二月就悄悄地淡掉了。問題在於，人們常常把決心訂得太大、太模糊，或者太理想化。決心可以點起火花，但真正讓火持續燃燒的，是每天的習慣。如果一個人沒有建立規律、沒有追蹤進度，或者在動力下降時仍然堅持下去，那麼那個決心最後就很可能只剩下口號，而沒有真正落實。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, many resolutions sound great in January and
then quietly fade away by February. The problem is that people often make them
too big, too vague, or too idealistic. A resolution can light the spark, but daily
habits keep the fire burning. If someone does not build a routine, track their
progress, or keep going when motivation dips, the resolution may end up being
all talk and no follow-through.
[老實說，很多決心在一月的時候聽起來都很好，但到了二月就悄悄地淡掉了。問題在於，
人們常常把決心訂得太大、太模糊，或者太理想化。決心可以點起火花，但真正讓火持續燃
燒的，是每天的習慣。如果一個人沒有建立規律、沒有追蹤進度，或者在動力下降時仍然堅
持下去，那麼那個決心最後就很可能只剩下口號，而沒有真正落實。]

components.english: For instance, someone may make a New Year’s resolution to save money, but if they keep spending impulsively and never set a budget, that resolution will fall flat. By contrast, if they save a small amount every week and stick to it, the goal suddenly becomes much more achievable.
components.chinese: 例如，有些人可能會立下新年決心，要開始儲錢；但如果他仍然不停衝動消費，而且從來沒有訂下預算，那個決心很快就會落空。相反，如果他每星期固定存下一小筆錢，並且堅持下去，那個目標就會一下子變得實際得多，也更有可能達成。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, someone may make a New Year’s resolution to save
money, but if they keep spending impulsively and never set a budget, that
resolution will fall flat. By contrast, if they save a small amount every week and
stick to it, the goal suddenly becomes much more achievable.
[例如，有些人可能會立下新年決心，要開始儲錢；但如果他仍然不停衝動消費，而且從來
沒有訂下預算，那個決心很快就會落空。相反，如果他每星期固定存下一小筆錢，並且堅持
下去，那個目標就會一下子變得實際得多，也更有可能達成。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say resolutions do help, but only when they are realistic, specific, and followed by consistent action. In other words, a resolution may open the door, but it is everyday effort that actually gets you through it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說決心的確有幫助，但前提是它必須現實、具體，而且後面真的有持續的行動去配合。換句話說，決心也許能幫你把門打開，但真正帶你走進去的，始終還是每天的努力。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say resolutions do help, but only when they are
realistic, specific, and followed by consistent action. In other words, a resolution
may open the door, but it is everyday effort that actually gets you through it.
[總結來說，我會說決心的確有幫助，但前提是它必須現實、具體，而且後面真的有持續的
行動去配合。換句話說，決心也許能幫你把門打開，但真正帶你走進去的，始終還是每天的
努力。]

question.english: What are the benefits of making New Year’s resolutions?
question.chinese: 新年訂立目標有甚麼好處？

source_lines.text: Topic: What are the benefits of making New Year’s resolutions?
新年訂立目標有甚麼好處？

components.english: I think one major benefit is that New Year’s resolutions give people a clear sense of direction.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個主要好處是，新年目標能給人一種清晰的方向感。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one major benefit is that New Year’s resolutions give people a
clear sense of direction.
[我認為，其中一個主要好處是，新年目標能給人一種清晰的方向感。]

components.english: The start of a new year often feels like a clean slate, so people are naturally more willing to reflect on their lives and make changes. Without some kind of intention, it is very easy to drift and let another year slip through your fingers. A resolution can work like a compass: it helps people decide what matters, what needs to change, and where they want to put their energy. In that sense, it turns vague hopes into something more concrete.
components.chinese: 新一年的開始往往像一張乾淨的白紙，所以人們自然會更願意反思自己的生活，並作出改變。如果沒有某種明確的意圖，人其實很容易渾渾噩噩地過日子，讓一年又從指縫間溜走。目標就像一個指南針：它能幫助人決定甚麼重要、甚麼需要改變，以及自己想把精力放在哪裡。從這個角度來看，它能把模糊的希望變成較具體的方向。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The start of a new year often feels like a clean slate, so people are
naturally more willing to reflect on their lives and make changes. Without some
kind of intention, it is very easy to drift and let another year slip through your
fingers. A resolution can work like a compass: it helps people decide what
matters, what needs to change, and where they want to put their energy. In that
sense, it turns vague hopes into something more concrete.
[新一年的開始往往像一張乾淨的白紙，所以人們自然會更願意反思自己的生活，並作出改
變。如果沒有某種明確的意圖，人其實很容易渾渾噩噩地過日子，讓一年又從指縫間溜走。
目標就像一個指南針：它能幫助人決定甚麼重要、甚麼需要改變，以及自己想把精力放在哪
裡。從這個角度來看，它能把模糊的希望變成較具體的方向。]

components.english: For example, someone may decide to exercise more, spend less money, or cut down on screen time. Even that simple act of naming the goal can make a difference, because it gives the person something to aim at instead of just saying, “I should probably do better somehow.”
components.chinese: 例如，有人可能會決定多做運動、少花點錢，或者減少看螢幕的時間。即使只是把目標清楚說出來，往往都已經能帶來改變，因為那會令一個人有一個明確可以努力的方向，而不只是含糊地說一句「我大概應該改善一下」。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone may decide to exercise more, spend less
money, or cut down on screen time. Even that simple act of naming the goal can
make a difference, because it gives the person something to aim at instead of just
saying, “I should probably do better somehow.”
[例如，有人可能會決定多做運動、少花點錢，或者減少看螢幕的時間。即使只是把目標清
楚說出來，往往都已經能帶來改變，因為那會令一個人有一個明確可以努力的方向，而不只
是含糊地說一句「我大概應該改善一下」。]

components.english: So, one clear benefit is that New Year’s resolutions help people begin the year with focus rather than with wishful thinking alone.
components.chinese: 所以，其中一個很明顯的好處，就是新年目標能讓人以專注的態度展開新一年，而不只是停留在空想。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear benefit is that New Year’s resolutions help people
begin the year with focus rather than with wishful thinking alone.
[所以，其中一個很明顯的好處，就是新年目標能讓人以專注的態度展開新一年，而不只是
停留在空想。]

components.english: Another benefit is that resolutions can build self-discipline and self-awareness over time.
components.chinese: 另一個好處是，目標能隨著時間幫助人建立自律，以及更了解自己。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another benefit is that resolutions can build self-discipline and
self-awareness over time.
[另一個好處是，目標能隨著時間幫助人建立自律，以及更了解自己。]

components.english: To be honest, people often joke that New Year’s resolutions do not last very long, and that may be true in some cases. Even so, I still think they are valuable, because they encourage people to take stock of their habits and hold themselves accountable. The point is not to transform your whole life overnight; it is to get the ball rolling. Once people start making small but consistent changes, confidence tends to grow, and progress becomes more realistic.
components.chinese: 老實說，人們常常會開玩笑說，新年目標維持不了多久，而在某些情況下，這的確是真的。即使如此，我仍然覺得它們很有價值，因為它們會推動人檢視自己的習慣，並對自己負責。重點不是要一夜之間把整個人生翻天覆地地改變，而是先讓事情開始轉動起來。一旦人開始作出一些微小但持續的改變，自信通常就會慢慢增加，而進步也會變得更實際。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, people often joke that New Year’s resolutions do
not last very long, and that may be true in some cases. Even so, I still think they
are valuable, because they encourage people to take stock of their habits and
hold themselves accountable. The point is not to transform your whole life
overnight; it is to get the ball rolling. Once people start making small but
consistent changes, confidence tends to grow, and progress becomes more
realistic.
[老實說，人們常常會開玩笑說，新年目標維持不了多久，而在某些情況下，這的確是真的。
即使如此，我仍然覺得它們很有價值，因為它們會推動人檢視自己的習慣，並對自己負責。
重點不是要一夜之間把整個人生翻天覆地地改變，而是先讓事情開始轉動起來。一旦人開
始作出一些微小但持續的改變，自信通常就會慢慢增加，而進步也會變得更實際。]

components.english: For instance, a person who sets a resolution to read more may not end up reading fifty books, but if they go from reading none to reading one book a month, that is still meaningful progress. In the same way, someone who wants to get fitter may begin with two short workouts a week, and that small routine can gradually snowball into a healthier lifestyle.
components.chinese: 例如，一個立志要多看書的人，也許最後未必真的看完五十本書；但如果他從原本一本都不看，變成每個月看一本，那其實已經是很有意義的進步。同樣地，一個想讓自己更健康的人，可能最初只是每星期做兩次短時間運動，而這樣一個小習慣，慢慢就可能滾雪球般變成更健康的生活方式。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a person who sets a resolution to read more may not
end up reading fifty books, but if they go from reading none to reading one
book a month, that is still meaningful progress. In the same way, someone who
wants to get fitter may begin with two short workouts a week, and that small
routine can gradually snowball into a healthier lifestyle.
[例如，一個立志要多看書的人，也許最後未必真的看完五十本書；但如果他從原本一本都
不看，變成每個月看一本，那其實已經是很有意義的進步。同樣地，一個想讓自己更健康的
人，可能最初只是每星期做兩次短時間運動，而這樣一個小習慣，慢慢就可能滾雪球般變成
更健康的生活方式。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say New Year’s resolutions can be very beneficial because they give people focus, motivation, and a chance to reset. The key, though, is to make them realistic, because a good resolution should be a stepping stone, not a stick to beat yourself with.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說新年目標可以帶來很大的好處，因為它能給人方向感、動力，以及一個重新調整自己的機會。不過，關鍵在於目標要訂得切合實際，因為一個好的目標應該是一塊踏腳石，而不是拿來責怪自己的工具。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say New Year’s resolutions can be very beneficial
because they give people focus, motivation, and a chance to reset. The key,
though, is to make them realistic, because a good resolution should be a stepping
stone, not a stick to beat yourself with.
[總結來說，我會說新年目標可以帶來很大的好處，因為它能給人方向感、動力，以及一個
重新調整自己的機會。不過，關鍵在於目標要訂得切合實際，因為一個好的目標應該是一塊
踏腳石，而不是拿來責怪自己的工具。]

question.english: Describe an event that you attended recently.
question.chinese: 描述一個你最近參加過的活動。

source_lines.text: Topic: Describe an event that you attended recently.
描述一個你最近參加過的活動。

components.english: One recent event I attended was a close friend’s wedding banquet, and to be honest, it was one of the warmest and most memorable events I have been to in quite a while.
components.chinese: 我最近參加過的一個活動，是一位好朋友的婚宴。老實說，那是我這段時間以來參加過最溫暖、也最令人難忘的活動之一。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: One recent event I attended was a close friend’s wedding banquet, and
to be honest, it was one of the warmest and most memorable events I have been
to in quite a while.
[我最近參加過的一個活動，是一位好朋友的婚宴。老實說，那是我這段時間以來參加過最
溫暖、也最令人難忘的活動之一。]

components.english: I think it stood out because it was more than just a formal celebration. It felt like a room full of shared memories, old friendships, and new beginnings all rolled into one. Everyone was dressed up, the atmosphere was lively, and there was that kind of excitement in the air that you could almost feel with your hands. In a way, it reminded me that some events are not just about what happens on the day, but about what they represent.
components.chinese: 我覺得這個活動之所以特別，是因為它不只是一場形式上的慶祝而已。它更像是一個把共同回憶、舊友情，以及新開始都聚在一起的場合。大家都穿得很正式，現場氣氛很熱鬧，而且空氣中有一種幾乎摸得到的興奮感。某程度上，它令我明白，有些活動的重要之處，不只是當天發生了甚麼，而是它背後代表了甚麼。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: I think it stood out because it was more than just a formal
celebration. It felt like a room full of shared memories, old friendships, and new
beginnings all rolled into one. Everyone was dressed up, the atmosphere was
lively, and there was that kind of excitement in the air that you could almost feel
with your hands. In a way, it reminded me that some events are not just about
what happens on the day, but about what they represent.
[我覺得這個活動之所以特別，是因為它不只是一場形式上的慶祝而已。它更像是一個把共
同回憶、舊友情，以及新開始都聚在一起的場合。大家都穿得很正式，現場氣氛很熱鬧，而
且空氣中有一種幾乎摸得到的興奮感。某程度上，它令我明白，有些活動的重要之處，不只
是當天發生了甚麼，而是它背後代表了甚麼。]

components.english: For example, the banquet was held in the evening at a hotel, and I went there with a few mutual friends. There was a ceremony, speeches, and the usual toasting, but what I remember most clearly was the moment when the couple walked in and the whole room burst into applause. It was simple, but it hit home. You could see how genuinely happy people were for them, and that made the whole evening feel heartfelt rather than just glamorous.
components.chinese: 例如，那場婚宴是在晚上於一間酒店舉行的，我和幾位共同朋友一起去。現場有儀式、有致辭，也有一般婚宴常見的敬酒環節，但我記得最清楚的，反而是新人進場時全場突然爆出掌聲的那一刻。那一刻其實很簡單，卻很打動人。你可以看得出大家是真心為他們高興，而這也令整個晚上感覺更真摯，而不只是表面上的華麗。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, the banquet was held in the evening at a hotel, and I
went there with a few mutual friends. There was a ceremony, speeches, and the
usual toasting, but what I remember most clearly was the moment when the
couple walked in and the whole room burst into applause. It was simple, but it
hit home. You could see how genuinely happy people were for them, and that
made the whole evening feel heartfelt rather than just glamorous.
[例如，那場婚宴是在晚上於一間酒店舉行的，我和幾位共同朋友一起去。現場有儀式、有
致辭，也有一般婚宴常見的敬酒環節，但我記得最清楚的，反而是新人進場時全場突然爆出
掌聲的那一刻。那一刻其實很簡單，卻很打動人。你可以看得出大家是真心為他們高興，而
這也令整個晚上感覺更真摯，而不只是表面上的華麗。]

components.english: So, if I had to describe it briefly, I would say it was a joyful and meaningful event, because it brought people together and left a lasting impression on me.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要我簡單形容這個活動，我會說它是一個既歡樂又有意義的場合，因為它把很多人聚在一起，也在我心裡留下了很深的印象。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to describe it briefly, I would say it was a joyful and
meaningful event, because it brought people together and left a lasting
impression on me.
[所以，如果要我簡單形容這個活動，我會說它是一個既歡樂又有意義的場合，因為它把很
多人聚在一起，也在我心裡留下了很深的印象。]

components.english: What made it even more special was that it also gave me a chance to reconnect with people I had not seen for a long time.
components.chinese: 令這個活動更特別的是，它也給了我一個機會，讓我可以再次見到一些很久沒見的人。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: What made it even more special was that it also gave me a chance to
reconnect with people I had not seen for a long time.
[令這個活動更特別的是，它也給了我一個機會，讓我可以再次見到一些很久沒見的人。]

components.english: In daily life, people are often so tied up with work and responsibilities that they drift apart without even meaning to. An event like that can act like a bridge: for one evening, people slow down, sit together, talk properly, and pick up old conversations as if no time has passed. I really like that kind of feeling, because it makes life seem a little less rushed and a little more human.
components.chinese: 在日常生活中，人們往往都被工作和責任綁得很緊，結果不知不覺就漸漸疏遠了。像這樣的活動其實就像一座橋樑：在那一個晚上，大家都會慢下來，坐在一起，好好聊天，甚至像時間沒有流逝過一樣，把以前的話題重新接上。我很喜歡那種感覺，因為它會令生活看起來沒那麼匆忙，也更有溫度。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In daily life, people are often so tied up with work and
responsibilities that they drift apart without even meaning to. An event like
that can act like a bridge: for one evening, people slow down, sit together, talk
properly, and pick up old conversations as if no time has passed. I really like that
kind of feeling, because it makes life seem a little less rushed and a little more
human.
[在日常生活中，人們往往都被工作和責任綁得很緊，結果不知不覺就漸漸疏遠了。像這樣
的活動其實就像一座橋樑：在那一個晚上，大家都會慢下來，坐在一起，好好聊天，甚至像
時間沒有流逝過一樣，把以前的話題重新接上。我很喜歡那種感覺，因為它會令生活看起來
沒那麼匆忙，也更有溫度。]

components.english: For instance, during dinner I ended up chatting with an old classmate I had not spoken to in ages, and within a few minutes it felt as though we had slipped back into a familiar rhythm. Moments like that are easy to overlook, but to me, they are often the hidden heart of an event.
components.chinese: 例如，在晚宴期間，我剛好和一位很久沒聯絡的舊同學聊了起來，沒過幾分鐘，就有一種好像又回到熟悉節奏裡的感覺。這些時刻其實很容易被忽略，但對我來說，它們往往才是一個活動最隱藏、也最動人的核心。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, during dinner I ended up chatting with an old
classmate I had not spoken to in ages, and within a few minutes it felt as though
we had slipped back into a familiar rhythm. Moments like that are easy to
overlook, but to me, they are often the hidden heart of an event.
[例如，在晚宴期間，我剛好和一位很久沒聯絡的舊同學聊了起來，沒過幾分鐘，就有一種
好像又回到熟悉節奏裡的感覺。這些時刻其實很容易被忽略，但對我來說，它們往往才是一
個活動最隱藏、也最動人的核心。]

components.english: Overall, I would say the event was memorable not only because it was beautifully organised, but because it brought warmth, connection, and a genuine sense of celebration. To me, a good event is not just something lively for one evening; it should be like a small light that still leaves a glow in your heart after it is over.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說這個活動之所以難忘，不只是因為它安排得很妥當，更因為它帶來了溫度、連結，以及一種真誠的慶祝感。對我而言，一個好的活動不只是熱鬧一晚而已；它應該像一盞燈一樣，在結束之後，還能在心裡留下一點光。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say the event was memorable not only because
it was beautifully organised, but because it brought warmth, connection, and a
genuine sense of celebration. To me, a good event is not just something lively for
one evening; it should be like a small light that still leaves a glow in your heart
after it is over.
[總結來說，我會說這個活動之所以難忘，不只是因為它安排得很妥當，更因為它帶來了溫
度、連結，以及一種真誠的慶祝感。對我而言，一個好的活動不只是熱鬧一晚而已；它應該
像一盞燈一樣，在結束之後，還能在心裡留下一點光。]

question.english: Describe an important choice you had to make in your life.
question.chinese: 描述你人生中曾經需要作出的一個重要選擇。

source_lines.text: Topic: Describe an important choice you had to make in your life.
描述你人生中曾經需要作出的一個重要選擇。

components.english: One of the most important choices I had to make was deciding what to study after finishing school.
components.chinese: 我人生中其中一個最重要的選擇，就是在完成學校課程之後，決定自己要讀甚麼。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: One of the most important choices I had to make was deciding what to
study after finishing school.
[我人生中其中一個最重要的選擇，就是在完成學校課程之後，決定自己要讀甚麼。]

components.english: That decision mattered a great deal because it was not just about choosing a subject; it was really about choosing a direction in life. I was torn between a safer, more practical option and a field I genuinely cared about. In a way, it felt like a choice between my head and my heart. One path looked more secure on paper, while the other felt more meaningful to me personally.
components.chinese: 那個決定之所以非常重要，是因為它不只是選一個學科，而其實更像是在選擇人生的方向。當時我一直在一個較安全、較實際的選項，和一個自己真正有興趣的範疇之間掙扎。某程度上，那感覺就像是在理智和內心之間作選擇。一條路在現實層面上看起來較穩妥，而另一條路則對我來說更有意義。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: That decision mattered a great deal because it was not just about
choosing a subject; it was really about choosing a direction in life. I was torn
between a safer, more practical option and a field I genuinely cared about. In a
way, it felt like a choice between my head and my heart. One path looked more
secure on paper, while the other felt more meaningful to me personally.
[那個決定之所以非常重要，是因為它不只是選一個學科，而其實更像是在選擇人生的方
向。當時我一直在一個較安全、較實際的選項，和一個自己真正有興趣的範疇之間掙扎。某
程度上，那感覺就像是在理智和內心之間作選擇。一條路在現實層面上看起來較穩妥，而另
一條路則對我來說更有意義。]

components.english: For example, I seriously considered choosing a subject that would probably lead to a more stable career, simply because it seemed sensible and would make other people less worried. However, after thinking it through carefully, I decided to choose the field I was more passionate about. It felt like a leap of faith at the time, but I knew that if I spent years studying something I did not really care about, I would probably lose motivation sooner or later.
components.chinese: 例如，我曾經很認真考慮過選一個大概會帶來較穩定職業出路的學科，因為那看起來比較理性，也會令身邊的人少一點擔心。不過，經過仔細思考之後，我最後還是選了自己更有熱情的方向。那在當時確實像是一場放手一搏，但我知道，如果我要花好幾年去讀一個自己其實並不在乎的東西，我遲早都會失去動力。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, I seriously considered choosing a subject that would
probably lead to a more stable career, simply because it seemed sensible and
would make other people less worried. However, after thinking it through
carefully, I decided to choose the field I was more passionate about. It felt like a
leap of faith at the time, but I knew that if I spent years studying something I
did not really care about, I would probably lose motivation sooner or later.
[例如，我曾經很認真考慮過選一個大概會帶來較穩定職業出路的學科，因為那看起來比較
理性，也會令身邊的人少一點擔心。不過，經過仔細思考之後，我最後還是選了自己更有熱
情的方向。那在當時確實像是一場放手一搏，但我知道，如果我要花好幾年去讀一個自己其
實並不在乎的東西，我遲早都會失去動力。]

components.english: So, that was a very important choice in my life, because it shaped not only what I studied, but also the kind of person I wanted to become.
components.chinese: 所以，這是我人生中一個非常重要的選擇，因為它不只影響了我讀甚麼，也影響了我想成為一個怎樣的人。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, that was a very important choice in my life, because it shaped
not only what I studied, but also the kind of person I wanted to become.
[所以，這是我人生中一個非常重要的選擇，因為它不只影響了我讀甚麼，也影響了我想成
為一個怎樣的人。]

components.english: What made that choice even more important was that it taught me how to take responsibility for my own life.
components.chinese: 令這個選擇更加重要的是，它教會了我如何為自己的人生負責。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: What made that choice even more important was that it taught me
how to take responsibility for my own life.
[令這個選擇更加重要的是，它教會了我如何為自己的人生負責。]

components.english: Before that, many decisions in life had more or less been made for me by school routines, family expectations, or the path that seemed most obvious. This was different. I had to weigh advice, listen to other people, and then, at the end of the day, make up my own mind. That was not easy, but it was a turning point. It taught me that an important decision does not have to feel comfortable to be right. Sometimes growing up begins the moment you stop waiting for a perfect answer and choose a path you can truly stand behind.
components.chinese: 在那之前，人生中很多決定其實多多少少都已經被學校規律、家庭期望，或者那條看起來最明顯的路安排好了。但這一次不一樣。我必須衡量建議、聽取別人的意見，然後在最後真正由自己作出決定。那並不容易，但它是一個轉捩點。它讓我明白，一個重要的決定即使不令人感到舒服，也不代表它不是對的。有時候，成長正正就是從你不再等待完美答案，而開始選擇一條自己真正願意承擔的路開始。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Before that, many decisions in life had more or less been made
for me by school routines, family expectations, or the path that seemed most
obvious. This was different. I had to weigh advice, listen to other people, and
then, at the end of the day, make up my own mind. That was not easy, but it was
a turning point. It taught me that an important decision does not have to feel
comfortable to be right. Sometimes growing up begins the moment you stop
waiting for a perfect answer and choose a path you can truly stand behind.
[在那之前，人生中很多決定其實多多少少都已經被學校規律、家庭期望，或者那條看起來
最明顯的路安排好了。但這一次不一樣。我必須衡量建議、聽取別人的意見，然後在最後真
正由自己作出決定。那並不容易，但它是一個轉捩點。它讓我明白，一個重要的決定即使不
令人感到舒服，也不代表它不是對的。有時候，成長正正就是從你不再等待完美答案，而開
始選擇一條自己真正願意承擔的路開始。]

components.english: Looking back, the choice was not all plain sailing, and I did have moments of doubt. However, because it was my own decision, I was more willing to work hard for it and see it through. That made a huge difference. Even when things became difficult, I did not feel trapped, because I knew I had chosen that road for myself.
components.chinese: 現在回頭看，那個選擇並不是一路平坦的，我當中也確實有過懷疑的時候。不過，正因為那是我自己作出的決定，我反而更願意為它努力，也更願意堅持到底。這帶來了很大的分別。即使事情變得困難，我也不會覺得自己是被困住，因為我知道，那條路是我自己選的。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: Looking back, the choice was not all plain sailing, and I did have
moments of doubt. However, because it was my own decision, I was more willing
to work hard for it and see it through. That made a huge difference. Even when
things became difficult, I did not feel trapped, because I knew I had chosen that
road for myself.
[現在回頭看，那個選擇並不是一路平坦的，我當中也確實有過懷疑的時候。不過，正因為
那是我自己作出的決定，我反而更願意為它努力，也更願意堅持到底。這帶來了很大的分
別。即使事情變得困難，我也不會覺得自己是被困住，因為我知道，那條路是我自己選的。]

components.english: Overall, I would say that choosing what to study was one of the most important decisions of my life. It taught me that big choices are rarely easy, but they often become the moments that quietly shape who we are.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說選擇讀甚麼，是我人生中其中一個最重要的決定。它讓我明白，重大的選擇很少是輕鬆的，但它們往往正是那些會靜靜塑造我們成為甚麼樣的人的時刻。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say that choosing what to study was one of the
most important decisions of my life. It taught me that big choices are rarely easy,
but they often become the moments that quietly shape who we are.
[總結來說，我會說選擇讀甚麼，是我人生中其中一個最重要的決定。它讓我明白，重大的
選擇很少是輕鬆的，但它們往往正是那些會靜靜塑造我們成為甚麼樣的人的時刻。]

question.english: Describe a very difficult task that you succeeded in doing.
question.chinese: 描述一件你成功完成、但非常困難的事。

source_lines.text: Topic: Describe a very difficult task that you succeeded in doing.
描述一件你成功完成、但非常困難的事。

components.english: One very difficult task that I succeeded in doing was giving an important presentation in English in front of a large group of people.
components.chinese: 我成功做到的一件非常困難的事，就是用英文在一大群人面前做一次重要的簡報。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: One very difficult task that I succeeded in doing was giving an
important presentation in English in front of a large group of people.
[我成功做到的一件非常困難的事，就是用英文在一大群人面前做一次重要的簡報。]

components.english: That was extremely challenging for me because it pushed me out of my comfort zone in more ways than one. It was not just about standing up and speaking; it was about speaking clearly, staying calm, and trying not to let my nerves get the better of me. At the time, I was not fully confident in my spoken English, and I also found public speaking quite intimidating. So, for me, it felt a bit like being thrown in at the deep end.
components.chinese: 那對我來說非常有挑戰性，因為它在很多方面都把我推離了自己的舒適圈。那不只是站起來說話那麼簡單，而是要說得清楚、保持冷靜，還要盡量不讓緊張情緒壓過自己。那時候，我對自己的英文口語還不是完全有信心，而且我本身也覺得公開演講很有壓力。所以，對我來說，那感覺有點像突然被丟進深水區。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: That was extremely challenging for me because it pushed me out
of my comfort zone in more ways than one. It was not just about standing up
and speaking; it was about speaking clearly, staying calm, and trying not to let
my nerves get the better of me. At the time, I was not fully confident in my
spoken English, and I also found public speaking quite intimidating. So, for me,
it felt a bit like being thrown in at the deep end.
[那對我來說非常有挑戰性，因為它在很多方面都把我推離了自己的舒適圈。那不只是站起
來說話那麼簡單，而是要說得清楚、保持冷靜，還要盡量不讓緊張情緒壓過自己。那時候，
我對自己的英文口語還不是完全有信心，而且我本身也覺得公開演講很有壓力。所以，對我
來說，那感覺有點像突然被丟進深水區。]

components.english: I still remember preparing for it again and again. I wrote notes, practised in front of the mirror, timed myself, and even rehearsed the opening so many times that I could almost say it in my sleep. When the day finally came, my heart was racing, and for the first few seconds I felt as though my mind had gone blank. But once I got into the flow, things began to click. I finished the presentation, answered a few questions, and, to my great relief, it actually went well.
components.chinese: 我到現在仍然記得自己為那次簡報反覆準備了很多遍。我寫了筆記、對著鏡子練習、計時，甚至把開場白練到幾乎做夢都能背出來。到了正式那天，我心跳得很快，一開始的幾秒鐘甚至覺得自己腦袋一片空白。不過，當我慢慢進入狀態之後，事情就開始順了起來。我最後完成了整個簡報，還回答了幾個問題，而最令我鬆一口氣的是，結果其實做得不錯。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: I still remember preparing for it again and again. I wrote notes,
practised in front of the mirror, timed myself, and even rehearsed the opening
so many times that I could almost say it in my sleep. When the day finally came,
my heart was racing, and for the first few seconds I felt as though my mind had
gone blank. But once I got into the flow, things began to click. I finished the
presentation, answered a few questions, and, to my great relief, it actually went
well.
[我到現在仍然記得自己為那次簡報反覆準備了很多遍。我寫了筆記、對著鏡子練習、計時
，甚至把開場白練到幾乎做夢都能背出來。到了正式那天，我心跳得很快，一開始的幾秒鐘
甚至覺得自己腦袋一片空白。不過，當我慢慢進入狀態之後，事情就開始順了起來。我最後
完成了整個簡報，還回答了幾個問題，而最令我鬆一口氣的是，結果其實做得不錯。]

components.english: So, although it may not sound dramatic from the outside, it felt like a huge achievement to me, because I had managed to do something that I had genuinely been afraid of.
components.chinese: 所以，雖然從外人看來，這件事未必很戲劇化，但對我來說，它真的是一個很大的成就，因為我成功做到了自己一直真心害怕的事。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, although it may not sound dramatic from the outside, it felt
like a huge achievement to me, because I had managed to do something that I
had genuinely been afraid of.
[所以，雖然從外人看來，這件事未必很戲劇化，但對我來說，它真的是一個很大的成就，因
為我成功做到了自己一直真心害怕的事。]

components.english: What made it even more meaningful was that it taught me something important about difficulty and growth.
components.chinese: 而這件事更有意義的地方在於，它也讓我學到了一個關於困難和成長的重要道理。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: What made it even more meaningful was that it taught me something
important about difficulty and growth.
[而這件事更有意義的地方在於，它也讓我學到了一個關於困難和成長的重要道理。]

components.english: Before that experience, I used to think confidence came first and success came after. Now I see it a little differently. Very often, confidence comes after you have done the hard thing, not before. In other words, success is sometimes built the other way round. You do not wait until fear disappears; you move forward while your hands are still shaking. That experience really showed me that what looks like a mountain at a distance can become manageable once you climb it step by step.
components.chinese: 在那次經歷之前，我一直以為，自信是先出現的，成功則是之後才來。現在我看法有點不同了。很多時候，自信其實是在你做完那件困難的事之後才長出來的，而不是之前就已經存在。換句話說，成功有時正好是反過來建立的。你不是等到恐懼完全消失才前進，而是在雙手還微微發抖的時候照樣往前走。那次經歷真的讓我明白，遠遠看像一座山的事情，一旦你一步一步去爬，它其實是可以處理的。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Before that experience, I used to think confidence came first
and success came after. Now I see it a little differently. Very often, confidence
comes after you have done the hard thing, not before. In other words, success is
sometimes built the other way round. You do not wait until fear disappears; you
move forward while your hands are still shaking. That experience really showed
me that what looks like a mountain at a distance can become manageable once
you climb it step by step.
[在那次經歷之前，我一直以為，自信是先出現的，成功則是之後才來。現在我看法有點不
同了。很多時候，自信其實是在你做完那件困難的事之後才長出來的，而不是之前就已經存
在。換句話說，成功有時正好是反過來建立的。你不是等到恐懼完全消失才前進，而是在雙
手還微微發抖的時候照樣往前走。那次經歷真的讓我明白，遠遠看像一座山的事情，一旦你
一步一步去爬，它其實是可以處理的。]

components.english: After that presentation, I found later speaking tasks much easier to deal with. I was still nervous sometimes, of course, but I no longer felt paralysed by it. It was as if I had broken a mental barrier. Once that wall came down, other challenges did not seem quite so frightening anymore.
components.chinese: 在那次簡報之後，我發現自己之後再面對類似的說話任務時，已經容易處理得多了。當然，我有時還是會緊張，但我不再像以前那樣被它困住。那感覺就像我打破了一道心理關口。一旦那道牆倒下來，其他挑戰看起來也就沒有那麼可怕了。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: After that presentation, I found later speaking tasks much easier
to deal with. I was still nervous sometimes, of course, but I no longer felt
paralysed by it. It was as if I had broken a mental barrier. Once that wall came
down, other challenges did not seem quite so frightening anymore.
[在那次簡報之後，我發現自己之後再面對類似的說話任務時，已經容易處理得多了。當然
，我有時還是會緊張，但我不再像以前那樣被它困住。那感覺就像我打破了一道心理關口。
一旦那道牆倒下來，其他挑戰看起來也就沒有那麼可怕了。]

components.english: Overall, I would say that giving that presentation was one of the most difficult tasks I had successfully completed, not because it was physically hard, but because it demanded courage, preparation, and self-control. Looking back, I am really glad I did it, because it turned out to be more than just a task; it became a turning point.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，那次簡報是我成功完成過最困難的事情之一，並不是因為它在體力上有多難，而是因為它要求我拿出勇氣、準備，以及自我控制。回頭看，我真的很慶幸自己做到了，因為它最後已經不只是一項任務，而更像是一個轉捩點。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say that giving that presentation was one of
the most difficult tasks I had successfully completed, not because it was
physically hard, but because it demanded courage, preparation, and self-control.
Looking back, I am really glad I did it, because it turned out to be more than
just a task; it became a turning point.
[總結來說，我會說，那次簡報是我成功完成過最困難的事情之一，並不是因為它在體力上
有多難，而是因為它要求我拿出勇氣、準備，以及自我控制。回頭看，我真的很慶幸自己做
到了，因為它最後已經不只是一項任務，而更像是一個轉捩點。]

question.english: Describe an interesting discussion you had related to your work or studies.
question.chinese: 描述一次與你的工作或學業有關、令你覺得有趣的討論。

source_lines.text: Describe an interesting discussion you had related to your work or studies.
[描述一次與你的工作或學業有關、令你覺得有趣的討論。]

components.english: One discussion that really stands out to me was a conversation I had with my classmates about whether students should use AI tools in their coursework.
components.chinese: 對我來說，有一次特別難忘的討論，是我和同學之間關於學生是否應該在課業中使用 AI工具的對話。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: One discussion that really stands out to me was a conversation I had
with my classmates about whether students should use AI tools in their
coursework.
[對我來說，有一次特別難忘的討論，是我和同學之間關於學生是否應該在課業中使用 AI
工具的對話。]

components.english: I found it interesting because it was not a black-and-white issue at all. Some people felt AI could save time, spark ideas, and help students organise their thoughts more clearly, while others argued that if people lean on it too heavily, they may stop thinking for themselves. What made the discussion so engaging was that both sides had a point. It felt less like a simple debate and more like watching two truths rub against each other.
components.chinese: 我覺得那次討論很有意思，因為那根本不是一個非黑即白的問題。有些人認為 AI 可以節省時間、激發靈感，並幫助學生把想法整理得更清楚；但另一些人則認為，如果人們過度依賴它，就可能會慢慢失去自己思考的能力。那場討論之所以吸引人，正正在於雙方其實都有道理。它與其說像一場單純的辯論，不如說更像兩種真實觀點在互相碰撞。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: I found it interesting because it was not a black-and-white issue
at all. Some people felt AI could save time, spark ideas, and help students
organise their thoughts more clearly, while others argued that if people lean on
it too heavily, they may stop thinking for themselves. What made the discussion
so engaging was that both sides had a point. It felt less like a simple debate and
more like watching two truths rub against each other.
[我覺得那次討論很有意思，因為那根本不是一個非黑即白的問題。有些人認為 AI 可以節
省時間、激發靈感，並幫助學生把想法整理得更清楚；但另一些人則認為，如果人們過度依
賴它，就可能會慢慢失去自己思考的能力。那場討論之所以吸引人，正正在於雙方其實都有
道理。它與其說像一場單純的辯論，不如說更像兩種真實觀點在互相碰撞。]

components.english: For example, one classmate said using AI to improve language was no different from asking a friend to check your work, whereas another said that once the tool starts shaping the content itself, the line becomes blurry. I remember saying that the real issue was not the tool, but how honestly people used it. That was the point when the discussion really came alive, because we stopped talking only about technology and started talking about responsibility.
components.chinese: 例如，有一位同學說，用 AI 去改善語言表達，其實和請朋友幫忙看功課沒有太大分別；但另一位同學則認為，一旦那個工具開始直接塑造內容本身，界線就會變得模糊。我記得我當時說，真正的問題不在工具本身，而在於人們是否誠實地使用它。也是從那一刻開始，整場討論才真正變得有火花，因為我們不再只是在談科技，而是開始談責任。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, one classmate said using AI to improve language was
no different from asking a friend to check your work, whereas another said that
once the tool starts shaping the content itself, the line becomes blurry. I
remember saying that the real issue was not the tool, but how honestly people
used it. That was the point when the discussion really came alive, because we
stopped talking only about technology and started talking about responsibility.
[例如，有一位同學說，用 AI 去改善語言表達，其實和請朋友幫忙看功課沒有太大分別；但
另一位同學則認為，一旦那個工具開始直接塑造內容本身，界線就會變得模糊。我記得我當
時說，真正的問題不在工具本身，而在於人們是否誠實地使用它。也是從那一刻開始，整場
討論才真正變得有火花，因為我們不再只是在談科技，而是開始談責任。]

components.english: So, what made that discussion memorable for me was not just the topic itself, but the way it pushed all of us to think more carefully about learning, fairness, and academic integrity.
components.chinese: 所以，令那次討論對我來說特別難忘的，不只是題目本身，而是它迫使我們所有人都更仔細地思考學習、公平，以及學術誠信。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, what made that discussion memorable for me was not just the
topic itself, but the way it pushed all of us to think more carefully about
learning, fairness, and academic integrity.
[所以，令那次討論對我來說特別難忘的，不只是題目本身，而是它迫使我們所有人都更仔
細地思考學習、公平，以及學術誠信。]

components.english: What stayed with me even more was that the discussion actually changed my own view.
components.chinese: 更令我印象深刻的是，那次討論其實真的改變了我自己的看法。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: What stayed with me even more was that the discussion actually
changed my own view.
[更令我印象深刻的是，那次討論其實真的改變了我自己的看法。]

components.english: Before that, I tended to see AI either as a helpful shortcut or as a possible problem. After the discussion, I realised it was more complicated than that. It is a bit like a tool in the kitchen: useful in the right hands, but not something that should cook the whole meal for you. In other words, it should be a stepping stone rather than a crutch. That discussion reminded me that the best conversations do not hand you a neat answer; they make you think better.
components.chinese: 在那之前，我通常會把 AI 看成不是一種方便的捷徑，就是一個潛在的問題。但討論過後，我意識到事情其實比這更複雜。它有點像廚房裡的一件工具：在適當的人手中很有用，但不應該由它替你把整頓飯都煮完。換句話說，它應該是一塊踏腳石，而不是一根拐杖。那次討論提醒了我，最好的對話不是直接把整齊的答案塞給你，而是讓你思考得更好。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Before that, I tended to see AI either as a helpful shortcut or
as a possible problem. After the discussion, I realised it was more complicated
than that. It is a bit like a tool in the kitchen: useful in the right hands, but not
something that should cook the whole meal for you. In other words, it should be
a stepping stone rather than a crutch. That discussion reminded me that the
best conversations do not hand you a neat answer; they make you think better.
[在那之前，我通常會把 AI 看成不是一種方便的捷徑，就是一個潛在的問題。但討論過後，
我意識到事情其實比這更複雜。它有點像廚房裡的一件工具：在適當的人手中很有用，但不
應該由它替你把整頓飯都煮完。換句話說，它應該是一塊踏腳石，而不是一根拐杖。那次討
論提醒了我，最好的對話不是直接把整齊的答案塞給你，而是讓你思考得更好。]

components.english: For instance, after that conversation, I became much more careful when using digital tools for assignments. I felt it was fine to use them to brainstorm, improve structure, or polish expression, but not to do the heavy lifting for me. In a way, that discussion gave me a clearer compass when it came to my own study habits.
components.chinese: 例如，在那次對話之後，我在做功課時使用數碼工具就變得更加小心了。我覺得用它們來腦力激盪、改善結構，或者修飾表達是可以的，但不應該讓它們替我完成最核心的思考工作。某程度上，那次討論就像給了我一個更清晰的指南針，讓我知道自己在學習習慣上應該怎樣拿捏。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, after that conversation, I became much more careful
when using digital tools for assignments. I felt it was fine to use them to
brainstorm, improve structure, or polish expression, but not to do the heavy
lifting for me. In a way, that discussion gave me a clearer compass when it came
to my own study habits.
[例如，在那次對話之後，我在做功課時使用數碼工具就變得更加小心了。我覺得用它們來
腦力激盪、改善結構，或者修飾表達是可以的，但不應該讓它們替我完成最核心的思考工
作。某程度上，那次討論就像給了我一個更清晰的指南針，讓我知道自己在學習習慣上應該
怎樣拿捏。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say it was one of the most interesting study-related discussions I’ve had, because it was timely, thought-provoking, and genuinely changed how I approached my work. A good discussion does not just fill the air; it leaves an echo.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說那是我經歷過其中一次最有意思的學業相關討論，因為它既切合時代，也發人深省，而且真的改變了我面對自己課業的方式。一場好的討論不只是把空氣填滿；它會在心裡留下回響。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say it was one of the most interesting study-related
discussions I’ve had, because it was timely, thought-provoking, and genuinely
changed how I approached my work. A good discussion does not just fill the air;
it leaves an echo.
[總結來說，我會說那是我經歷過其中一次最有意思的學業相關討論，因為它既切合時代，
